title: Product Manager (m/w/d) – AI experienced
jd_text:
Scopevisio ist ein führender Anbieter cloudbasierter Unternehmenssoftware für den deutschen Mittelstand. Unsere integrierte Plattform verbindet ERP, CRM, DMS und Finanzbuchhaltung in einer modernen SaaS-Lösung. Wir befinden uns in einer Phase der gezielten Weiterentwicklung — sowohl technologisch als auch organisatorisch. Als AI-first Company legen wir einen besonderen Fokus auf den Ausbau und die Umsetzung innovativer AI-Initiativen in unseren Produkten und Services. Der Produktbereich wird professionalisiert, und wir suchen erfahrene Produktmanager, die diesen Weg mitgestalten.
Wir suchen
erfahrene Product Manager mit tiefem methodischen Produktmanagement Wissen
— Leute, die die fachlichen Problemstellungen unserer Kunden nicht erst lernen müssen, sondern bereits aus der Tiefe heraus verstehen.
Wir besetzen mehrere Produktbereiche und suchen Profile mit fundiertem Hintergrund in einem oder mehreren der folgenden Felder: Finanzbuchhaltung & Rechnungswesen, ERP & kaufmännische Prozesse, DMS & dokumentenbasierte Workflows oder KI-gestützte Automatisierung im Unternehmenskontext. Als AI-first Company legen wir einen besonderen Fokus auf den Ausbau und die Umsetzung innovativer AI-Initiativen in unseren Produkten und Services. Du übernimmst die strategische Verantwortung für einen dieser Produktbereiche.
Du arbeitest dabei im
PM-Tandem
mit einem zweiten Product Manager, der tiefe Domänen- und Produktkenntnis mitbringt. Ihr ergänzt euch: Du bringst strategisches PM-Handwerk, datengetriebene Priorisierung und Stakeholder-Management ein — dein Tandem-Partner bringt fachliche Tiefe, Kundennähe und Delivery-Exzellenz ein. Gemeinsam verantwortet ihr den Erfolg eures Produktbereichs.
Du baust Product Management als Disziplin bei Scopevisio mit auf. Das ist kein Job für Leute, die funktionierende Strukturen suchen — sondern für Leute, die sie schaffen wollen.
Dies sind Deine Aufgaben:
Domänenexpertise & Geschäftsverständnis
Du bringst
tiefes fachliches Verständnis
in deiner Domäne mit — und setzt dieses Wissen ein, um Produktentscheidungen fachlich fundiert zu treffen
Aufbau eines klaren Bildes der Wertschöpfung und Wirtschaftlichkeit deines Produktbereichs: Nutzungsmuster, kommerzielle Hebel und die Bedürfnisse unserer Zielkunden im Mittelstand
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Sales, Customer Success und Finance, um zu verstehen, wie dein Produkt Umsatz generiert, Kunden bindet und wo Wertschöpfungslücken bestehen
Fähigkeit, dem C-Level und internen Stakeholdern in ihrer Sprache zu erklären, warum eine bestimmte Produktinvestition die richtige ist — mit Zahlen, nicht mit Meinungen
Strategische Verantwortung für deinen Produktbereich
Fachliche und strategische Führung eines Produktbereichs innerhalb der Scopevisio-Plattform — du entwickelst eine klare Vision, wie dein Bereich zum Geschäftserfolg beiträgt
Übersetzung fachlicher Anforderungen und Marktbedürfnisse in eine priorisierte Roadmap mit messbaren Outcomes
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Engineering, um technologische Möglichkeiten (insb. KI und Automatisierung) in marktfähige Produktfunktionen zu überführen
Steuerung der Stakeholder-Erwartungen in deinem Bereich — mit klarem Blick darauf, was den größten Kundennutzen und Geschäftsimpact liefert
Datengetriebene Produktentscheidungen
Nutzung von Product Analytics, Kundenfeedback und Geschäftsdaten, um Priorisierungsentscheidungen fundiert zu treffen
Definition und Tracking der richtigen Metriken für deinen Produktbereich — Kennzahlen, die echten Geschäftsimpact messen: Adoption, Retention, Revenue Impact, Time-to-Value
Aufbau einer Kultur des Messens: Jede größere Initiative hat eine klare Hypothese, eine messbare Erwartung und ein Review nach Launch
Product Discovery & Strategie
Durchführung systematischer Product Discovery: Kundeninterviews, Nutzungsdatenanalyse, Wettbewerbsbeobachtung und Opportunity Assessment

title: Technical Product Manager (d/f/m)
jd_text:
Job description
mobile.de is Germany’s largest vehicle marketplace, with more than 1.6 million listed cars, commercial vehicles, motorcycles, and e-bikes (annual average 2025). Both private customers and registered vehicle dealers use the platform and benefit from more than 140 million visits per month*. As a “one-stop shop,” mobile.de’s offering includes not only buying and selling, but also financing and leasing solutions. Founded in 1996, the company is a subsidiary of Adevinta, a global leader in online classified portals.
Based in Berlin-Charlottenburg, we offer a dynamic environment where growth, learning, and collaboration are at the heart of everything we do.If you are driven by an eagerness to disrupt, have a passion for collaboration, and are excited about shaping the future of mobility, we would love to hear from you.
About the role
We are looking for a Technical Product Manager (d/f/m) to join our Dealer Product Team organisation. In this role, you will take ownership of our central Vehicle Data API —a complex, business-critical platform capability that powers key experiences and processes across mobile.de.
You’ll work cross-functionally with engineering, design, business development, marketing, and sales to increase dealer and mobile.de business value—by ensuring our vehicle data is reliable, scalable, and easy to consume for internal and external stakeholders.
This is a role for someone who’s comfortable owning a technical product that isn’t directly customer-facing, but forms the foundation for many customer-facing products.
Job requirements
Own and evolve the vehicle taxonomy and attribute coverage (make/model/trim/options).
Define and enforce data quality foundations for listing creation, including validation rules and quality thresholds.
Lead external vehicle data partnerships: requirements, data exchange specs, and contract/vendor management.
Partner with engineering on data ingestion pipelines, validation routines, and operational quality controls.
Evaluate AI/ML opportunities for automation of data enrichment
Lead and empower a team of :6 developers by providing direction, context, prioritization, and clarity.
Take technical product ownership of :40 artefacts that touch the core of the mobile.de platform (e.g., APIs, schemas, data contracts, tooling, documentation, and supporting components).
Translate business needs into technical roadmaps, maintain a clean backlog, and ensure reliable quarterly deliverables.
Align and communicate across departments—ensuring shared understanding of priorities, constraints, dependencies, and trade-offs.
Manage a broad stakeholder set by translating complex technical topics into clear, decision-ready language and translating business goals into actionable engineering work.
Help shape the vision and roadmap for Vehicle Data - both product-wise and tech-wise—including modernization, scalability, reliability, and usability improvements.
Job responsibilities
Solid experience as a Technical Product Manager in a platform, infrastructure, or API-heavy environment.
Strong analytical skills—comfortable working with large datasets
Experience with B2B / API products involving external vendors/partners and negotiating requirements or data contracts.
Understanding of data integration patterns (REST APIs, batch imports, validation/quality checks).
Strong ability to work with engineering teams on technical concepts such as APIs, service boundaries, data models, reliability, and operational excellence.
Ability to create clarity in ambiguity: crisp problem framing, prioritisation, and decision-making under constraints.
Strong communication skills—able to simplify complex technical topics without losing accuracy.
Experience translating business goals into roadmaps, execution plans, and outcome-oriented delivery.
Nice to have
Experience working with REST APIs and API lifecycle management (contracts, versioning, documentation, backwards compatibility, consumer-driven changes).
Passion for auto

title: Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Come make a difference in a fast-growing, innovative, Software Product Management team that combines a scale-up atmosphere with a global industry-leading product portfolio. You'll be part of building the future of Trackunit's connected ecosystem - helping shape products that connect people, data, and machines to build a smarter construction industry.
At Trackunit, we put our customers first. As a Product Manager, you'll work across teams and departments to guide software products from research and idea through to launch - connecting the customer, technical, and business worlds. You'll break down complex problems into clear, actionable steps and drive product development with focus, purpose, and measurable outcomes.
This role drives Trackunit's Data Products, including the Analytics Suite and Benchmarking product. These tools sit on top of Trackunit's connected data platform and give fleet operators, operations teams, and data-driven decision-makers a way to turn machine and site data into actionable insight. Data Products are one of Trackunit's highest-leverage growth areas: the ability to surface meaningful, industry-specific analysis is what keeps customers on the platform and drives expansion.
We're looking for someone who thrives in a dynamic environment, brings clarity amid change, and builds strong alignment across diverse teams. If you're passionate about creating meaningful products and want to make a tangible impact in a global SaaS company - we'd love to hear from you.
You will be connected to one of our offices in Denmark, Germany or the Netherlands with the possibility to work from one of the dedicated offices and remotely in a flexible capacity.
What's in it for you?
You'll join the Platform Pod - the team building the foundation everything else runs on. The pod's mandate is to evolve IrisX into a Platform-as-a-Service: enabling AI enabled and faster development across the ecosystem, powering data-driven value creation, and unlocking multi-sided business models at construction scale. Portals is a core pillar of that vision.
A place in a hyper-growth SaaS business, with possibilities to grow internally in the company. We are moving quickly, so fasten your seat belt and prepare to adjust. Back in 2020 we were 150 employees at Trackunit and we have just passed 450 employees. International environment with regular check-ins and social events across teams, departments and borders.
We take your personal and professional development goals seriously and we support them through training, coaching and mindfulness as well as with an open feedback culture to enable you to identify your strengths and opportunities. We do this by touching upon your impact on our triple focus: impacting yourself, impacting teams/relations and impacting the organisation/strategy.
Flexibility and hybrid working is not just a cliché to us or something we state in a job posting. We do offer you a flexible setup and believe that you best can structure how your routines look like. And once you have chosen how you work best, let us know what you need: we got you covered with the IT equipment you need for your work.
Turning the Tide. At Trackunit, we aim to create and contribute to an inclusive workplace. And this priority starts with our hiring - we do not discriminate on the basis of gender identity, sexual orientation, personal expression, ethnicity, religious belief, or disability status. We only assess candidates on their qualifications and merit. Learn more about TIDE and other DE&I initiatives here.
Who are you ideally?
You've done product management in a B2B SaaS or platform/industrial software environment.
You've built products for data-intensive user personas. You know what "good" looks like for a data analyst, BI team, or data scientist, and you understand the gap between what a data platform can technically do and what users actually adopt.
You're a technical PM with genuine data stack depth (i.e. databricks), close enough to engineering to

The company offers a dynamic environment, flexibility, and opportunities for growth and development.

title: Product Manager, Portals Ecosystem (m/f/d)
jd_text:

It’s the foundation for multi-company collaboration and data-driven services across the construction ecosystem.
As PM for Portals, you’ll own the product that makes that vision real. You’ll collaborate closely with Engineering, Design, Product Marketing and other Go-to-Market teams to ensure we deliver customer value while supporting Trackunit’s strategic goals.

Who are you ideally?

You think and build AI-first. You reach for AI capabilities as a default design tool — not a bolt-on — and you know how to evaluate when AI genuinely reduces friction versus when it add

title: Product Manager - (Consumer Discovery, Agentic)
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
We are looking for a
Product Manager (Agentic Discovery)
to lead the charge in redefining how users find and interact with our marketplace through conversational search and AI agents. In this highly experimental and fast-moving role, you will collaborate with leadership, tech, and design to build a delightful agent-led experience from the ground up. You will be responsible for shaping product strategy, launching rapid experiments, and translating cutting-edge AI capabilities into tangible consumer value.
Shape the Agentic Product Experience: Help drive the transition from traditional search to conversational discovery, defining how agents solve complex user intents.
Rapid Prototyping & Evaluations: Partner with Tech to build agentic workflows, evaluations, and guardrails in a fast-moving, experimental environment.
Define New Success Metrics: Establish frameworks for measuring agent performance in a non-deterministic world, focusing on conversational quality, accuracy, and user delight.
Cross-Functional Alignment: Collaborate with leadership, tech and design to turn ambiguous AI capabilities into a cohesive, high-impact product experience.
At Delivery Hero, you’ll shape the global consumer experience for millions of users. Your work will have a direct impact on how we attract and retain customers, creating data-driven, personalized interactions that keep them coming back. As part of our Consumer Team, you’ll enhance customer satisfaction and drive global growth and profitability through innovative projects.
Qualifications
3–5 years experience in consumer-facing Product Management, in a startup or fast-moving environment
Expertise in rapid prototyping with AI, A/B testing and making data-driven decisions
AI-native: Experience using, building & launching AI-driven products
Extreme ownership & bias for action: You are a self-starter with a growth mindset and can operate in high ambiguity situations
Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear strategies for stakeholders and leadership
Nice to Have:
Experience with evaluation & measurement of AI-driven products
Hands-on analytics experience
Experience working on consumer products with millions of users
Additional Information
Ensuring you and all our Heroes are looked after, happy, and healthy is always on the menu. Because if you’re in good shape, then we’re in good shape.
Make the most of our hybrid working model and join the team for face-to-face connection and collaboration in our beautiful Berlin campus 2 days a week
We offer 27 days holiday with an extra day on 2nd and 3rd year of service
We will support you in developing yourself and your career growth opportunities: 1.000 € Educational Budget, Language Courses, Parental Support and access to the Udemy Business platform to explore a variety of online courses.
Get moving and release those wonderful, mind-boosting endorphins: Health Checkups, Meditation, Gym & Bicycle Subsidy
Cash. Dough. Cheddar. Whatever you call it, we’ll help you with it: Employee Share Purchase Plan, Sabbatical Bank, Public Transportation Ticket Discount, Life & Accident Insurance, Corporate Pension Plan
The power of getting together over some food is unrivaled. Here are a few ways to help you do that. All the yum: Digital Meal Vouchers, Food Vouchers, Corporate Discounts. Courses.
Wondering what relocati

The role involves collaborating with leadership, tech, and design teams to build a cohesive product experience. title: Product Manager (AI)
jd_text:
Company
Founded in Berlin, autarc is a ~40 person start-up who were part of Y Combinator’s S24 batch in San Francisco. Our team consists of people in Sales, Tech and Operations, with the Engineering & Product department currently ~20 people, as we continue to grow throughout the year.
Role Description
We're looking for a Product Manager to work in one of our key product teams in building the leading OS for Europe's heat pump and solar workforce.
You'll work alongside a team that consists of a Machine Learning Engineer, Software Engineers and an iOS Engineer, helping shape and maintain our product roadmap, run planning sessions and sprint preps with team leads, manage tickets in Linear and open Cursor to ship stuff yourself.
We're always looking to build the next generation product and with a clear vision to become the agentic OS for the workforce behind the energy transition, that includes leveraging LLMs and NLP in building an AI voice agent to increase efficiencies.
Other responsibilities will be as follows:
Support leadership in shaping and prioritising the product roadmap
Run product discovery alongside our teams through direct customer engagement
Manage product operations in Linear: triage, prioritisation, sprint hygiene
Build and ship features or prototypes directly using AI-assisted development tools (Lovable, Figma, Cursor, Copilot)
Facilitate planning sessions with product teams and sprint preps with engineering leads
Represent autarc in customer calls, sales meetings, and training sessions
Work alongside our Engineers in building and shipping material
This is a Product Manager position but as the landscape of the industry shifts, we also expect you to build and tangibly contribute on a day-to-day basis.
Requirements
2-5+ years of experience as a Product Manager
Exposure to working in B2B SaaS
AI-native in working with tools to help you run faster and jump higher
Experience having worked in an AI-first team/company
Track record in conducting user interviews and product discovery
A desired obsession to go deep AI, Machine Learning and Computer Vision
Berlin-based and willing to work hybrid
Native German speaking
What We Offer
Freedom to choose your own equipment (within a budget)
26 paid vacation days
An annual team retreat
The choice between Dance or JobRad for mobility
The choice between Classpass or Urban Sports Club for fitness
Regular team events, lunches and activities
Next steps
If your application is successful, you will participate in the following:
Screening call with Louis (Recruiter)
Culture fit call with an Engineer
Take home case study/exercise
Final on-site interview with Etienne (CEO) and the team
About Us
Right now, 25% of CO2 emissions in Europe are caused by heating our buildings. In order for this to be reduced, the transition towards renewable energy needs to be simplified and made more accessible. There are too few installers to meet the growing demand for heat pumps, and a solution to match this trend needs to be brought to the market.
autarc is developing the leading operating system (OS) for heat pump installers, enabling a more digital and efficient installation process for heat pumps.
Through this, autarc is reducing the hurdles to decarbonizing single-family homes and small apartment buildings in Europe, with the goal to help with the planning and designing of 1 million heat pumps by 2030.
summary: The role is for a Product Manager at autarc, a start-up developing an operating system for heat pump installers, with a focus on emerging tech and AI-native tools. The ideal candidate has 2-5+ years of experience in Product Management, exposure to B2B SaaS, and a track record of conducting user interviews and product discovery. The company offers a hybrid work environment, freedom to choose equipment, and regular team events.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Company Description
Consultport is the leading digital platform that connects some of the world's most iconic brands with world-class consultants. We're scaling hard, and
we need a Product Manager obsessed with product-led growth (PLG).
Role Description
This role is not about babysitting a roadmap but turning the product itself into our best sales channel — removing friction, driving activation, and making users fall in love before they ever talk to sales.
What you'll do
Own the PLG strategy: activation, onboarding, habit loops, and expansion
Turn data into decisions — define metrics, run experiments, kill what doesn't work
Work directly with Engineering, Design, and Data in a fast-moving Agile setup
Identify and remove every drop of friction in the consultant and client journey
Collaborate with Sales and Management to align product motions with revenue
What we're looking for
2 to 4 years of PM experience, ideally at a marketplace or SaaS company
You know what PLG actually means and have shipped it
You use AI tools daily. If you don't, don't apply
Sharp analytical skills: you live in dashboards and trust numbers over opinions
Strong English, both written and spoken (C1 minimum)
Based in or willing to relocate to Berlin for an on-site role
Why you'll like it here
Direct impact on a two-sided marketplace used by thousands worldwide
A sharp, driven team that moves fast and ships faster
Work from our newly renovated Berlin office
Real ownership from day one
Company partnership with Urban Sports Club and contributions to your monthly public transportation ticket
Unlimited snacks and drinks at the office
Competitive compensation package
If product-led growth is what you eat for breakfast — not just a buzzword on your CV —
your boarding call is now.

title: Product Manager, Courier Delivery Experience
jd_text:
About Wolt
At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
We seek a Product Manager to join our Courier and Delivery Experience (CDX) team. This role will focus on ensuring world-class courier and consumer delivery experiences while continually evolving the delivery process with a future-oriented mindset. The Courier and Delivery Experience team owns the experiences that elevate every Wolt delivery, from the moment a courier opens the app to every order they accept, all the way to dropping an order off at the customer’s doorstep. The team sits at the intersection of a multi-sided marketplace product, real-world operations, and safety, and the work directly moves how reliable Wolt feels to consumers and how satisfied our couriers are when delivering.
You will play a crucial role in shaping the courier experience, ensuring that our couriers feel valued and supported while enhancing the efficiency of deliveries. By blending operational expertise with a deep understanding of courier needs, you will improve the user journey from onboarding couriers to completing deliveries. From designing new features that boost courier satisfaction to refining delivery processes, your work will contribute to key metrics across courier engagement, performance, and satisfaction. You will also be influential in shaping the evolution of the live delivery experience for our courier partners, the merchant courier interaction at pickup and the consumer courier interaction at the doorstep via the Courier app surfaces.
The ideal candidate will have an exceptional track record in product management, with proven success in working with cross-functional teams across engineering, design, product, and operations. You will define the vision and roadmap for courier and delivery experiences across multiple projects. Working in partnership with business stakeholders, you will also collaborate on cross-team initiatives, contributing to the broader business strategy while ensuring a deep understanding of the courier experience.
Join us in shaping the future of delivery, where every delivery is an opportunity for delight and loyalty.
Our humble expectations
You could come to this role from various backgrounds, but ideally, you bring:
Several years of experience in product management, specializing in consumer-facing products
Expertise in developing user-centric products, with the ability to address complex user experience-related challenges.
Demonstrated expertise in supporting cross-functional teams and fostering operational excellence in fast-paced, high-demand industries.
Proven track record in building products influencing cross-functional teams, including engineering, data, design, and operations.
High ownership and collaborative capabilities, making thoughtful decisions, and focusing on product details to ensure execution aligns with the vision.
Strong ability to define, execute, and measure meaningful OKRs, ensuring alignment with company-wide goals and fostering bottom-up alignment from your team.
Ability to focus on the bigger picture, creating solutions that benefit couriers, operations, and customer experiences across the organization.
Excellent stakeholder management skills, able to work effectively with diverse internal teams and external partners, while confidently engaging with leadership in a high-visi

title: Product Manager (f/m/d) - Berlin
jd_text:
About Us
At CYCLE, we’re redefining urban mobility for the people who keep cities moving: couriers, riders, and delivery pros. Our e-bike subscription service combines durable vehicles, fast repairs, and real support, everything riders need to stay on the road.
From Berlin to London, Vienna to Amsterdam, we operate in 200+ cities across Europe, providing thousands of riders with bikes built for work, not for show.
Our mission is simple: make last-mile delivery more reliable, affordable, and sustainable, one ride at a time. With every bike we deploy, we’re helping build the backbone of smarter, greener cities.
Ride with us into the future – sustainable, efficient, and full speed ahead!
Your Mission
As a Product Manager, you will own the development of our B2B Bike-as-a-Service platform. You will work closely with Engineering, Design, Operations and our B2B customers to shape a product that delivers real value to delivery services and fleet operators.
Defining and prioritizing the product roadmap based on customer and business needs
Working closely with Engineering and Design teams on new feature concepts
Conducting customer interviews and requirements analysis with B2B delivery customers
Defining user stories, acceptance criteria and success metrics (KPIs)
Driving the product development process from idea to launch
Analyzing the market and competitive landscape in Bike-as-a-Service / Mobility
Acting as the interface between leadership, sales, operations and the tech team
Your Skills And Qualifications
Several years of product management experience, ideally in a B2B or platform environment
Experience working with Engineering and Design teams using agile methods
Strong analytical thinking and a data-driven approach to decisions
Strong communication skills with internal and external stakeholders
Experience using tools like Jira, Linear, Notion or similar
Good German and English skills
Nice to have
Interest in mobility, logistics or sharing-economy topics
Experience in mobility, logistics or fleet management
Background in hardware-adjacent or physical products
What You Can Expect From Us
Competitive salary aligned with your skills and experience
High ownership and direct influence on product strategy
Work on a product with tangible social and environmental impact
Flexible working hours and remote/hybrid options
Dynamic environment with short decision-making paths
Diversity
CYCLE values diversity and is an equal opportunity employer, providing a safe and inclusive space for everyone. No matter your gender, gender identity, age, disability, body size, race, religion, sexual orientation or nationality, we would love to receive your application! If there is anything we can do to make your recruiting process more comfortable or accessible, please don’t hesitate to let us know in advance.

The ideal candidate will have several years of product management experience, strong analytical thinking, and excellent communication skills. The company values diversity and offers a competitive salary, flexible working hours, and a dynamic environment.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Who We Are
At JD.com, also known as JINGDONG, we are building one of the world’s most trusted technology and supply chain ecosystems. What started as a bold idea in China has evolved into a NASDAQ-listed, Fortune 44 global enterprise serving over 700 million active customers. Powered by advanced logistics, innovative technology, and a commitment to integrity, JD.com operates across retail, logistics, technology, property and more, creating solutions that make everyday life smarter, faster, and more connected.
Our Global Reach
JD.com is expanding its international footprint through a digitally intelligent, cross-border supply chain and world-class logistics network, supported by platforms such as Joybuy, which brings trusted products to customers worldwide. Today, our teams operate across China, the U.S., U.K., Netherlands, France, Germany, Spain, Brazil, Hungary, Japan, South Korea, Australia, Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia, Indonesia, Saudi Arabia, the UAE and beyond, reaching customers in every corner of the world.
Learn more about who we are and what we do: https://corporate.jd.com/
What We Offer
Joining JD.com means becoming part of a company where people, innovation, and impact go hand in hand. We offer a competitive salary, a great location, and a supportive, people-first culture where your growth truly matters. With access to global projects, learning opportunities, and an inclusive environment that values every voice, you’ll have the space to shape the future with us.
About The Team
You’ll be joining the JD.com team, which values collaboration, ownership, and a commitment to delivering high-quality results in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
Key Responsibilities:
Identify and enhance opportunities within the mobile apps user journey, including features like new customer acquisitions, search, recommendations, promotions, product details, guided shopping, payment options and customer reviews.
Define customer lifecycle and segments, establish performance metrics, and create a structured approach to product performance.
Manage product roadmaps and launch milestones, integrating customer insights, industry trends, and data analytics to optimise user experience.
Work with cross-functional teams to shape and implement app product strategies based on user needs and business plans that extend to our global markets.
Empower Business Initiatives and facilitate decision making from both a user-centric perspective and machine learning capability.
Minimum Qualifications:
Recent 5+ years of experience in product management (mobile apps and web apps) in creating / mapping customer user journey experience from end to end in the e-commerce industry for European countries.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Business Administration, Product Management, Finance, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong analytical and communication skills to effectively collaborate across various teams and leverage data for decision-making.
Bilingual level language proficiency in English and at least one of the following, Dutch, German or French.
Preferred Qualifications:
Proven track record of large user base apps (e.g. managing app products with over 1 million daily active users).
Experience working across different regions and diverse cultures is advantageous.
What We Offer:
A dynamic and challenging work environment in a leading global e-commerce company.
The opportunity to work with a diverse international team and to make a significant impact on JD.com's global presence.
Competitive salary and benefits package, including health insurance, retirement plans, and performance bonuses.
JD.com is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Founding Product Manager @ handly - 20VC backed
jd_text:
handly is an early stage, VC backed company building an AI first operating system for craft businesses. We recently raised €3.75m from 20VC, Stride and Base10 and are building a small, performant team with high ownership and high trust.
We are looking for a
Product Manager
who wants real responsibility: shaping product direction, making decisions with imperfect information, shipping themselves and working hands on with engineering to ship fast and learn faster. This is not a process heavy PM role. It is a builder role.
Tasks
Own product direction end to end, from understanding user problems to shaping solutions and shipping them
Translate company goals into clear product bets, priorities, and tradeoffs
Work very closely with engineering to scope, design and ship features and iterate quickly
Make product decisions with incomplete data and course correct when needed
Develop a deep understanding of users, workflows, and real world constraints
Bring clarity to the team on what we are building, why, and what done means
Help shape how product work happens at handly as we scale
Requirements
This role is about
how you work
, not just where you worked.
You should strongly identify with most of the following:
High agency. You take ownership, push things forward, and do not wait for permission
AI native mindset. You actively use AI tools and think in AI first product patterns
Strong technical understanding and ability to reason about systems and constraints
Good product taste and a clear opinion on what makes a product great
You care about UX and details, even if you are not a trained designer
Comfortable making decisions with imperfect or incomplete information
Comfortable being wrong and correcting course quickly
Experience in fast paced, early stage environments
Side projects or things you have built on your own are a strong plus
Language requirement
Fluent German (spoken and written). The product interface is fully in German and most of our partners communicate exclusively in German.
Fluent English for internal communication.
We care more about how you think and act than years of PM experience.
Benefits
In-office daily:
Berlin-Mitte, modern light-filled workspace with a large balcony
Velocity:
fast loops, direct feedback, no management layers
Growth:
as much responsibility as you can handle
Team:
tight, ambitious group that enjoys building hard things together
Focus:
No stand-ups and only two short meetings per week
If this sounds like your kind of role, we would love to talk.
Our process is simple and fast:
Intro call
Conversation with Basti
Practical discussion around product thinking and collaboration with Kais
On site session with Kais, Basti and Alain. Whiteboarding, product and culture focus (half day)
No lengthy case studies. No drawn out process.

title: Product Manager – Platform Scaling & Product Operations (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Ampr
gestaltet die Verbindung von Immobilie, Finanzierung und Nachfrage. Mit der
LeadSmart Plattform
vernetzen wir Immobilienmakler, Immobilienportale, Finanzierungsvertriebe, Banken und Systeme – und machen aus Kundeninteresse
qualifizierte Leads, planbare Geschäftschancen und skalierbares Wachstum
.
Unsere Technologie schafft die Grundlage für eine bessere Zusammenarbeit im Markt:
früher im Funnel, strukturierter
im
Prozess
und
wirksamer
im
Ergebnis
. So helfen wir unseren Partnern zu wachsen und unterstützen Verbraucher auf dem Weg ins eigene Zuhause.
Wir laden dich ein, Ampr in einer entscheidenden Wachstumsphase mitzugestalten. Aus einer funktionierenden Plattform wird ein wachsendes
Betriebssystem für Lead-Management, Kooperation und digitale Kundenprozesse
im Immobilien- und Finanzierungsmarkt.
Du triffst auf ein erfahrenes, crossfunktionales und hoch motiviertes Team aus
Engineering, Product Management, Design und Business
, das schnell entscheidet, pragmatisch arbeitet und Verantwortung ernst nimmt.
Wir bieten dir eine
hybride Arbeitsweise
mit zwei bis drei Treffen pro Monat an unserem Standort in
Berlin oder Lübeck
. Je nach Terminen und Projektanforderungen können es
vereinzelt auch mehr
Vor-Ort-Termine werden.
Über die Rolle
Unser Product Manager braucht
Verstärkung
. Mit unserer wachsenden Plattform steigen die Anforderungen, die sich

besser bearbeiten lassen. Du bist die
Schnittstelle
zwischen
Technik, Stakeholdern und Nutzerbedürfnissen
und sorgst dafür, dass wir, mit unserem
User Feedback und User Research
im Blick, die richtigen Dinge in der richtigen
Reihenfolge
bauen. Gemeinsam stellt ihr das Product Management breiter auf, bespielt alle relevanten Disziplinen und erschließt neue Themenfelder.
Was erwartet dich bei uns:
Delivery & Engineering
Du steuerst die
Product-Delivery
gemeinsam mit einem erfahrenen, schnellen Engineering-Team – von der
Priorisierung des Backlogs
über die
Definition der Sprint-Ziele
bis zur
Moderation
von Refinements, Reviews und Plannings.
Du sorgst dafür, dass das Team
ohne Blocker arbeiten
kann, und klärst offene Fragen
schnell und pragmatisch
Lösungen & Backlog
Du
entwickelst gemeinsam
mit dem Ampr-Team Lösungen, die Nutzerbedürfnisse zielgenau adressieren und pragmatisch umgesetzt werden können - die
Tickets
entstehen dabei als
gemeinsamer Prozess
, nicht als Einbahnstraße.
Du führst
Triage
durch: Was ist Bug, was ist Produktchance, was ist Kundenwunsch und
entscheidest
, wie dies am besten angegangen wird und welche
Priorität
es hat
Du entwickelst unser
Product-Operations
weiter, dabei ist dein Motto: so viel Prozesse wie nötig, so wenig wie möglich
Nutzer, Stakeholder & Entscheidungen
Du bist verlässlicher
Ansprechpartner
für interne Stakeholder und steuerst die Kommunikation zwischen Technik, Business und Nutzern
Du arbeitest nah am Produktalltag, erkennst Muster in Nutzerfeedback und Produktdaten und
leitest daraus konkrete Produktentscheidungen
ab – pragmatisch und (meistens) ohne wochenlange Rechercheprojekte
Du bist in der Lage
Entscheidungen zu treffen
und
Prioritäten
zu setzen, auch wenn dir nicht immer alle Informationen vorliegen
Was wünschen wir uns von dir:
Erfahrung
2-3 Jahre
als Product Manager oder Product Owner, idealerweise im Startup- oder Scale-up-Umfeld
Du bringst
praktische Erfahrung
mit
agilen
Arbeitsweisen mit und hast dabei auch idealerweise erste
Moderationsaufgaben
in gängigen Formaten.
Fachliche Kenntnisse
Du hast ein tiefes Verständnis für
technische Abhängigkeiten
, APIs und Datenflüsse und kannst mit dem Engineering-Team das Für- und Wieder verschiedener technischer
Lösungswege diskutieren
Du arbeitest mit
Produkt- und Nutzungsdaten
und stützt deine Entscheidungen darauf. Du weißt aber auch, wann du tief genug nachgeforscht hast und dich auf deine eigene Urteilsfähigkeit verlassen kannst
Du verstehst, wie
Priorisierung im Produktkontext
funktioniert, und kannst unterschiedliche Faktoren und Perspektiven zusammenbringen, um fundierte Entsche

title: Senior Product Manager (on-site)
jd_text:
Your role
We're looking for a Senior Product Manager with proven experience building B2B SaaS products to take full ownership of a core slice of our platform. Cloover connects installers and homeowners to accelerate the transition to renewable energy, and we need someone who has shipped multi-sided SaaS products and understands what it takes to make them work for both sides of the marketplace.
You'll work at the intersection of engineering, operations, and real customer problems, turning ambiguous challenges into well-defined, reliably shipped product. This isn't a coordination role. You'll be close to the work, making daily decisions that shape what gets built and how.
You'll own both customer-facing features and the internal infrastructure that powers our operations, from installer onboarding and KYC to invoicing and credit workflows.
You'll report to our Co-Founder & CPO and work within a cross-functional squad alongside engineers and designers. From your first week, your work will reach real customers.
Your mission
Translate customer, commercial, and operational needs into clear product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria — turning vague problem spaces into concrete, buildable solutions.
Own and continuously refine your product backlog, ensuring work is well-prioritised, well-scoped, and ready for engineering to pick up.
Run product discovery — talk to installers, dig into usage data, study competitor approaches, and validate assumptions before committing engineering time.
Work closely with engineers daily to clarify scope, navigate trade-offs, and unblock execution.
Drive initiatives through development, testing, and release, ensuring features ship reliably and with high quality.
Own your product area end-to-end — from identifying opportunities and defining strategy to shipping, measuring outcomes, and iterating post-launch.
Use AI tools as a natural part of how you work — for research, writing, analysis, and prototyping. We're an AI-native team and expect this to be part of your default workflow.
Your profile
We care more about how you think and what you've built than where you've been. Here's what will set you up for success in this role:
5-10+ years of experience as a Product Manager in a software-driven environment, with a track record of shipping product that moved real metrics.
Strong experience with backlog ownership, story writing, and working closely with engineering teams on a daily basis.
The ability to go from a vague problem space to a clear product direction through research, structured thinking, and good judgement under uncertainty.
A pragmatic approach to trade-offs between speed, quality, and scope — you know when to ship and when to push back.
Comfortable making decisions with imperfect information — you identify what needs solving and move forward without waiting to be directed.
Experience running discovery: user interviews, data analysis, usability testing, or other methods to validate direction before building.
You thrive in environments where urgency is the norm, not the exception.
Strong communication skills across technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Based in Berlin and energised by being in the room. We're intentionally office-first — decisions get made in real time, prototypes get stress-tested over lunch, and the founding team is ten metres away. If that's how you thrive, you'll feel at home here.
Nice to have (but not required):
Experience with B2B fintech, embedded finance, or process-heavy internal tooling.
German language skills, given our installer base and internal stakeholders.
Don't meet every requirement?
Apply anyway. We hire for potential and perspective — credentials are a starting point, not a ceiling.
How We Hire
We keep things transparent and respect your time. Most go from first call to offer in under three weeks.
Intro call (30 min) — We want to understand what you're looking for, not just screen your CV.
Hiring manager interview (30 min) — We go deep: your

The candidate will work at the intersection of engineering, operations, and real customer problems, turning ambiguous challenges into well-defined, reliably shipped product.

title: AI Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Info:
Als AI Product Manager (w/m/d) treibst du AI-Projekte in unseren Portfoliounternehmen und im Fund selbst voran - von der ersten Use-Case-Idee bis zur fertigen Umsetzung in Production. Du verbindest Produkt- und Userdenken mit technischer Nähe, baust selbst Prototypen und agierst an der Schnittstelle zwischen Tech-Teams, Portfolio-Stakeholdern und unserem Operating Partner AI.
Deine Aufgaben
AI-Projekte vorantreiben: Du begleitest Use Cases von der Idee über die Konzeption bis zur Umsetzung - gemeinsam mit den Teams aus dem Portfolio und dem FLEX-AI-Team
Produkt- und Userperspektive einbringen: Du stellst die richtigen Fragen, schärfst Anforderungen konsequent vom User her und sorgst dafür, dass aus technischen Möglichkeiten echte Produkte werden, die im Alltag und in Production funktionieren.
Schnelle Prototypen selbst bauen: Mit Tools wie Cursor, Claude Code und etwas Python entwickelst du PoCs, die Portcos und wir im Fund als Ausgangspunkt für die Production-Umsetzung nutzen.
An der Schnittstelle agieren: Du arbeitest eng mit unserem internen AI-Team zusammen und vermittelst zwischen technischer Umsetzung, Portfolio-Stakeholdern und Operating Partner - als Übersetzer, Treiber und Sparringspartner.
Du arbeitest sehr eigenständig, priorisierst selbst und treibst Themen auch ohne enge Führung voran.
Dein Profil
3-5 Jahre Berufserfahrung, idealerweise in einer Mischung aus Produkt- oder Projektarbeit und technischer Nähe
Erfahrung im Bauen von Prototypen: Python-Grundlagen, Umgang mit AI-Coding-Tools wie Cursor oder Claude Code, idealerweise erste Berührung mit APIs und LLM-Integrationen
Solides Verständnis moderner AI-Stacks (LLMs, RAG, Agenten, Evals)
Strukturierte, eigenständige und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise
Klare Kommunikation auf Deutsch und Englisch
Leidenschaft dafür, AI in unternehmerische Praxis zu übersetzen
Was Dich erwartet:
Gestaltungsfreiheit: Viel Raum für eigene Ideen - du entscheidest mit, welche Use Cases wir angehen und wie wir sie umsetzen
State-of-the-Art Tooling: Arbeiten mit den neuesten AI-Tools und -Frameworks, ohne lange Freigabeschleifen
Direkter Impact: Projekte mit spürbarem Effekt auf Business-Prozesse - in mehreren Industrien gleichzeitig
Lernkurve & Verantwortung: Enge Zusammenarbeit mit unserem Operating Partner AI, Tech-Teams und C-Level-Leaders
Benefits
Ein Umfeld, in dem dein Beitrag sichtbar ist und direkte Wirkung entfaltet – sowohl in den Portfoliounternehmen als auch bei FLEX
Unternehmerische Verantwortung und schneller Lern- und Entwicklungspfad
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit erfahrenen Gründern und C-Level-Leaders
Moderne Arbeitsumgebung in Berlin mit flexibler Hybrid-Struktur
Wettbewerbsfähige Vergütung inkl. Bonus und unbefristeter Vertrag
Regelmäßige Events, ein gemeinsamer Sommerurlaub sowie verschiedene Gesundheitsangebote wie beispielsweise Massage / Physiotherapie im Büro
Über uns
FLEX Capital ist ein Private Equity Fonds, der sich auf Wachstums- und Buy-out-Möglichkeiten im deutschen Software- und Tech-Mittelstand fokussiert. Hinter FLEX Capital stehen sechs Seriengründer, die mehrfach marktführende Unternehmen mit internationaler Geschäftstätigkeit aufgebaut haben. Auf dieser Basis teilt das FLEX-Team seine Erfahrungen mit Unternehmern und Management Teams, unterstützt sie Hands-on und hilft ihnen so, die gemeinsamen Ziele zu erreichen.
Kontakt
Join us! Wir freuen uns auf Deine Bewerbung.

title: Product Manager, SaaS (F/M/D)
jd_text:
OUR VISION
When people use our technology to "bridge the gap" between the physical and digital worlds, they don't just capture reality - they create a new one. In this new reality, they are smarter, more productive, more streamlined, and more creative - because they have the digital foundation to build the world they want to live in.
That's what NavVis offers in all our products and services: the tools to not just map the world as it is, but to pave the way to a better future. To forge something new. Physical or digital, there is only one reality. And it's the reality NavVis empowers people to build better.
THE OPPORTUNITY
Does the opportunity to contribute to the strategic vision and roadmap of the NavVis' cloud-based 3d visualization software appeal to you?
In this role, you will closely work with our engineers, UX designers, product managers, and customer-facing teams, to determine strategic priorities and translate them into product enhancements. You will be a crucial part of our Product Reality Access team, which manages NavVis IVION and NVC products. People that are part of this team actively shape the future of 3D reality capture and visualization technologies, bringing state-of-the-art cloud-based software to customers around the globe.
We are looking for your expertise to shape the discovery & development processes and further empower our product teams!
How You Will Make An Impact
You will use your curiosity for continuous improvement by deep-diving into our customers' needs, their "job-to-be-done" and workflows, as well as the reality capture industry
You will play an active part in translating opportunities into product requirements and prioritizing them accordingly
You will be empowered to successfully deliver solutions in very close collaboration with our engineers, designers, and program managers
You will proactively bring together all stakeholder voices to the table, driving key decisions, and removing roadblocks for the team to deliver
You will leverage your analytical and problem-solving skills to measure our products' progress towards NavVis' business and strategic goals and translate this data into action
What Will Help You Succeed In The Role
A university degree in business, science or engineering
At least 3 years' experience in a software product management role, preferably managing a B2B SaaS product
Demonstrated experience with agile software development processes from prototyping to release
A natural curiosity and interest to collaborate with our engineers to understand technical feasibility, dependencies and opportunities deeply
Proven track record of managing diverse stakeholder needs
Data driven approach to tackling product decisions
Fluency in English as it is our official language of communication
German would be an added benefit
How We Will Know We Are a Perfect Match
Your recruiting partner for this role is Rina (she/her). You can expect to go through a screening call, and up to 3 rounds of interviews, where we would love to discover your passion and interests, introduce you to who we are and what drives us, and finally understand how we can potentially add value to each other's growth.
How We Will Keep You Smiling
It's important to take a break from work! We offer 30 days of paid time off per year
Affordable access to a vast network of fitness and wellness facilities through EGYM Wellpass subsidy
Deutschlandticket subsidy to support sustainable travel using public transport
We offer flexible working hours and a hybrid work setup, enabling you to plan your work around your life, and not your life around work!
We offer full visa and relocation support for international candidates
An attractive bike leasing model through JobRad, in line with our commitment towards sustainable mobility
A competitive compensation package that values the skills and experience you bring
Up to 4000 EUR employee referral bonus
Financial support for local language classes to help you in your journey of integrating into th

The company offers a competitive compensation package, flexible working hours, and a hybrid work setup, as well as opportunities for professional growth and development.

title: Produkt Manager (m/w/d) eHealth
jd_text:
Warum diese Stelle?
Rund 7.500 psychotherapeutische Praxen in Deutschland arbeiten täglich mit EPIKUR. Als Product Manager entscheidest du, wie sich dieses Produkt weiterentwickelt – nicht in Abstimmungsrunden, sondern mit echtem Hebel.
Du gestaltest ein Produkt, das direkt beeinflusst, wie gut Therapeut:innen ihre Patient:innen versorgen können. Kein generisches SaaS, keine Konzernhierarchie – sondern ein spezialisiertes System in einem regulierten Markt, der gerade durch Telematikinfrastruktur und Digitalisierung tiefgreifend umgebaut wird.
Deine Aufgaben
Strategische und operative Weiterentwicklung unseres PVS verantworten – von der Nutzeranforderung über die Roadmap bis zum Release
Produktentscheidungen auf Basis von Nutzerforschung, Marktbeobachtung und regulatorischen Anforderungen (TI, gematik, DiGA-Kontext) treffen und klar ins Team kommunizieren
Zentrale Schnittstelle zwischen Engineering, UX und den Fachbereichen sein – du schaffst Klarheit, keine Tickets
Release-Management steuern und Rollouts so gestalten, dass sie für Praxen reibungslos funktionieren
Aktiv bewerten, wie KI-Funktionalitäten sinnvoll in bestehende Workflows integriert werden können – von der Konzeption bis zur Evaluation
Was du mitbringst
Unverzichtbar:
✓ Mehrjährige Erfahrung im Produktmanagement, davon mindestens ein Teil in einem regulierten Umfeld (Healthcare, FinTech, GovTech oder vergleichbar)
✓ Nachweisbare Zusammenarbeit mit crossfunktionalen Engineering-Teams in agilen Strukturen
✓ Sicherer Umgang mit Jira, Confluence und Figma – du kannst technische Anforderungen schreiben, ohne Entwickler:innen als Übersetzer zu brauchen
✓ Deutschkenntnisse C1+ – Nutzer:innen, regulatorische Dokumente und interne Kommunikation laufen auf Deutsch
Ein echtes Plus (kein Muss):
Erfahrung mit Telematikinfrastruktur, gematik-Produkten oder DiGA-Prozessen
Praktische Erfahrung mit KI-gestützter Produktentwicklung (z. B. LLM-Integration, Prompt-Engineering in Produktkonzepten)
Hintergrund in Gesundheitsinformatik, Medizinmanagement oder klinischer Psychologie
Was wir dir bieten
30 Tage Urlaub – für deine Erholung
40-Stunden-Woche mit transparentem digitalem Zeiterfassungssystem
Hybrides Arbeiten: in der Regel 2–3 Tage pro Woche im Büro Berlin-Charlottenburg
Wöchentlicher Company Day mit Teamlunch und echtem Austausch
Deutschlandticket (optional) + betriebliche Altersvorsorge
Corporate Benefits – Mitarbeiterrabatte bei vielen Marken
Gestaltungsspielraum, den du in vergleichbaren Konzernstrukturen nicht findest
Bewerbung — so einfach wie möglich
Kein Anschreiben nötig. Schick uns deinen Lebenslauf – formlos – als PDF per E-Mail. Du hörst innerhalb von 5 Werktagen von uns.
Fragen vorab? Jan Moritz antwortet direkt.

The company offers a range of benefits, including 30 days of vacation, a 40-hour workweek, and opportunities for professional development.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
The Company
PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.
We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.
We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.
Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.
Job Summary:
We are seeking a Product Manager for our enterprise open banking product team. This person will be partnering with internal product and business teams seeking to leverage open banking in a wide range of use cases, as well as with engineering teams to define the requirements and understand the technical architecture to advance our enterprise platform and deliver scalable solutions. This role is an individual contributor.
Job Description:
Essential Responsibilities:
Uses data to build insights on product or platform requirements consistent with the shared vision for the product.
Gathers insights from the customer experience and customer needs to input to product requirements
Analyzes research, market analysis and usability studies, research and market analysis to support data-driven decision making
Monitors product profitability measures, including budget.
Lead sprint planning, daily standups and retrospectives to drive execution. Interfaces with product and technology leadership as needed.
Partners with content developers, data scientists, product designers and user experience researchers to identify new opportunities.
Minimum Qualifications:
3+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.
Additional Responsibilities & Preferred Qualifications:
Key Responsibilities
Provide inputs to help shape the strategy and roadmap for open banking at PayPal.
Drive execution of the open banking roadmap initiatives under your responsibility and become the subject matter expert for these, ranging from technical to business/user centric.
Work closely with open banking use case teams to understand their needs, how open banking can most effectively deliver value to their areas, the benefits that open b

title: (Junior) Product Manager Wheeled Goods (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Germany / Bayreuth
CYBEX is a company that puts tomorrow’s people at the heart of its brand. Always questioning the status quo and not afraid of challenging the establishment, CYBEX has become not just a leader in child safety but is seen as an innovative lifestyle and fashion brand for parents. We offer car seats, baby carriers, kids furniture and strollers enabling parents to keep their little ones safe in style. After growing rapidly in the last few years, we look for ambitious talent to contribute to CYBEX’s success story.
Your seat to fill:
Lead the development of products and manage a full product line within the Wheel Goods category throughout the entire product lifecycle
Identify consumer requirements & specifications and initiate, develop and implement those into the organization
Monitor, analyze and evaluate market trends, consumer behavior and competitor activities through market research to identify market opportunities
Act as a stroller category owner and coordinate between Design, Sales, Marketing, R&D, Quality and Procurement and if required further areas of the organization
Monitor commercial success of product portfolio in market and implement interventions
Continuous review of the product line with regards to sales volume, margin, quality and market positioning
Your stroll to CYBEX:
Successfully completed degree in industrial design or equivalent
First experience as a product manager in an international environment, preferably in the area of consumer goods
Proven track record in managing a global product portfolio as well as good understanding of technical and commercial aspects of a product development process
High affinity for lifestyle and design
Ability to deal with ambiguity and work in a dynamic, results-oriented environment
Highly structured and analytical way of working paired with hands-on mentality
Fluent English skills, German would be a plus
CYBEX operates globally and has employees from copious nations. If you are keen to leave your footprint in a swiftly growing business that offers plenty of opportunities to grow both on a professional and individual level, you’ve come to the right place. You’ll have the chance to develop your personal profile in an international environment. As part of Goodbaby International Holdings Ltd., CYBEX is dedicated to creating an inclusive working environment that encompasses diversity and equal opportunities. At CYBEX, we place great importance on our values: Imagination, Belief, Passion, Strength, Respect, and Love. These values shape our company culture and drive us to develop innovative solutions and achieve exceptional results.

The company operates globally and offers opportunities for professional and individual growth.

title: Principal Product Manager – Internal Developer Portal
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
About the opportunity We are looking for a
Principal Product Manager
to own Jarvis, the Internal Developer Portal for over 4,000 Product and Tech people in Delivery Hero SE. In this critical individual contributor role, you will lead the "Unified Builder Experience" pillar, scaling Jarvis to become the single pane of glass for all engineering teams globally. You will own the product strategy and execution, transforming Jarvis from a proven entity-level solution into an active, AI-augmented control plane that radically abstracts infrastructure complexity and accelerates our software development lifecycle.
At Delivery Hero, we don’t just deliver food; we deliver technology at a staggering global scale. Our engineering ecosystem consists of 4,000+ developers spread across the globe, building the future of "Everyday Apps" for iconic brands including Glovo, talabat, foodpanda, PedidosYa, and foodora.
As the Principal Product Manager for our Internal Developer Platform (Jarvis), you are the architect of the developer experience. Your "product" is the very interface that 4,000 of your peers use every day to build, deploy, and monitor services. We believe our teams should spend their brilliance solving complex logistics for riders, expanding markets for vendors, and creating joy for customers not fighting with CI, hunting documentation or navigating fragmented infrastructure.
Your goal is to build a platform so intuitive and automated that every developer feels it is a tailwind move from "code to customer" with zero friction.
What you will do
Define and Execute Strategy: Own the Jarvis product vision, overseeing its rollout from early adopters (like HungerStation) to global scale across DH Tech Foundations, Glovo, and beyond.
Integrate AI Capabilities: Lead the transition from static directories to AI-powered intelligent discovery, seamlessly connecting code, documentation, people, and service ownership.
Drive 360-Degree Insights: Implement proactive, agentic analysis within Jarvis to alert engineering managers to reliability risks, cost anomalies, and quality dips before they escalate into incidents.
Minimize Cognitive Load: Design the IDP interface (Backstage) to abstract away the "boring" complexities of cloud infrastructure. If an engineer has to look at a manual to deploy a standard service, we haven't succeeded yet.
The "Paved Road" Experience: Create "Golden Paths" that are so efficient and high-quality that developers choose them over custom solutions. You are responsible for making the right way the easiest way.
Value-Stream Focus: Constantly audit the developer journey. Identify where "platform friction" is slowing down delivery to our vendors and riders, and ruthlessly prioritize its removal.
Qualifications
What you need to be successful
8+ years of product management experience, with a proven track record of owning the end-to-end lifecycle for highly technical products (e.g., internal developer portals, developer experience tooling, or platform infrastructure).
Deep empathy for the developer experience, acting as a relentless advocate for our builders to ensure every feature actively reduces cognitive load and solves real user pain points.
Exceptional ability to lead without authority and influence cross-functional teams, securing buy-in from senior engineering stakeholders across disp

The successful candidate will own the product strategy and execution, transforming Jarvis into a unified builder experience. The role involves defining and executing strategy, integrating AI capabilities, driving 360-degree insights, and minimizing cognitive load.

title: PRODUCT MANAGER (M/W/D)
jd_text:
Die Semperit Profiles Leeser GmbH ist ein führender Hersteller hochwertiger EPDM-Dichtungen für Fenster, Türen, Tore und Fassaden am Standort Hückelhoven. Wir entwickeln und produzieren hochwertige EPDM-Dichtungen für die Hochbaubranche und sind eingebettet in die Semperit-Gruppe mit ca. 4.000 Mitarbeiter:innen an zahlreichen internationalen Standorten, die auf eine mehr als 200-jährige Tradition zurückblickt. Hohe Innovationskraft, flache Hierarchien, kurze Entscheidungswege, Flexibilität und Freiraum für Eigenverantwortung zeichnen das Arbeiten in unserem Team aus.
Haben Sie Lust, mit uns die Zukunft zu gestalten und dabei zu helfen, die Welt in Bewegung zu halten?
Bereichern Sie unser Product Management-Team am Standort Hückelhoven ab sofort in Vollzeit als:
Product Manager (m/w/d)
Als Product Manager (m/w/d) sind Sie die zentrale Schnittstelle zwischen Markt, Vertrieb, Entwicklung und internen Fachbereichen. Sie treiben aktiv die Weiterentwicklung unseres Produktportfolios voran und leisten damit einen direkten Beitrag zum Wachstum und zur Zukunftsfähigkeit unserer Business Unit Profiles.
Ihre Verantwortung:
Sie entwickeln unser Produktportfolio kontinuierlich weiter und optimieren interne sowie externe Schnittstellenprozesse
Sie unterstützen den Vertrieb bei technischen Kundenanfragen, Neukundenprojekten und Produkteinführungen
Sie pflegen produkt‑ und marktrelevante Datenbanken und stellen die Datenverfügbarkeit innerhalb der Organisation sicher
Sie erstellen vertriebsunterstützende Unterlagen wie Datenblätter, Produktfolder und Kataloge
Sie sind zentrale interne Ansprechperson für kunden‑ und marktrelevante Fragestellungen
Sie analysieren Markttrends und Wettbewerbsaktivitäten zur Identifikation neuer Produkt‑ und Geschäftschancen
Sie steuern den Ideenfindungsprozess und begleiten die Umsetzung im Produktentwicklungsprozess
Sie übersetzen Markt‑ und Kundenanforderungen in klare technische und produktspezifische Spezifikationen
Sie arbeiten aktiv im Produktentwicklungsprozess mit, leiten Projekte und koordinieren interdisziplinäre Teams
Sie übernehmen die Leitung von Sonderprojekten, insbesondere in den Themenfeldern Nachhaltigkeit und Carbon Footprint
Sie erstellen und pflegen Produktschulungsunterlagen und vermitteln die Inhalte in internen Schulungen
Folgende Kenntnisse und Fähigkeiten qualifizieren Sie für diesen Job:
Abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium im Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen oder ein vergleichbarer technischer bzw. kaufmännischer Studienhintergrund
Mehrjährige Berufserfahrung im Produkt‑ oder Projektmanagement nach dem Studium wünschenswert
Technische Kenntnisse im Bereich Gummi oder Kunststoff bzw. Branchenerfahrung sind von Vorteil
Eine selbstständige, strukturierte und lösungsorientierte Arbeitsweise
Freude an Teamarbeit sowie ausgeprägte Kommunikations‑ und interkulturelle Kompetenz
Sehr gute Deutsch‑ und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
Eine hohe IT‑Affinität und professionelle MS‑Office‑Kenntnisse
Bereitschaft zu gelegentlichen Dienstreisen (ca. 20 %)
Wir bieten Ihnen:
Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten und individuelle Entwicklung
Flexible Arbeitsmodelle und Remote-Optionen
30 Tage Jahresurlaub
Vermögenswirksame Leistungen
Die Möglichkeit, ein JobRad zu leasen
Betriebsärztliche Sprechstunde
Corporate Benefits
Unser Angebot gefällt Ihnen?
Dann bewerben Sie sich direkt über unser Bewerberportal auf www.semperitgroup.com/karriere unter Angabe Ihres frühesten möglichen Starttermins sowie Ihrer Gehaltsvorstellung.

title: Veralto Enterprise System Leader (VESL), Growth Innovation (R&D / Product Management) (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Imagine yourself…
Using your passion to create a culture for continuous improvement across innovation and product teams.
Acting as a change agent, shaping innovation strategy by optimizing systems and workflows.
Empowering teams to unlock breakthrough thinking using data, insight, and experimentation.
It’s all possible with a role at X-Rite Pantone, the global leaders in color science and technology.
X-Rite Pantone, a Veralto company, provides complete end-to-end color management solutions for clients in every industry where color matters. At X-Rite Pantone, you’ll deftly blend the art and science of color to help customers inspire, select, measure, formulate, communicate, and match color so that users get color right the first time, every time. And along the way, you’ll build a vibrant career through rich skill-building opportunities, on-the-job learning, and coaching and training that support your continued development and growth.
This role reports directly to the VP, Global Operational Excellence & VES and is structured as a remote position with approximately 25% travel to Regensdorf (Switzerland) and the United States, where the R&D teams are located. Travel typically consists of one week per month onsite in Switzerland or the US, followed by remote work. The ideal candidate can be based in any city within a 3.5–4 hour train ride of Regensdorf. The primary responsibility of this role is to drive the deployment of VES tools and methodologies across global R&D and Product Management, accelerating innovation outcomes and embedding a culture of continuous improvement.
How You’ll Make a Difference:
Support the deployment of VES tools and methodologies — including Accelerated Product Development (APD), Continuous Delivery (CD), Product Planning (PPG), Strategic Innovation Review (SIR), and Rationalized Roadmap (RRM) within R&D and Product Management teams to accelerate innovation outcomes globally in an agile manner.
Partner with R&D and Product Management leadership to identify, prioritize, and execute high-impact improvement across the innovation pipeline.
Facilitate Kaizen events, rapid improvement workshops, and Value Stream Mapping / Visual Project Management (VSM / VPM) to eliminate waste and reduce New Product Development (NPD) cycle times
Coach cross-functional teams on VES principles and tools, build internal capability, and a self-sustaining culture of continuous improvement across global sites.
Drive adoption of structured problem-solving methods (root cause analysis) to resolve R&D and product development bottlenecks.
Help establish visual management systems and daily management cadence that improve transparency, accountability, and decision velocity across innovation teams.
Collaborate with the VP, Global Operational Excellence & VES, and senior leadership to define, track, and communicate key performance metrics aligned to innovation strategy.
Champion cross-functional alignment by bridging Operational Excellence and R&D/Product Management, ensuring VES initiatives support growth and commercial objectives.
Key Responsibilities & Behavioral Expectations:
Innovation Excellence & Leadership
Help define a winning roadmap for attractive segments utilizing Customer Insight, Problem to Portfolio (P2P), Strategy Partner Grow (SPG), and Technology Development Process (TDP).
Implement, improve, and sustain standard work and operating rhythms for Innovation and Product Management teams.
Support the effective deployment of NPD Planning and NPD Execution
Coaching, Facilitation & Capability Building
Facilitate Kaizen events, workshops, JDIs, and problem-solving sessions.
Provide ongoing coaching on NPD tools – including FMEA, VSS, VPM, and LEx for NPD Commercialization.
Model situational leadership, resilience, and positive change adoption behaviors
Kaizen, CP/CT, & PSP Funnel Ownership
Own the Innovation Kaizen Funnel and ensure high-quality intake, charter, and sustainment.
Help grow the CP/CT talent pipeline, partnering with

title: Senior Consultant (m/w/d) - Strategy and Innovation
jd_text:
Dein Team
Im Team Strategy & Innovation identifizieren und entwickeln wir für unsere Kunden vielversprechende Wachstumsmöglichkeiten und schaffen nachhaltige Lösungen für eine erfolgreiche Skalierung innovativer Geschäftsmodelle.
Mit der systematischen Validierung von Geschäftsideen entlang der Dimensionen Desirabilty, Viability und Feasibility schaffen wir die Grundlagen für sichere unternehmerische Entscheidungen. Wir beraten unsere Kunden bei der Implementierung einer Geschäftsidee hinsichtlich einer Build-, Partner- oder Investitionsstrategie und unterstützen sie als Partner aktiv bei deren Umsetzung. Dabei berücksichtigen wir die gesamte Wertschöpfungskette, ausgehend von einer intelligenten Lieferantensteuerung, über notwendige Transformationsprozesse in der Produktion bis hin zu digitalen Kundenschnittstellen im Vertrieb und der Kommunikation. Auch beim Aufbau eines MVPs, eines Minimum Viable Ventures oder bei der Skalierung bestehender Geschäftsmodelle stehen wir unseren Kunden als kompetente Mitunternehmer oder Investoren zur Seite. Wir handeln dabei stets so, als wäre es unser eigenes Business und arbeiten immer für den Erfolg unserer Kunden.
Deine Mission
Du arbeitest in Projekten in unterschiedlichen Branchen und mit unterschiedlichen Themen und verantwortest dabei u.a. Dinge wie Konzeption, Steuerung und Durchführung. Du stellst dabei eine exzellente Ablieferung und zielsichere Wirkung der Projekte sicher
Du verantwortest den Aufbau von MVPs und Produkten bzw. Produktbereichen bei unseren Kunden sowie auch hy-intern
Du validierst Ideen auf Basis von Prototypen, analysiert in der Validierung gesammelte Daten und definierst Produktvisionen, Strategien und Roadmaps
Als Ansprechpartner:in für unsere Kunden bist Du maßgeblich für das Projektmanagement, Vorbereitung von Kundenterminen und exzellenten Kundenpräsentationen verantwortlich
Du unterstützt bei der Herangehensweise, Unternehmensberatung neu zu definieren in Deiner Rolle als aktiver Teamplayer
Du nutzt verschiedene KI-gestützte Anwendungen selbstständig in deiner Arbeit
Was wir uns wünschen
Du bringst mindestens 3 Jahre Berufserfahrung in anspruchsvollen Umfeldern wie Consulting, Corporate Innovation, Company Builder oder Start-ups mit ausgeprägter Erfahrung in der Entwicklung und Realisierung von neuen digitalen Geschäftsmodellen mit
Dabei lag Dein Fokus vor allem auf analytischen Themen oder Du hast Erfahrungen in den Bereichen Geschäftsmodellentwicklung, Produktentwicklung und Technologieintegration sammeln können
Ein großes Plus ist, wenn Du selber schon einmal gegründet oder ein „Side Hustle” betrieben hast
Du verfügst über einen Master oder vergleichbaren Abschluss
Du bringst ein tiefes Verständnis für Innovationsprozesse, neue Technologien und digitale Geschäftsmodelle sowohl im Bereich B2B als auch B2C mit
Du hast erste Führungserfahrungen gesammelt. Die Kommunikation mit unterschiedlichen Stakeholder:innen stellt für Dich kein Problem dar
Große Datensätze schrecken Dich nicht ab. Ganz im Gegenteil, sie lassen Dich Muster erkennen, die Du mit Deinen herausragenden analytischen Fähigkeiten kreativ aufarbeitest, um daraus pragmatisch Handlungsempfehlungen abzuleiten
Womit Du sonst noch punkten kannst
Du hast ein gut gepflegtes Netzwerk
Dein Teamgeist, der auf Werten wie Zusammenhalt, Exzellenz, Neugier und Unternehmertum basiert, zeichnet Dich aus
Dein Präsentationsstil ist authentisch, souverän und Du bist verhandlungssicher auf Englisch und Deutsch
Du hast bereits Erfahrung mit No-Code/Low-Code- und KI-gestützten Automatisierungstools sammeln können und verstehst die Implikationen aktueller KI-Entwicklungen für unser Geschäftsmodell
Was Dich bei hy erwartet
Ownership & Impact
Direkte Verantwortung ab Tag 1 und sichtbarer Impact auf Kundenprojekte und interne Themen
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit erfahrenen Unternehmer:innen, Strateg:innen und Berater:innen
Möglichkeit zur Beteiligung an hy und Teilhabe an unserem langfristigen Erfolg Viel Gestaltungsspielraum
