title: Business Development Representative
jd_text:
Company Mission
Payhawk is a leading global spend management solution for scaling businesses. Headquartered in London and combining company cards, reimbursable expenses and accounts payable into a single product; its future-facing technology enables finance teams to control and automate company spending at scale.
The Payhawk customer base includes fast-growing and mature multinational companies in 32 countries including LuxAir and Wagestream. With offices in New York, London, Berlin, Munich , Barcelona, Paris, Amsterdam, Vilnius and Sofia; Payhawk is backed by renowned investors such as Lightspeed Venture Partners, Greenoaks, QED Investors, Bek Ventures and Eleven Ventures.
Our values include supporting flat hierarchies, taking ownership and responsibility, seeking and providing feedback, managing constructive critique, and speaking our minds. We understand that the best ideas don’t all come from the same place, so we encourage diversity and inclusion in all areas of our work.
The future of fintech is about more than money, and we believe in work-life balance, continual learning, and empowered teams. We’re also on a journey to measure and improve our environmental and social impact
.
From virtual cards to digital subscriptions, our software and automation help take paper out of the equation for our customers, too.
We’re changing the world of payments, and we’re looking for an exceptional team to help us.
Role Mission
We’re looking for an ambitious, self-driven, and adaptable individual based in Berlin, who
wants to be a part of a huge success story
. As a Business Development Representative (BDR) at Payhawk, you’ll generate new leads and business opportunities for the DACH market,
playing a pivotal role in shaping our success.
You’ll be the first to introduce our platform to potential customers, building relationships and driving pipeline opportunities for our Account Executives.
What will your day look like?
Take ownership of your outbound prospecting campaigns, using a mix of outreach strategies—such as emails, calls, social selling, and events—to engage prospective clients
Leverage marketing-led demand generation campaigns, identifying and following up with prospects to build relationships and generate leads
Build a strong sales pipeline for the DACH market, specifically targeting companies with over 200 employees that fit our Ideal Customer Profile
Proactively identify new niches and create innovative campaign ideas to continuously improve outreach
Analyse your outreach efforts and campaign results to draw insights and refine future strategies
Manage a pipeline of potential clients, ensuring consistent coverage to exceed monthly targets and drive sustainable growth
What does it take to be a successful BDR at Payhawk?
A drive to exceed sales targets and progress quickly your career
Motivation to make a measurable impact
Resilience and a growth mindset
Feeling comfortable working in a fast-paced environment
Strong communication and relationship-building skills
Fluency in English and German (min. C1)
Excitement to work in an AI-first environment, actively using AI tools to move faster, cut repetitive work and have more impact
Why should you join us?
Clear development opportunities toward a Senior BDR role, followed by a transition into an Account Executive position
Structured onboarding, sales coaching, and powerful enablement tools to help you succeed
Competitive compensation package with uncapped commission
Global FinTech (Series B) with $236M in funding
First Bulgarian unicorn with 350 employees globally
4 days in the office to collaborate, learn and win together as a team
30 days of paid time off + 12 work-from-anywhere days
Health and fitness membership
Two company on-sites per year in Bulgaria
Opportunity to use the Payhawk product, with a monthly commuting allowance of 150EUR
Please note that unfortunately we cannot provide visa sponsorship, and to be considered for this role, candidates must be able to provide proof o

title: FCM Business Development Manager
jd_text:
About FCM
FCM is more than just one of the world’s largest travel management companies with a 24/7 reach in over 100 companies. We’re a movement of over 6,500 changemakers, travel gurus, performance improvers and leaders without limits, on a mission to help the world’s biggest companies move smoother. We’re officially a great place to work, but also a company like no other. Why? Because we thrive on change. We’re fuelled by what we call our alternative mindset. This is FCM, where different happens.
About The Role
As Business Development Manager, you will play a pivotal role in accelerating FCM Travel’s growth in one of our most strategically important European markets. Based ideally in Berlin, you will be responsible for winning new small to mid-size corporate clients across Germany, typically managing organisations with annual travel spend of €500k–€20M.
You will own the full end-to-end sales cycle, from prospecting and qualification through to solution design, presentations, negotiation, and closing. Acting as a trusted advisor, you’ll engage with senior decision-makers across procurement, finance, and travel, demonstrating how FCM’s technology-led, flexible service model delivers measurable value and enhances traveller wellbeing.
Working closely with a local sales team and wider regional and global stakeholders, you’ll help build FCM’s brand presence, identify high-potential verticals, and contribute market insight to shape our ongoing commercial strategy. While Germany is the core focus, there is future opportunity to support expansion into the Swiss German market.
This role offers the chance to make a visible impact, combining autonomy, collaboration, and long-term career growth within a high-performing, people-first organisation.
About You
You are a driven, consultative B2B sales professional with a strong track record of winning complex mid-market clients in competitive environments. You bring deep understanding of the German business landscape, including local procurement processes, buying behaviours, and corporate culture.
You will likely bring:
4+ years’ experience in B2B sales, with at least 2 years managing complex, multi-stakeholder deals
Proven success securing mid-market contracts, including six- and seven-figure agreements
An existing network of German business contacts and confidence engaging C-level stakeholders
Fluency in German and English, with strong presentation and negotiation skills
Experience managing a full sales pipeline using Salesforce or a comparable CRM
The ability to work independently, manage a territory, and travel as required
What's In It For You
A welcoming team: Become part of a friendly, collaborative team where everyone is on a first-name basis.
Flat hierarchies and autonomy: We combine flat hierarchies with plenty of personal responsibility, giving you room to showcase your skills and make an impact.
Professional growth: Benefit from excellent training and development opportunities, as well as the chance to work with our international teams.
Flexible hybrid work culture: Remote work is part of our DNA. Our modern, centrally located Berlin office provides an inspiring space whenever you want to work on-site.
30 days annual leave: Enjoy a full 30 days of vacation per year (full-time equivalent) to recharge and explore.
Monthly benefits voucher: Receive a 50€ monthly voucher to choose from various offers via Navit or Emplu.
Employee stock program: Participate in our company's success and growth through our employee stock program.
Health & wellbeing: Take advantage of our health initiatives including company runs, wellness workshops, challenges and more.
Exciting events: Join us at local and international events – our way of saying thank you for your great work.
Travel perks: As part of an international travel company, you'll enjoy exclusive travel-related discounts and appealing benefits.
Our number one philosophy is Our people. Flight Centre Travel Group's promise is to provide an environ

title: Product Manager (Mobile Apps/AI) (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Since the foundation in 2017, MindDoc has developed into Germany's leading provider of video-based psychotherapy.
In addition to video-based psychotherapy, MindDoc also operates a mental health app that has been downloaded onto smartphones by over three million people worldwide.
With our customised offer, we encourage people to take their mental health into their own hands. We make help and support easily accessible. Anytime, anywhere and for everyone.
You can expect a warm and committed team with a start-up mentality, who together and with great passion will do everything they can to raise awareness of video-based psychotherapy and the digital health application and achieve our company goals.
Join the MindDoc team and make psychotherapy and digital health applications easier and more accessible for more people!
The Role:
We are looking for a
Product Manager to help shape the next generation of AI-powered features in our mobile app. You will own capabilities that power the app experience, from conversational features and adaptive onboarding to content personalisation and recommendation. The role is hands-on, app-focused, and rooted in user value, turning modern AI tooling into product experiences that feel intuitive, trustworthy, and genuinely useful.
As a Product Manager (Mobile Apps/AI) (m,f,d) at MindDoc you...
Partner closely with the Product Lead and senior stakeholders to align your roadmap with the wider product strategy, while retaining ownership of your dedicated workstreams.
Drive measurable impact in core areas of the app experience, including conversational features, adaptive onboarding, content personalisation, and emerging AI-powered surfaces.
Own the full lifecycle of AI initiatives, from problem framing and discovery through to vendor coordination, evaluation, production launch, and iteration.
Act as the bridge between the stakeholders working on AI and our product, design, content, BI, and marketing teams, ensuring that models address real user needs and translate into clear app experiences.
Balance day-to-day delivery with longer-term investment, maintaining a healthy mix of new feature launches and the reliability, safety, and quality standards expected of our product.
Contribute to shared frameworks, prompt libraries, evaluation guidelines, and content blueprints that allow other teams to adopt AI capabilities safely and consistently.
You…
Have 2+ years of experience as a Product Manager in a technology-driven environment, with a clear track record of shipping mobile and/or app-based products powered by AI, LLMs, or Machine Learning.
Have experience operating in a regulated or high-trust domain such as health, fintech, insurance, or public services is a strong plus.
Have strong sense of mobile product design and user experience, with attention to flows, friction, and the details that make a feature feel right inside an app.
Have a solid understanding of AI fundamentals, including a clear sense of when to use Large Language Models versus traditional ML approaches such as classification, clustering, or recommendation models.
Have an AI product fluency: familiarity with prompt design, evaluation methods, conversation flow design, retrieval pipelines, and the practicalities of working with third-party LLM providers and vendors.
Have a Data-driven mindset: comfort working with complex datasets and the ability to define and track the right KPIs to measure model performance, user impact, and business value.
Have experience working within an agile environment and a proven ability to coordinate multiple stakeholders across UX, engineering, content, BI, and marketing.
Have ability to translate technical concepts into clear, actionable updates for non-technical partners, bridging product, data, and business.
Regulatory awareness: Familiarity with data protection and high-trust product contexts is appreciated.
Traits
User empathy: You care deeply about the people who use our product and bring that perspective into ever
summary: MindDoc is seeking a Product Manager to shape the next generation of AI-powered features in their mobile app, focusing on conversational features, adaptive onboarding, content personalization, and emerging AI-powered surfaces. The role involves partnering with stakeholders, driving measurable impact, and owning the full lifecycle of AI initiatives. The ideal candidate has 2+ years of experience as a Product Manager in a technology-driven environment, with a clear track record of shipping mobile and/or app-based products powered by AI, LLMs, or Machine Learning.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Consultport is the leading digital platform that connects some of the world's most iconic brands with world-class consultants. We're scaling hard, and
we need a PM obsessed with product-led growth (PLG).
Tasks
This role is not about babysitting a roadmap but turning the product itself into our best sales channel — removing friction, driving activation, and making users fall in love before they ever talk to sales.
What you'll do
Own the PLG strategy: activation, onboarding, habit loops, and expansion
Turn data into decisions — define metrics, run experiments, kill what doesn't work
Work directly with Engineering, Design, and Data in a fast-moving Agile setup
Identify and remove every drop of friction in the consultant and client journey
Collaborate with Sales and Management to align product motions with revenue
Requirements
2+ years of PM experience, ideally at a marketplace or SaaS company
You know what PLG actually means and have shipped it
You use AI tools daily. If you don't, don't apply

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
About the role
Handyman is a well-established field service management platform used by more than 45,000 people across Europe.
We are now entering a major transformation. We are modernising our platform, moving from legacy technology to a modern cloud- and web-based architecture, and changing the way we build products.
We are moving away from feature-led development shaped by individual customer requests. Instead, we want to make clearer product decisions based on customer value, market opportunity and commercial impact.
We are looking for a Product Manager to take responsibility for
Handyman Mobile
, our app for technicians and field service professionals.
This is not a backlog-management or delivery coordination role. You will be expected to lead the product, define priorities and ensure that Handyman Mobile creates measurable value for both users and the business.
What you will do
Own the direction and development of Handyman Mobile
Define and evolve the product strategy and roadmap
Identify and prioritise the most valuable customer and business problems
Build business cases and assess the commercial viability of product decisions
Work directly with customers and users through interviews, research and validation
Use qualitative and quantitative insights to guide priorities
Work closely with Product Design and Engineering in an empowered product trio
Play a central role in the transition from the legacy product to the new platform
Align Sales, Marketing, Support and other stakeholders around clear product goals
Measure outcomes after release and use the results to improve the product
Help strengthen a more product-led, value-driven culture
What this role is not
This is not a Scrum Master, project management or traditional Product Owner role.
We are not looking for someone who mainly manages tickets, writes user stories or passes stakeholder requests to Engineering.
We are looking for someone who can take ownership, make decisions, challenge assumptions and lead the product forward.
What we are looking for
3+ years of experience in Product Management
Experience working according to modern product management principles
Proven ownership of a product or significant product area
Strong experience with mobile applications, ideally in B2B SaaS
Good commercial judgement and the ability to build business cases
Experience with discovery, customer interviews, validation and product analytics
A solid understanding of software development and agile ways of working
Strong communication and stakeholder-management skills
The confidence to stand behind well-founded product decisions
The ability to work independently and get things done
Fluent German and English
Experience in field service management, operational software, replatforming or mature B2B products is a plus.
Why join now
Handyman already has an established customer base, strong market presence and a product used in business-critical daily operations.
However, we are operating a deep product and organisational transformation.The future platform, product experience and ways of working are still being shaped.
This gives you the opportunity to influence not only Handyman Mobile, but also how the company approaches product development going forward.
You will report to the Head of Product and work closely with Product Design and Engineering. Some travel to customers and European offices is expected.

The role involves working closely with customers, Product Design, and Engineering to drive product decisions. The company is undergoing a major transformation, moving from legacy technology to a modern cloud- and web-based architecture. The ideal candidate will have experience in Product Management, strong commercial judgment, and the ability to build business cases, with a solid understanding of software development and agile ways of working.

title: AI Product Manager (m/f/x)
jd_text:
About Us
Cortea
is building the q
uality layer for AI-powered audits
.
Our Audit Quality Agents help audit firms use AI with greater
trust, consistency, and professional oversight
. Built around auditing standards and review workflows, they enable firms to deliver more reliable and reviewable work while increasing capacity across the audit process.
We're building an
AI-native company from day one
. We believe small teams, equipped with AI and empowered to move fast, can solve some of the biggest challenges facing the audit profession.
Your Role
As an
AI Product Manager
, you'll work on products in our AI audit toolbox alongside engineers, designers, auditors and our founders. You'll spend your time understanding what auditors need, helping shape what we build next, and making sure we ship things that actually solve real problems.
What You'll Do
Contribute to the discovery, definition, and delivery of products in our AI toolbox
Talk to customers weekly to uncover pain points and validate solutions
Prototype with AI tools (Cursor, Claude, v0, etc.) to test ideas in hours, instead of weeks
Work tightly with engineering and design throughout the build cycle
Help us understand the messy reality of AI capabilities (and limits) and turn that into product decisions: what's worth building, what's not yet ready, where humans stay in the loop
Bring structure to the problem space: clear writing, sharp questions, useful PRDs
What Success Looks Like
You've contributed to and shipped 3+ meaningful product bets within your first 6 months, learning through iteration and customer feedback along the way.
You've gone deep on at least one core product area (e.g. a specific tool in our AI toolbox, a customer segment, our evals & quality framework, or the UX of agentic workflows) and become exceptionally strong in it
You're trusted by founders, engineers, and customers to steward important product decisions
Who You Are
Have 4–7 years of experience, with at least 2 years in a product role
Have shipped B2B SaaS products that real customers pay for and use
Are AI-native in how you work: you use LLMs, agents, and AI tooling daily and have strong intuition for what they can and can't do today
Have shipped at least one AI-powered feature or product and understand the unique product challenges (evals, latency, hallucinations, trust, UX of probabilistic systems)
Go deep into context to truly understand user problems
Are comfortable in early-stage startup chaos. High autonomy, high ambiguity, high speed
Bonus points
You've founded a company or shipped a serious AI side project
You can read code, run small scripts, or fine-tune prompts yourself
Experience in audit, compliance, legal, or another document-heavy professional services domain
What we offer
High impact & growth: Front-row seat to a fast-scaling AI startup, with the chance to grow alongside the product
Mission-driven culture: First-principles thinking, bold ideas, and a supportive team
Attractive compensation: competitive salary + meaningful equity
Personal development: Learning budget for courses and conferences
Dynamic environment: Central Berlin office, team lunches, retreats, flexible vacation
Interview process
First Call: Intro to Cortea with our Founders Associate Liza
Second Call: Product deep-dive with our Co-Founder & CPO Philipp
Third Call: Deep dive into our culture with our Co-Founder & CEO Valentin
On-site Day (Berlin): Meet the team and work on a real problem together

The company offers a high-impact and growth environment, mission-driven culture, attractive compensation, and personal development opportunities.

title: Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Munich | Hybrid (3 days remote) | Full-time | Product
The role
As Product Manager, you work end-to-end: you understand customer needs, validate them early, prioritise according to business potential, and ship in tight feedback loops. You partner closely with Engineering, Design, and Go-to-Market so the solutions we build are useful in daily work, not just convincing in a demo.
At Alasco, you will build products that help real estate teams plan, control, and communicate financial decisions across investment and construction projects.
AI is part of how we build product at Alasco. In this role, you will also identify where AI can remove manual work, improve decision quality, or make complex workflows easier to handle for our customers.
What you will do
Evaluate product opportunities strategically:
Identify and prioritize product opportunities based on customer workflows, product data and business value, and turn them into clear recommendations.
Validate fast and stay close to users:
Run customer interviews, test assumptions early, and use feedback to sharpen the problem before the team builds.
Own features from discovery to rollout:
Define scope, success criteria, and priorities, ship incrementally, and improve features based on usage and customer feedback.
Work closely with Engineering and Design:
Shape solutions together, clarify edge cases, make trade-offs visible, and help the team move from concept to production.
Turn market feedback into product iterations:
Discover customer needs, product signals, and analyse Go-to-Market feedback, to translate them into concrete product investments.
Contribute to AI-supported product work:
Identify use cases where AI can solve a clear customer problem, and help define quality expectations, evaluation criteria, feedback loops, and user-facing guidance.
Align stakeholders around decisions:
Keep Product, Design, Engineering, Management, and Go-to-Market aligned by making priorities, risks, and trade-offs explicit.
What we’re looking for
Deep experience building B2B SaaS products end-to-end, from discovery to launch and iteration.
Strong product judgment in prioritization, assumption mapping, success metrics, and decisions with incomplete information.
Comfort working closely with engineers and designers, including shaping solutions, clarifying edge cases, and moving from concept to production.
A practical understanding of software products, data-rich workflows, and the constraints that affect product decisions.
Experience with AI in product development, with enough hands-on curiosity to understand where it helps, where it fails, and what users need to trust it.
Clear communication in German and English.
Nice to have
Experience with B2B software in regulated or complex domains (e.g., finance, construction, real estate).
You have worked with evaluation setups, prompting strategies, or human-in-the-loop workflows.
What we offer
VSOP: you participate in Alasco’s long-term success and share in the upside you help create.
€2,000 learning and development budget per year plus 3 L&D days.
Hybrid setup: 2 days in our Munich office, 3 days remote.
Up to 4 weeks workation per year.
28 days vacation.
Navit or Wellpass subsidy of €40 per month.
Regular company and team events.
Modern tools, including AI, to help you work more efficiently, stay better prepared, and learn faster.
A product problem space with real complexity: large budgets, many stakeholders, and decisions that matter long before a building is finished.
Hiring process
Intro Call with Recruiting
Hiring Manager Interview
On-site Interview (including a task)
Meet one of the Co-CEOs
About us
Real estate is one of the world’s largest, most capital-intensive - and least digitized - industries. We’re changing that. Alasco is building the leading AI Platform for Real Estate, purpose-built for an era in which AI fundamentally reshapes how decisions are made.
If you want to work where technological possibility has a direct seat at the decision-making t

The company offers a hybrid setup, learning and development budget, and opportunities for growth and professional development.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
About the Company
We're a fast-growing, VC-backed startup on a mission to bring precision AI forecasting to one of the most under-digitized industries out there: industrial spare parts and procurement. Our platform helps businesses avoid costly, unplanned downtime, which can cost upwards of €20,000 per hour, by giving them accurate, AI-driven forecasts and, ultimately, fully automated procurement decisions.
We've proven our core forecasting technology works. Now we're looking for a Founding Product Manager to help us turn that technical strength into a product customers love to use.
The Role
This is a true founding PM role with high autonomy, direct access to the founding team, and the chance to shape not just the roadmap, but the future direction of the company. You'll bridge the gap between complex technical/AI capability and real customer usability, working hands-on with both our engineering team and our customers.
What You'll Do
Own the product roadmap end-to-end, balancing technical feasibility, commercial priorities, and customer needs
Get deep into complex industrial procurement workflows, including multi-departmental ordering, negotiation processes, and real-world operational constraints
Translate customer feedback into clear, well-scoped tickets, working closely with engineering to prioritise and ship
Engage directly with customers to understand pain points and validate solutions
Get comfortable with the logic behind our forecasting and AI models. You don't need to be a data scientist, but you should be able to verify implementations and challenge assumptions
Bring a strong sense of usability and design. There's no dedicated product designer yet, so this is your chance to shape that function
Support go-to-market efforts, helping translate new features into clear customer-facing communication
What We're Looking For
Proven product management experience, ideally in B2B SaaS, industrial tech, or AI/analytics
Comfort with quantitative and technical concepts, unafraid of formulas, data models, and forecasting logic
Strong commercial instincts and the confidence to make (and defend) prioritisation calls
A hands-on, builder mentality; this is an execution role as much as a strategic one
Bonus: technical fluency strong enough to make small AI-assisted UI changes yourself, with engineering review
An entrepreneurial mindset, comfortable with autonomy and ambiguity in an early-stage environment

The role of a Founding Product Manager is to bridge the gap between complex technical/AI capability and real customer usability. title: Senior Product Manager 🇫🇷
jd_text:
Are you looking to have an impact on the daily life of millions of entrepreneurs in France (and tomorrow in Europe)?
Are you looking for a work environment that values trust, proactivity, and autonomy?
Are our Product Principles aligned with your vision?
Then Pennylane is the right place for you !
Our vision
We aim to become the most beloved financial Operating System of French SMEs and Accounting Firms (and soon, European ones).
We help entrepreneurs rid themselves of time-consuming tasks related to accounting and finance while providing them with access to key financial information to assist in making the best decisions for their business.
About Us
Pennylane is one of the fastest growing Fintechs in France (and soon to be in Europe!)
In 5 years of existence, we’ve managed to :
💻 Make ourselves known as a groundbreaking accounting and financial software for small businesses and their accountants
💰 Raise a total of €225 millions, including from Sequoia, the famous fund from the Silicon Valley who invested early in companies like Google, Facebook, Airbnb, Stripe, Paypal and much more...
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Grow from 7 cofounders to 850+ happy Pennylaners : we’re now recognized as one of the greatest places to work in France (and also remotely), with a 4.6/5 rating on Glassdoor.
🌍 Build an international environment with more than 25 nationalities, with a strong remote-friendly culture, where 30% of the employees are already working from all parts of Europe
🤝 Earn the trust of thousands of customers and accounting firms and obtain outstanding ratings
🚀 Already more than 700,000 small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) and over 4,500 accounting firms use Pennylane in France!
WHY this position is of utmost importance to reach our mission
While some companies count on marketing and sales to gain market share, we are a product-led company and are investing primarily in our platform. You’ll join a team with a highly strategic role and a great opportunity to put your skills and ideas to a good use.
HOW you will contribute to the company as a PM
As a Product Manager, you’ll be part of a 100+ people Product organisation, supported by an engineering team of more than 300 people.
You’ll spend an extensive amount of time continuously discovering the users and their pain points. You will interview them, conduct user research and get your hands in our data platform to gather insights on what needs to be solved.
With those insights and with the company’s objectives in mind, you’ll be empowered to define your OKRs and your roadmaps, to have the strongest possible impact on solving user problems and reaching the company's objectives.
You’ll work hand in hand with a Product Designer dedicated to your squad, as well as a team of Product-centered engineers. You will start with small steps (we like the MVP approach!) and will follow-up on the implementation and future iterations. For us, achieving a common goal matter more than just building features.
WHAT You Can Expect From Your Life At Pennylane
Within one month
You’ll learn everything about our company, our teams, and our vision during the first onboarding week
You’ll familiarize yourself with the product and the accounting industry (You will have access to a lot of documentation and will assist to trainings organized by your peers)
You’ll get a very deep knowledge of our Product organization, methods, and culture
Within 3 months
You’ll have imagined and implemented your first solutions, hand-in-hand with Product Designers and Engineers
You’ll have impacted positively at least one OKR
You will have setup your continuous discovery routine and will be ready to build your roadmap
Within 6 months
You’ll be autonomous at defining your OKRs and your roadmap
You’ll keep having a strong impact via iterations on the solutions you've shipped
You’ll share your learnings and best practices within the team
And beyond
: The product team will double in size in two years.
Which means you’ll have :
Multiple opportunities t

title: Founding Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
You'll join an early-stage, AI-native startup with a product that has already proven market fit. We build cutting-edge AI solutions for Governance, Risk and Compliance (GRC) for enterprises around the world.
Our customers are auditors, risk managers, and compliance teams, which means evaluation rigor, auditability, and EU AI Act readiness aren't afterthoughts for us. They're product requirements.
Tasks
As our first Product Manager, you'll own product end-to-end and work directly alongside the founding team. You will:
Own product discovery and be accountable for outcomes
— we hand you problems and constraints, not solutions. You're measured on the value you create for customers and the business, not on features shipped.
Own the roadmap and prioritization
— driven by customer evidence and company strategy, not by whoever shouts loudest. We do not want a sales-driven roadmap, and neither will you.
Drive customer discovery yourself
— you'll be in front of auditors, risk managers, and compliance teams regularly. We have strong customer relationships and access is provided, but you make it happen; nobody hands it to you.
Build the product operating model from scratch
— discovery cadence, how we decide what to build, how product, design, and engineering work as one team. You're our first PM; you set the standard.
Work as one team with design and engineering
— addressing value, usability, and feasibility together, not throwing requirements over a wall.
Partner with our CEO on go-to-market and pricing
— you bring the product and customer evidence; he owns the commercial side. You'll collaborate closely.
Become a GRC expert, fast
— controls, RACM, audit evidence, the regulatory frameworks. We'll backstop you, but you'll own becoming fluent.
Requirements
You've worked inside a genuinely empowered product organization, and you can show us what good product discovery actually looked like there — we care more about what you've shipped and the outcomes you drove than years on a CV
A track record of owning outcomes, not just shipping features — you can point to a decision you made from customer evidence and the result it drove
Strong product experience in B2B SaaS
You drive your own access to customers — proactive, relentless about learning from real users, never waiting to be handed them
Comfort being the sole PM with no playbook — you can stand up the operating model, not just operate within one
You learn deep, complex, regulated domains fast — and you enjoy it
Strong product judgment under ambiguity — you run discovery on real customers, build conviction, and know when to commit
German at C1 and English at C1 — you'll run discovery with German-speaking GRC teams and work with an international team daily
Nice to have
Comfort working closely with technical founders and engineering teams
Experience with AI-native products or LLM-powered features
Exposure to GRC, compliance, audit, risk, or regulated industries (regtech, Big4, infosec)
Benefits
Full ownership of the product as our first Product Manager, working directly with the founding team from day one
Work at the frontier of how AI-native products are built
A steep learning curve at the intersection of product, enterprise GRC, and AI
A clear path to Head of Product as the company and the product team scale — your trajectory tracks the company's
Direct access to real enterprise GRC customers to learn from and build for
Hybrid in Munich — minimum three days per week in our Munich North office. In-person matters while we build trust and you absorb the domain
Relocation support — if you're moving to Munich, we'll cover the moving company
Auxilius .ai is building AI-powered GRC solutions for enterprises. We're early-stage, fast-growing, and backed by real customers. Our tech stack includes Java & Spring Boot, Angular, Kubernetes on Azure, and OpenAI & Anthropic LLMs.

The company offers a unique opportunity to work at the frontier of AI-native products and build a product operating model from scratch.

title: Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
As our new
Product Manager (m/w/d),
you are responsible for the end-to-end lifecycle management of assigned vehicle lines, ensuring competitive market positioning, strong commercial performance, and alignment with customer needs. Reporting directly to our Head of Carlines, you will be working closely with European markets, Global HQ, and manufacturing partners. You drive product, pricing, and specification strategies across the entire vehicle lifecycle.
We want you to:
Manage the complete lifecycle of assigned vehicle lines, from launch preparation through lifecycle enhancements to run-out strategies
Develop and execute lifecycle initiatives including model year updates, facelifts, special editions, and product enhancements
Define and implement pricing strategies that balance market competitiveness, profitability, and brand positioning
Develop and steer pan-European trim, specification, and option strategies in collaboration with markets and manufacturing partners
Monitor competitor activities, market trends, and customer preferences to identify risks, opportunities, and strategic actions
Lead product enhancement planning processes covering powertrains, features, technology, and design updates
Define portfolio positioning and the strategic role of each model within the European product portfolio
Coordinate pan-European launch activities across Product, Marketing, Sales, PR, and market organizations
Ensure consistency between product positioning, pricing strategy, brand communication, and launch execution
Collaborate closely with European subsidiaries, distributors, Global HQ, and factories to achieve sales and profitability targets
Provide management with strategic recommendations based on market intelligence, performance analysis, and competitive benchmarking
About you:
University degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Engineering, or a related field
7–10 years of experience within the automotive industry, ideally in Product Planning, Pricing, Product Management, or Strategic Planning
Deep understanding of the European automotive market and industry dynamics
Strong expertise in automotive pricing, revenue management, and portfolio strategy
Experience managing vehicle lifecycles, product launches, and specification planning
Strong analytical capabilities with the ability to translate market data into actionable business recommendations
Experience working with automotive market intelligence and benchmarking tools such as JATO
Advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills
Excellent stakeholder management skills with experience coordinating international and cross-functional teams
Resilient, proactive, and comfortable operating in a fast-paced, performance-driven environment
Strong communication skills combined with a creative and commercially minded approach
Fluent in English; additional European languages are an advantage
We offer:
A competitive salary and benefits package
Flexible working hours and a hybrid working policy
Continued learning and professional development
Car sharing and lease mobility program
Complimentary daily lunch at our canteen and subsidized breakfast options
Complimentary fitness facility and the opportunity to join various sporting clubs
Highly international working environment

title: Product Manager - Interfaces
jd_text:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Product Manager - Interfaces based in Germany.
This is a high-impact product role focused on shaping the external and internal interfaces of a leading developer platform used by millions of developers worldwide. You will own the product experience across critical surfaces such as SDKs, APIs, CLI, documentation, and dashboards, ensuring they are cohesive, intuitive, and highly reliable. A key part of your mission will be to redefine these interfaces for both human developers and AI-driven coding agents. You will work at the intersection of product, engineering, and design, translating complex technical systems into seamless developer experiences. The environment is fast-moving, highly technical, and deeply collaborative, with a strong emphasis on autonomy and written communication. Your decisions will directly influence developer adoption, product usability, and the evolution of agent-friendly infrastructure.
Accountabilities
Define and own the product strategy for key developer-facing interfaces, including SDKs, APIs, CLI, documentation systems, and dashboards, ensuring consistency and usability across all surfaces.
Engage directly with a wide range of users—from indie developers to enterprise engineering teams and AI-native platforms—to identify friction points and prioritize impactful improvements.
Translate user needs and technical constraints into clear product requirements, working closely with engineering and design teams to deliver high-quality solutions.
Define what “agent-friendly” means in practice by shaping how AI systems interact with APIs, documentation, and tooling behavior.
Establish success metrics for each release, tracking adoption, activation, comprehension, and automated usage across human and AI-driven workflows.
Drive alignment across distributed teams, ensuring all interface-related work contributes to a unified and coherent developer experience.
Requirements
7+ years of product management experience in developer tools, platforms, or infrastructure products, or equivalent founder-level experience with strong product ownership.
Proven experience owning and shipping external-facing surfaces such as SDKs, APIs, CLIs, dashboards, or developer platforms used by other developers.
Strong technical fluency, with the ability to read architecture discussions, evaluate API design, and collaborate effectively with engineering teams.
Understanding of how modern AI coding agents interact with developer tools, and a clear point of view on designing for agent-driven workflows.
Bias toward execution speed, experimentation, and iterative learning rather than overly detailed upfront specifications.
Hands-on approach to product development, actively using tools, writing code where needed, and forming opinions through direct experience.
Strong async communication skills and ability to operate effectively in a fully remote, written-first environment.
Benefits
Fully remote work with global hiring flexibility
Co-working support or WeWork membership allowance
Equity participation (ESOP) giving ownership in the company’s growth
Tech setup allowance for equipment and work environment optimization
Comprehensive health coverage (100% employee, 80% dependents)
Annual company off-site in an international location
Flexible, asynchronous work culture with high autonomy
Annual learning and development budget for courses, conferences, and training
How Jobgether Works
We use an
AI-powered matching process
to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
Data Priv

title: (Senior) Product Manager (f/m/d)
jd_text:
About Us:
At Customer Alliance, we are literally obsessed with feedback - and that reflects in the culture and the product we’re building. We’re a diverse team (comprising 19 nationalities!) who support and inspire each other every day. We pride ourselves on a positive and growth-oriented work environment where employees are encouraged to share ideas, develop their skills, and have plenty of fun along the way.
About the Role:
As a Senior Product Manager at Customer Alliance, you'll play a central role in shaping how our product evolves — balancing user needs, business goals, and emerging technology. This is a high-impact position for someone who leads with curiosity, builds trust across teams, and turns complex problems into clear direction.
You'll work closely with engineering, design, and commercial teams to drive meaningful improvements to a mature SaaS product, while helping define where it goes next. Your work will directly influence how thousands of hospitality businesses collect, manage, and act on customer feedback.
Responsibilities
:
Strategic Alignment
Define and communicate a product vision aligned with business strategy and technical capabilities.
Analyze market trends, identify innovation opportunities, and validate product ideas.
Set priorities that balance engineering capacity, user needs, and business goals.
Collaboration across Teams
Work with engineering to assess feasibility, align on realistic timelines and resource allocation.
Support maintainability and scalability while advocating for innovation and technical debt reduction.
Execution and Delivery
Lead cross-functional alignment for product launches.
Balance feature development with technical debt and infrastructure improvements.
Market and Customer Focus
Conduct market research to align features with real-world demands.
Define and track product success metrics for user engagement and business impact.
What We Expect:
Qualifications:
You bring 5y+ experience as a Product Manager within B2B SaaS, with clear ownership of a mature product.
You enjoy building from scratch and are genuinely curious about emerging technologies.
You're a strong problem-solver — able to balance short-term customer needs with long-term product vision.
You communicate clearly and build trust easily across diverse teams and with external partners.
You're comfortable navigating ambiguity and helping create clarity where it doesn't yet exist.
If you have it, it’s a plus:
German language skills is a strong plus
Background in startups or high-growth technology companies.
Track record of shipping AI-powered features and integrations.
Experience in the customer feedback, hospitality tech, or review management space.
What We Offer:
An international team and great atmosphere with regular events.
Work from our dedicated Mindspace office in central Berlin (Hausvogteiplatz, Mitte) 2-3 days a week, with flexibility around that.
Full membership at Mindspace (co-working), allowing you to work from any of their locations.
Work with highly experienced and capable people motivated to build great products.
Budget to bring remote team members together and work in person.
The opportunity to define and build the future technology strategy of a growing SaaS company.
Direct partnership with the CEO and leadership team on strategic initiatives.
Pick your own device.
Earn extra vacation days each year (Start 26 days, Max 34 days).
FitX gym membership, available at all locations.

title: Senior Product Manager (all genders)
jd_text:
Join Us in Shaping a Greener Future!
At Resourcify, we're on an ambitious mission to make waste a thing of the past.
We help companies with product recovery and circularity reporting. By combining smart software with practical recycling and take-back solutions, we make it easier for businesses to reduce waste, recover valuable materials, and move towards a circular economy.
Today, global brands such as McDonald's, Johnson & Johnson, Siemens, and OBI trust Resourcify to manage and improve their waste and recycling operations. As a Series B company based in Germany, we're operating in 19 countries around the world building the technology and services that will power the future of circularity.
As our Senior Product Manager, you'll help shape how we create real value for our customers around the world - shaping the future of closed loop products and materials.
Your Mission
As Senior Product Manager, you take ownership from idea to impact.
You'll own a product space in our B2B platform and identify opportunities where AI can solve meaningful customer problems, improve internal processes, and unlock new value across our platform. Working closely with Product, Engineering, Operations, and Commercial teams, you'll turn promising ideas into practical solutions that deliver measurable results.
In this role, you'll:
Own a product space from discovery to launch and how it fits beyond to become part of a platform
Identify customer and business problems, and define product requirements, priorities, and success metrics
Work closely with Engineering teams to bring solutions to life
Validate ideas through customer feedback, testing, and experimentation
Monitor performance and continuously improve outcomes
Act as a bridge between technical and non-technical stakeholders
Help shape Resourcify's long-term product and AI strategy
Essential
What We Value In You
You have several years of experience in Product Management, taking end-to-end ownership of complex products in the B2B space
You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and creating structure where none exists
You have strong stakeholder management and communication skills
You can translate complex topics into clear decisions and actions
You enjoy taking ownership and driving initiatives independently
You are fluent in English - fluency in German is a strong plus!
Nice To Have
Experience working on AI-powered products or features
Experience in SaaS, data products, or platform businesses
Knowledge of sustainability, recycling, waste management, or circular economy topics
Experience working in a fast-growing startup or scale-up environment
Don't tick every box? That's okay. We're interested in people who are excited about the opportunity and motivated to learn.
How We Appreciate And Empower Resourcians
We believe great people do their best work when they have ownership, flexibility, and trust.
That's why we offer:
A fast-growing company with a meaningful mission
Flexible working hours and a high level of autonomy
30+ vacation days with additional flexibility when needed
Remote-first work within Germany and employment options in Austria
Beautiful office spaces in Hamburg and Berlin
Workation opportunities of up to 3 months
Annual company offsites and regular team events
High-quality equipment and a home office budget
A team that genuinely enjoys working together and supporting one another
Our Hiring Process
We aim to keep our process transparent and efficient.
Introductory conversation
Hiring Manager interview
Practical case study
Meet the team

title: Digital Product & Systems Lead-Xiaomi EV
jd_text:
Responsibilities:
• Own the European business systems and digital product roadmap across customer, partner and operational journeys.
• Partner with functional teams to understand business requirements and translate them into structured product requirements, user stories and delivery priorities.
• Act as the primary interface between European business teams and Global Product, Technology and Engineering teams.
• Lead the design, implementation and continuous improvement of customer-facing and internal systems, including CRM, lead management, test drive booking, customer communications, ordering and partner platforms.
• Establish governance frameworks for business requirements, prioritisation, release management and deployment readiness.
• Ensure solutions are designed with scalability, usability, operational efficiency and customer experience at the forefront.
• Lead user acceptance testing (UAT), deployment planning and business readiness activities for new system releases. • Monitor system performance, user adoption and business impact, identifying opportunities for optimisation and automation.
What we're looking for:
• Proven experience leading digital product, business systems, CRM or technology transformation initiatives within automotive, retail, e-commerce or other customer-centric industries.
• Strong track record of translating complex business requirements into successful technology solutions.
• Deep understanding of customer journeys, operational processes and system architecture.
• Experience working closely with product, engineering and technology teams in agile delivery environments.
• Strong programme and stakeholder management capabilities with the ability to influence across business and technical teams.
• Highly analytical with a structured approach to problem-solving and prioritisation.
• Comfortable operating in fast-paced, high-growth environments with evolving priorities and ambiguity.
• Customer-focused, commercially minded and passionate about leveraging technology to drive business performance and customer experience

title: Product Manager Spectrum Monitoring Software (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Introduction
Shape the future of spectrum‑monitoring software from strategy to market.
Tasks
In this role, you expand and optimize the spectrum‑monitoring software portfolio.
You define the product vision, market positioning, and value propositions.
You develop and maintain a strategic product roadmap and manage the full product lifecycle from concept to launch.
You engage with customers, analyze use cases, and derive system and software requirements.
You build ROI‑based business cases and prioritize roadmap items.
You collaborate closely with engineering to define requirements, provide architectural guidance, and set acceptance criteria.
You monitor market trends and the competitive landscape in system software and define go‑to‑market strategies, pricing, and commercial models (e.g., SaaS).
You support cross‑functional teams across sales, project management, and systems engineering and represent the portfolio at industry events, exhibitions, and standardization workshops.
Qualifications
You have a degree in Electrical Engineering, Communications, or a related field and operate confidently in both technical and commercial environments.
You demonstrate solid knowledge of the global spectrum‑monitoring market and relevant RF and signal‑analysis concepts (e.g., SDR, detection/classification); this is considered a strong plus.
You have experience with system‑software products, UX/UI, and requirements engineering.
You are familiar with AI/ML technologies, databases, cloud‑native deployments, and SaaS models.
You possess strong communication and presentation skills.
You show a proven track record of bringing products successfully to market in regulated environments.
You are able to apply market insights to guide product strategy and stakeholder decision‑making.
You are fluent in German and English and are willing to travel.
Contact and further information
We welcome your application! Submissions are preferred online via the application portal. For questions, our chatbot Paul and the recruiting team are available at any time.
Rohde & Schwarz promotes equal opportunities and welcomes applications from people with and without disabilities. We offer an inclusive work environment where all individuals can develop their skills and talents regardless of gender, nationality, ethnic and social background, religion, worldview, age, sexual orientation, identity, and other characteristics.
At Rohde & Schwarz, we develop and produce solutions in the fields of Test & Measurement, Technology Systems, and Networks & Cybersecurity – driven by the vision of a safer and connected world. From software to final production, our more than 15,000 employees create products and systems that set global standards and enable technological sovereignty for industrial, regulatory, and government customers worldwide. Our headquarters, located in the heart of Munich, also serves as our main development site.

title: Product Manager Services (m/f/d)
jd_text:
We, EOS – world market leader for industrial 3D printing, are looking for you to support our
Digital Service Solutions Team
in
Krailling
.
(Hybrid working up to 50% possible)
At EOS, we're more than just a team; we're a community united by technology, where every action is a step towards meaningful change, and our independence is the cornerstone of our mission to make the world a better place for everyone
Your Mission
You drive the development, positioning, and continuous improvement of our global service portfolio, ensuring strong customer value and alignment with the EOS strategy.
You translate market needs, customer requirements, and business objectives into scalable service products and sustainable business models across the entire service lifecycle.
You act as a key interface between Service, Sales, Product Management, and other stakeholders, creating a compelling and consistent value proposition for our service offerings.
You enable commercial success by supporting go-to-market activities, pricing strategies, portfolio planning, and the effective positioning of services within the EOS sales organization.
You continuously monitor business performance, demand, and resource requirements while driving improvements through data-driven decision-making, documentation, training, and stakeholder enablement.
Your Talents
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business, Engineering, or a comparable field of study.
Proven experience (at least 3-5 years) in Product Management, Service Product Management, or a comparable role, including responsibility for portfolio development, pricing, and go-to-market activities.
Experience managing products or services throughout their lifecycle, from concept development to market introduction and continuous optimization.
Strong stakeholder management, communication, and influencing skills in an international and cross-functional environment.
Experience with Microsoft Office tools, knowledge of Salesforce or similar systems is an advantage
Excellent analytical thinking and a structured working style with the ability to structure complex topics and deliver results within deadlines
Fluent English skills, German is a big plus.
Our Benefits
Flexible working time models, 30 days of vacation
Travel allowance for public transport, job bike, parking spaces, charging facilities for electric vehicles
Childcare allowance
Possibility of personal timeout
Health offerings, including a subsidized Wellhub membership (gyms, classes, online training)
Company restaurant
Targeted further education opportunities within our LMS
Corporate Benefits for employees
Are you in?
We are looking forward to your application via our online system! If you have any questions, feel free to contact
Jette Dietrich
via
recruiting@eos.info.
Please understand that we cannot accept applications by e-mail.
Our Vision
:
Over 1000 sharp minds, about 60 nationalities, one goal: accelerating the world’s transition to Responsible Manufacturing.
Our proven technology, industrial 3D printing, has been extending the boundaries of manufacturing for over 30 years.
We work globally networked and prefer to think outside the box – and we’re looking for people who want to shape the future with us.
EOS welcomes individuals from all backgrounds, ensuring equal opportunities without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender identity, expression, sexual orientation, age, or disability. At EOS, equity and inclusion are fundamental principles, and we believe in creating a work environment that is built on empathy, respect, and fairness. We are ALL IN.

title: Product Manager (Mönchengladbach, DE)
jd_text:
We are looking for you as Product Manager (m/f/d) System Solutions Focus Hardware to support product management at our subsidiary Scheidt & Bachmann Parking Solutions GmbH in Mönchengladbach.
Scheidt & Bachmann - Mobility that connects
Whether you're taking the train, parking, refueling or charging, or using public transport – you've most likely already used our technology. For over 150 years, we've been developing innovative and sustainable systems for a mobility infrastructure that gets people and goods to their destinations safely, quickly, and comfortably worldwide. As a family business, we live by values such as trust, reliability, and solidarity – and together we're shaping the mobility of tomorrow.
The business area
Parking Solutions
Under the brand name "entervo," the company offers integrated, modular system solutions for parking management. Its own service network, numerous subsidiaries, and sales partners ensure optimal customer support worldwide. Tailor-made and flexible solutions appeal to a broad range of customers.
We offer
A permanent position in an innovative, crisis-proof and established company where diversity and equality are part of our DNA.
Work-life balance: Look forward to flexible, family-oriented working hours, home office and part-time options, and our childcare allowance.
Good food : Discover the diverse range of dishes in our modern company restaurant.
Structured onboarding and buddy program: We want you to feel comfortable from the start and be able to contribute your know-how directly.
Personnel development and training programs: Continuously develop yourself to provide your team with new impetus time and again.
We value our employees highly: Attractive compensation is just as important to us as a collegial and respectful working environment.
Your tasks
You are responsible for the entire life cycle of selected hardware and system products in the Parking Solutions sector – from product idea to product discontinuation.
You analyze customer requirements, market trends and technological developments and derive requirements for our product roadmap from them.
Together with us you will define and prioritize product requirements, working closely with our stakeholders and representing the product requirements to customers, partners and suppliers.
You manage product changes, component substitutions, and measures to ensure product quality and profitability.
Evaluate the technical and economic impacts of product decisions and support make-or-buy decisions.
Support us with product documentation, regulatory requirements, and lifecycle management.
This is what you bring with you
Successfully completed studies in the field of electrical engineering, mechanical engineering, industrial engineering or a comparable discipline
Several years of professional experience in product management, systems engineering or in a comparable technical function
Technical understanding of electronic, mechatronic, or software-based products
Experience in product lifecycle management
Fluent German and English skills, valid EU work permit
Analytical thinking and working methods, as well as team spirit
Enjoyment in further developing products in a dynamic international environment and setting new trends.
Please note:
This job description is translated from German.
Apply for this job

title: Product Owner (f/m/d)
jd_text:
Sunday creates pocket-sized friends for the mass market. We turn idle moments into meaningful connections for millions of users worldwide.
Sunday focuses on the psychology of care, companionship, and empathy. We build accessible worlds where players don't just solve puzzles, they rescue friends. By combining mass-market accessibility with deep emotional hooks, we are defining the next era of digital entertainment.
About Us
We are a dynamic mobile games company currently operating in an exciting
exploration phase
within the hybrid casual market. Our mission is to blend the instant accessibility and mass appeal of hyper-casual games with fast IAP monetization, subscriptions, and the powerful emotional "I want to have that" triggers that drive high player value. To crack the code on our next big hit, we rely on rapid prototyping, innovative thinking, and a culture that isn't afraid to take chances.
What You’ll Do
Commercial Feature Ownership & ROI: Act as the ultimate owner for individual live titles or high-impact features. Drive the product evolution by applying a strict commercial ROI lens to all decisions—evaluating opportunity costs, resource estimation, and real business value rather than just gut feeling.
The MVP Mindset: Ruthlessly prioritize and define what "Minimum Viable" means for feature prototypes. Convincingly strip away nice-to-haves to test core hypotheses cheaply, validating player retention and monetization as quickly as possible.
Fast Monetization Design: Focus heavily on hybrid-casual monetization mechanics. Drive product strategies integrating early subscription funnels, automated pop-ups, and powerful emotional or visual triggers (cosmetics, exclusive drops, impulse-buys) instead of heavy, slow-burn mid-core systems.
Rigorous Scoping & PRD Creation: Translate product strategy into thorough Product Requirement Documents (PRDs) containing explicit effort estimations and primary/secondary KPI uplifts as a mandatory prerequisite for production hand-off.
Growth & Marketability Alignment: Work hand-in-hand with the User Acquisition (UA) and Creative Ads teams to treat growth and product as a singular engine. Map in-game progression loops directly to CPI and ROAS targets, ensuring marketability signals dictate what actually gets built.
End-to-End Experimentation: Proactively set up A/B tests, live-ops events, and player behavior experiments in our internal data platform (Sundash). Analyze metrics and player friction points to scale winning concepts aggressively or execute fast data-driven pivots.
Pure Product Focus: Dedicate your energy to analyzing systems, drafting feature concepts, and maximizing impact. You will not be bogged down by sprint planning, retros, or daily agile team coordination—those processes are managed entirely by our Production and Project Management team.
What We’re Looking For
Commercial Gaming Mindset: Proven experience in mobile game product management (specifically hybrid-casual, casual, or hyper-casual genres), operating with a "business leader first, game maker second" attitude.
Data-Driven Decisiveness: Exceptional ability to interpret data dashboards and early UA metrics (CPA, CPI, Blended ROAS) to adapt game design, features, and economy balancing on the fly.
Calculated Risk-Taker: Experience working in a fast-paced "fail fast, learn faster" exploration culture, with the ability to launch early, iterate rapidly, and ruthlessly "kill your darlings" when the math doesn't add up.
Monetization Instincts: Deep understanding of modern hybrid economies, early subscription funnels, and e-commerce psychology (retention, conversion, instant consumption hooks) that drive high player value.
Cross-Functional Generalist: An agile communicator who can align seamlessly with Data Scientists on ROI calculations, UA managers on campaign scaling, and Artists/Designers on player-centered UI flows.
Execution & Ownership: Hands-on experience as a clear Feature Owner, with a track record of writing clean, a

The company is operating in the hybrid casual market, blending instant accessibility and mass appeal with deep emotional hooks.

title: Product Owner
jd_text:
Moin – wir sind passify.
Wir automatisieren die Lkw-Abfertigung an Terminals und Logistikzentren – damit Fahrer*innen sich selbst einchecken können, ohne Büros aufzusuchen, und ohne dass Terminals Personal für Dateneingabe binden müssen. Unser Produkt ist live, wächst und löst echte Probleme in der Lieferkette.
Als
Product Owner
entwickelst du unsere SaaS-Lösung weiter und bist du die Brücke zwischen Markt, Kunden und Entwicklung – mit echtem Einfluss auf Produkt, Roadmap und Wachstum.
Das erwartet dich:
End-to-End Verantwortung
: Du setzt Prioritäten im Backlog, schreibst User Stories mit klaren Akzeptanzkriterien und sorgst dafür, dass das Team an den Dingen arbeitet, die wirklich zählen.
Sprints, Reviews, Retros, Planning
: Du führst agile Zeremonien eigenständig durch und hältst das Team im Rhythmus – pragmatisch, nicht dogmatisch.
Einsatz von AI-Tools
: Du setzt LLMs, Vibe-Coding und Rapid Prototyping aktiv ein – nicht als Gimmick, sondern um Ideen schnell zu validieren, Dokumentation zu beschleunigen und Prototypen zu bauen, bevor Entwicklungskapazität draufgeht.
Technische Kommunikation
: Du verstehst, was Entwickler*innen bewegt, kannst Anforderungen präzise formulieren und bist in der Lage, interkulturelle Kommunikationshürden – unser Entwicklungsteam sitzt hauptsächlich in Indien – proaktiv zu überbrücken.
Kundennähe
: Du sprichst direkt mit Kunden aus aller Welt, verstehst ihre Workflows und übersetzt Feedback in konkrete Produktentscheidungen.
Markt & Wettbewerb
: Du hast Entwicklungen im Logistikumfeld im Blick und erkennst frühzeitig, wann Anpassungen sinnvoll sind.
Releases mit Wirkung
: Du koordinierst Releases nah am Markt und stellst sicher, dass jede Iteration echten Mehrwert liefert.
Transparenz ist uns wichtig: Gelegentliche Wochenendarbeit sowie Reisen zu Kunden und Partnern gehören dazu.
Das bringst du mit:
Technisches Grundverständnis
: Du weißt, wie Software entsteht, und kannst mit Entwickler*innen auf Augenhöhe sprechen.
Hands-on mit AI-Tools
: Du arbeitest aktiv mit LLMs (z.B. ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor) und hast Erfahrung mit Vibe-Coding oder Rapid Prototyping – AI ist für dich ein Produktivitätshebel, kein Buzzword.
Agile Methodenkompetenz
: Scrum oder Kanban sind dein Alltag – Sprint Planning, Reviews und Retros führst du souverän.
Sprachkompetenz
: Fließende Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift. Eine dritte Sprache ist ein Plus.
Interkulturelle Stärke
: Du kommunizierst klar und empathisch über Kulturgrenzen hinweg – mit unserem indischen Entwicklungsteam genauso wie mit internationalen Kunden.
Ownership
: Du willst liefern, nicht verwalten. Du nimmst Ownership ernst und bringst Dinge bis zum Ende.
Hintergrund
: BWL, Informatik, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Ingenieurswesen – oder etwas ganz anderes. Was du kannst, ist uns wichtiger als was auf dem Abschluss steht.
Du hast Interesse, aber erfüllst nicht alle Anforderungen? Lass uns gern sprechen!
Das geben wir dir zurück:
Echte Gestaltungsmacht
: Ein Produkt, das live ist und täglich genutzt wird – du prägst es mit.
Attraktive Vergütung inkl. leistungsabhängigem Bonus
: 50.000 – 70.000 €
Flexible Arbeitsbedingungen
: Mobiles Arbeiten und Vertrauensarbeitszeit
Stabilität
: Unbefristete Zusammenarbeit und betriebliche Altersvorsorge
Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten
: Steile Lernkurve, tiefe Einblicke in KI-Technologien und ein Weiterbildungsbudget
Team
: Flache Hierarchien, direktes Feedback, respektvoller Umgang – und ein internationales Team mit echtem Startup-Charakter
Standort
: Modernes Büro in der Hamburger Speicherstadt – zwischen Hafencity und Innenstadt
Passify
steht für Weltoffenheit, Verlässlichkeit und Vielfalt. Bei uns verbinden sich Menschen mit unterschiedlichen Hintergründen, Perspektiven und Erfahrungen zu etwas Großem: einem starken, offenen Netzwerk. Vertrauen, Respekt und Wertschätzung bilden dabei die Grundlage unseres gemeinsamen Handelns und schaffen Zusammenarbeit auf Augenhöhe.
Wir begrüßen Bewerbungen von Menschen unabhängi

The ideal candidate should have a technical background, experience with agile methodologies, and excellent communication skills. The company offers a competitive salary, flexible working conditions, and opportunities for professional growth.

title: Lead Product Manager Trust&Safety
jd_text:
WELCOME TO OLX
We're building
marketplace sustainable ecosystems
that millions of people depend on every month – to buy and sell cars, find homes, land jobs, and trade secondhand goods. It's commerce that matters:
safe
,
smart
, and
environmentally responsible
.
We do this with
courage
to innovate,
commitment
to outcomes,
collaboration
across 50+ nationalities and 7 markets, and a
customer-led
approach that puts real people first. Add a
desire to win
– and you get a culture that's ambitious, fast-moving, and built on trust.
Our brands – including AutoTrader, OLX, Otomoto Property24, and more – are loved by millions. And we're backed by Prosus, one of the world's largest tech investors.
About The Role
Join OLX and see how far you can go.
We are a large-scale consumer marketplace building AI-native product experiences across multiple markets. Trust and Safety is not a defensive function here - it is a strategic growth lever. A platform users trust converts better, retains longer, and earns stronger word of mouth. Fraud, abuse, and bad actors actively destroy the value we create; eliminating them is product work at its core.
We are looking for a Lead Product Manager to own the Trust & Safety product agenda end-to-end. You will define the strategy for how we detect and prevent fraud, protect user integrity, and keep our marketplace safe - while building systems that are intelligent, proportionate, and do not create friction for the millions of legitimate users who rely on us every day. AI is central to how you will do this: not as an add-on, but as the foundation of a new generation of proactive, signal-driven safety infrastructure.
What You Will Do
Own the Trust & Safety product strategy and roadmap translating business risk and user insight into a clear, prioritised plan.
Shift the function from reactive incident response to proactive, intelligence-driven fraud prevention: build the Risk Engine, data signals intelligence, anomaly detection, and investigation tooling that make this possible.
Ship AI-native T&S product experiences: partner with Data Science and Engineering to design, prototype, and productionise real-time detection and decisioning systems using ML models, LLMs, and behavioural signals.
Balance fraud mitigation with growth protection—ensuring T&S interventions do not unnecessarily create friction for legitimate users or throttle marketplace supply/demand. Make hard, evidence-based trade-off decisions.
Define and track the right T&S success metrics: fraud loss rates, false positive/negative rates, time-to-resolution, user trust scores, and financial exposure. Communicate impact in business terms to senior leadership.
Partner closely with Engineering leads, Data Science, Legal, Compliance, and Operations—holding shared accountability for outcomes and navigating competing priorities.
Build long-term instrumentation, monitoring, and evaluation capabilities to measure fraud rates, detection model performance, and contributor/user impact at scale.
Establish business cases quantifying fraud losses and ROI of prevention investments; secure resources and drive prioritisation grounded in data.
Ensure products and strategies meet local and regional compliance, legal, and security requirements across all markets.
Apply AI to how you operate as a PM: faster threat analysis, synthetic user research on abuse patterns, AI-assisted spec writing, automated experimentation pipelines—whatever moves the needle on velocity.
Represent Trust & Safety at the leadership level, presenting strategy, risk posture, and results clearly to executive audiences.
What We Are Looking For
Significant product management experience in the digital sector: consumer facing platform, marketplace, mobility, e-commerce, classifieds, fintech, or similar high scale environment.
Proven experience owning trust related products or problem spaces such as Trust & Safety, Fraud Prevention, Risk, Identity & Verification, Marketplace Integrity, Account Security, Abuse
summary: OLX is seeking a Lead Product Manager to own the Trust & Safety product agenda, defining the strategy for detecting and preventing fraud, protecting user integrity, and keeping the marketplace safe. The role involves building AI-native product experiences, partnering with Data Science and Engineering, and making hard, evidence-based trade-off decisions. The ideal candidate will have significant product management experience in the digital sector, with a proven track record in owning trust-related products or problem spaces.

title: Produkt Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
HEY DU!
Ab sofort
suchen wir Dich als
Produkt Manager (m/w/d)
für unser Eventportal in
Voll- oder Teilzeit
an unserem Standort
im Herzen von München
. Du brennst für Produktentwicklung, liebst es, Wachstum voranzutreiben und hast Lust auf das Start-Up-Feeling? Du schätzt schnelle Entscheidungsprozesse und ein agiles Umfeld, in dem Du richtig viel bewegen kannst? Dann bist Du bei uns genau richtig! Ob als erfahrener Senior oder als Mid-Level Produktmanager auf der Suche nach neuen Herausforderungen – bei uns findest Du das ideale Umfeld, um Deine Ideen und Deine Karriere voranzutreiben.
Wir sind
Kartenliebe
- wachsendes E-Commerce-Unternehmen mit rund 40 Mitarbeitenden, spezialisiert auf individuell gestaltbare Papeterie. Als 100%ige Schwester von MYPOSTER vereinen wir emotionales Design mit digitaler Kompetenz - von KI-gestützter Personalisierung im Konfigurator bis zur automatisierten Produktion. Unsere Prozesse gestalten wir selbst, durchdacht und mit Liebe zum Detail. Dabei kombinieren wir strukturierte Qualität mit dem Spirit eines Start-ups: kurze Wege, viel Gestaltungsfreiraum und ein Team, das gemeinsam anpackt.
DEINE AUFGABEN – HIERFÜR BRENNST DU
Du übernimmst die Product Ownership für unser Eventportal und treibst die Weiterentwicklung von der ersten Idee bis zum fertigen Rollout aktiv voran
Du analysierst Märkte, erkennst Trends, verstehst Nutzerbedürfnisse und übersetzt sie in konkrete Initiativen mit klarem Business-Value
Du entwickelst Roadmaps, priorisierst hart entlang von Kundennutzen & Unternehmenszielen und sorgst dafür, dass Teams fokussiert bleiben
Du validierst Hypothesen mit Daten, entscheidest auf Basis von KPIs und baust Produkte, die messbar performen
Du bist vom Konzept über Briefings bis zum Go-Live involviert, sorgst für Fokus und bringst als Product Manager Dinge mit wirklich impact auf die Straße
Du hinterfragst Prozesse und Features kritisch, filterst Trends und setzt die um, die echten Mehrwert bringen
Du balancierst die Interessen von Design, Tech, Marketing und Customer Care und stellst sicher, dass alle in dieselbe Richtung ziehen
DAS BRINGST DU MIT
Du bringst mindestens 3 Jahre Erfahrung als Produktmanager, Product Owner oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle - gerne auch fortgeschrittene Kenntnisse als Senior
Du verstehst, was Nutzer begeistert und bringst psychologisches Feingefühl mit
Du hast Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit mit Softwareentwicklern und verstehst, wie digitale Produkte gebaut werden
Du packst an, bringst Themen voran und gehst pragmatisch in die Umsetzung, statt Dich in endlosen Meetings zu verlieren
Du gehst nicht nach Bauchgefühl sondern nutzt KPIs als technische Grundlage für smarte Entscheidungen
Du bist nicht nur Ideengeber und Konzeptentwickler, sondern brennst auch für die hands-on Umsetzung
Du besitzt sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (mind. C1-Niveau)
WARUM MIT UNS DURCHSTARTEN?
Deine Rolle, Dein Impact
Vom ersten Gedanken bis zum Livegang steuerst Du den gesamten Produktentwicklungsprozess und sorgst dafür, dass aus Ideen messbarer Erfolg wird
Deine Ideen finden Einzug in unsere Roadmap und beeinflussen damit den Kundennutzen und das Wachstum der Kartenliebe
Du verbindest Design, Tech, Marketing und Customer Care zu einer schlagkräftigen Einheit, die Produkte mit echtem Wow-Effekt liefert
Was Dich bei uns erwartet
Keine Ego-Show, kein Leerlauf: Bei uns zählt, wer mitdenkt, anpackt und mitgestaltet
Entwicklung, die zu Dir passt: Quartalsziele, Feedbackgespräche & Weiterbildung ohne Budgetdeckel - mit Raum für Eigeninitiative und Entwicklung im Alltag
KI als Werkzeug: Du arbeitest mit den neuesten Tools und automatisierst, was geht
Fünf Tage Präsenz für starken Teamspirit: Arbeiten vor Ort mit Flexibilität durch Homeoffice-Ausnahmen und 10 FLEX DAYS
Familienfreundliche & selbstbestimmte Arbeitsweise: Vertrauensarbeitszeit und Gleitzeit für eine Arbeitsgestaltung, die zu Deinem Alltag passt
Gesund & mobil bleiben: EGYM Wellpass, Jobrad oder MINI-Leasing - Du hast die Wahl
Team
