title: FP&A Business Analyst
jd_text:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.
Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.
Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.
Role Overview
The FP&A Business Analyst at eBay is a fantastic opportunity to join our world-class team in Berlin. This role plays a pivotal part in driving growth and excellence in information technology. It focuses on performance analysis, revenue forecasting, and turning data insights into actionable strategies that support our ambitious business objectives.
You will be part of a cross-functional, data-driven team. Collaborating with partners across Marketing, Risk, Product, and Engineering will be essential to provide clear insights and scalable solutions. Our team is committed to crafting business strategy, enhancing decision-making, and supporting balanced growth through analytics and planning excellence.
What You'll Accomplish
Monitor business performance by tracking important metrics, analyzing trends, and identifying risks and opportunities
Conduct variance analysis (actuals vs. forecast/plan) and determine actionable corrective measures
Prepare and present regular performance reports and dashboards for leadership
Develop and manage short- and long-term forecasts aligned with business goals, and lead annual, quarterly, and monthly topline planning cycles
Deliver timely, high-quality analyses to address business questions, investigate data discrepancies, and support evolving priorities
Improve data workflows and reporting processes by implementing automation and scalable solutions
Collaborate with partners across Marketing, Risk, Product, and Engineering to align initiatives with broader business objectives
Find opportunities to use modern technologies, including AI and advanced analytics, to improve reporting, efficiency, and decision-making
What You'll Bring
6+ years of experience applying data analytics to solve complex business problems
Strong hands-on expertise in SQL; proficiency in Python preferred; advanced Excel skills required
Experience in forecasting and financial modeling
Demonstred ability to work with large, complex datasets and perform deep-dive analyses to uncover insights and trends
Demonstrated experience collaborating with cross-functional and geographically distributed teams
Experience building automated workflows, data pipelines, and scalable reporting systems
Effective communication skills with the ability to translate analytical findings into clear business insights and recommendations
Ability to operate effectively in a dynamic environment with strong ownership and a structured problem-solving approach
Degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field, or equivalent experience
Additional Information
Candidates who are available to join at short notice will be preferred.
Additional Details
eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.
We

title: (Senior) Consultant (all genders) Aviation Strategy
jd_text:
What to expect?
We’re open to new ideas, and we welcome people who contribute and think for themselves. The more perspectives we draw on, the more innovative our work can be.
We need people who think strategically and act pragmatically, who want to help shape the future, who identify with our values and outlook, and who are committed to our goals.
People are our priority! We will challenge you and encourage your individual strengths. We offer an appreciative working environment with development opportunities and a wide range of responsibilities. Let’s set new standards together—send in your application today.
Developing aviation strategy for airlines, airports or MRO; sharpening future business models and identifying decisive capabilities to succeed in competition
Designing end-to-end aviation processes with high understanding of commercial and operational coherences
Building on practical experience to re-organize airline or airport functions
Optimizing MRO processes to shorten lead times and increase aircraft availability
Leveraging modern technology such as AI, robotics, IoT, UAS, or AR/VR amongst others towards new ways to approach key questions in aviation on operations and/or commercial side
Transforming airlines, airport and MRO organizations to constantly deliver high performance and customer centricity
Taking responsibility for sub-projects, presenting at Management Board level and actively contributing own ideas for our further development
What sets you apart?
Master’s degree in economics or (aerospace) engineering, ideally supplemented by MBA od PhD
Professional experience in airline, airport or MRO management ideally in a consulting environment
Experience in strategy development and implementation for airlines or airports
Collaborative mindset, intrinsic motivation and passion for teamwork
Fluent English and German, further languages are favorable
High willingness for domestic and international travel
What do we offer?
These benefits make the difference – in daily work, within the team, and for everything that matters beyond the job.
Benefits
Additional benefits can be found on our career page.
Retirement Provison
With the Porsche VarioRente, the Porsche Pension Plan, the MetallRente, and occupational disability insurance, employees are offered comprehensive and flexible retirement and insurance options.
Working Time Models
Flexible models such as remote working, flexible working hours, or sabbaticals allow individual freedom – tailored to personal life phases and needs.
Work & Family
A wide range of programs supports the balance between work and family – including childcare services, holiday childcare programs, parent networks, and a family support service. In addition, Porsche offers opportunities for social engagement.
Health Promotion
We take a holistic approach to health care – including check-ups, vaccinations, ergonomic consulting, digital tools, and comprehensive support through our health management.
Purchase & Leasing Programs
We offer our employees appealing options for purchasing or leasing vehicles, including exclusive long-term leasing offers and special discounts on Porsche, Volkswagen, Audi, and Ducati models.
Employee Meals
Employees can enjoy a wide variety of subsidized meal options with fresh, seasonal, and regional dishes in modern cafeterias, as well as sustainable to-go options.
Mobility
We promote sustainable mobility through discounted Deutschlandtickets, bicycle leasing, and a comprehensive charging infrastructure for electric vehicles at our sites.
Employee Discounts
Employees have access to corporate benefits as well as discounts on Porsche lifestyle products, and in addition, convenient services like DHL parcel lockers.

title: (Senior) B2B Strategy Consultant (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Du willst nicht einfach Marketingkonzepte abliefern. Du willst verstehen, wie B2B-Unternehmen wirklich wachsen und diesen Wachstumspfad mit deinen Kunden gemeinsam bauen.
Als (Senior) B2B Strategy Consultant berätst du direkt auf Entscheider-Ebene und entwickelst gemeinsam mit unseren Kunden integrierte Go-to-Market-Strategien und steuerst eigenständig die Beratungs- und Umsetzungsprojekte, die daraus entstehen.
Wir sind eine 2022 gegründete B2B-Beratung, die sich auf die Schnittstelle von Strategie, Marketing und Sales spezialisiert hat - mit HubSpot als zentralem technologischen Rückgrat. Unsere Kunden sind ambitionierte Software-, SaaS-, IT- und Beratungsunternehmen im DACH-Raum, die systematisches Wachstum anstreben.
Für unseren neuen Standort in Düsseldorf, suchen wir eine Person, die diese Entwicklung tatkräftig mitgestalten will.
Aufgaben
Strategische Beratung unserer Kunden in direkter Zusammenarbeit mit Verantwortlichen aus Marketing und Sales sowie mit der Geschäftsführung
Konzeption und Durchführung von Workshops zur Entwicklung komplexer Go-to-Market-Strategien, Positionierungen und Leadgenerierungskonzepten, remote und vor Ort
Erstellung von Strategiekonzepten, Präsentationen und Wirtschaftlichkeitsanalysen
Eigenverantwortliche Planung und Steuerung von Beratungs- und Umsetzungsprojekten
Aufbau und Weiterentwicklung von Kundenbeziehungen als primäre Ansprechperson
Enge Abstimmung mit dem Umsetzungsteam, damit Strategie und Implementierung in der Praxis zusammenwachsen
Identifikation von Entwicklungspotenzialen in bestehenden Accounts sowie aktive Mitarbeit an der Akquise neuer Kunden
Qualifikation
Beratungserfahrung:
Du bringst mehrjährige Erfahrung in der externen Beratung mit. Du weißt, wie man unterschiedliche Stakeholder strukturiert einbezieht, auf ein gemeinsames Ziel ausrichtet und am Ende belastbare Ergebnisse liefert.
B2B-Verständnis:
Du kennst die Welt komplexer B2B-Kaufprozesse aus eigener Erfahrung. Dein Hintergrund liegt klar im B2B-Kontext, auf der Marketing- oder der Sales-Seite, und du verstehst, wie beide Bereiche ineinandergreifen müssen, damit Wachstum entsteht.
Souveränität
: Du bewegst dich auf unterschiedlichen Ebenen eines Unternehmens sicher und baust schnell tragfähige Arbeitsbeziehungen auf, ob im Workshop mit dem Marketingteam oder im Strategiegespräch mit der Geschäftsführung.
Konzeptionelle Stärke:
Du kannst strategische Zusammenhänge strukturieren, auf das Wesentliche verdichten und in Präsentationen so aufbereiten, dass Kunden nicht nur zustimmen, sondern handeln.
Technologieverständnis
: Du findest dich in der Welt von CRM-Systemen, Marketing Automation und digitalen Sales-Prozessen zurecht. Konkrete Erfahrung in HubSpot ist ein Plus.
Benefits
Eine tolle Arbeitsatmosphäre in einem familiären, humorvollen und ambitionierten Team, in dem Zusammenhalt und Wertschätzung großgeschrieben werden
Spannende Projekte mit strategischem Tiefgang: Du berätst keine Kleinstunternehmen, sondern B2B-Companies aus dem gehobenen MIttelstand
Eine steile Lernkurve durch ständig neue Branchen, Kundenprofile und strategische Herausforderungen sowie viele Weiterbildungs- und Entwicklungsmöglichkeiten
Dein Ankerpunkt ist Düsseldorf. Solange du zu gängigen Bürozeiten erreichbar bist und Kundentermine es erlauben, kannst du flexibel entscheiden, von wo du arbeitest. Unsere Offices in Köln und Berlin stehen dir ebenfalls offen.
Gute Bezahlung, volle Bezuschussung der betrieblichen Altersvorsorge und weitere Benefits wie Urban Sports Club, Jobticket oder Mittagessenzuschuss
Regelmäßige Teamevents sowie unsere jährliche Workation, zuletzt auf Mallorca, Kreta und in Portugal
In unserem Geschäft geht es selten darum, bekannte Konzepte erneut anzuwenden, sondern darum, neue Zusammenhänge zu erkennen und daraus Strategien zu entwickeln, die das Wachstum des Kunden wirklich voranbringen. Wir freuen uns, wenn du ein Teil davon wirst!

title: (Senior) Business Development Representative / BDR (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Über uns
Pactos baut das Betriebssystem für die Zukunft der Fremdpersonalbranche und ist ein B2B SaaS Start-up mit Sitz in München. Die Branche für externe Arbeitskräfte ist riesig – aber geprägt von ineffizienten, manuellen Prozessen, fehlender Transparenz und hohen Compliance-Risiken. Unsere KI-gestützte Software löst genau diese Probleme - von der rechtssicheren Compliance-Prüfung in Echtzeit bis zur vollen digitalen Steuerung der gesamten Prozesse.
Als Sales Development Representative (SDR) bzw. Business Development Representative (BDR) bist du nicht "nur im Vertrieb". Du bist der Motor unseres Wachstums. Ohne dich weiß niemand, dass wir die Lösung für ihre größten Probleme haben.
Was du machen wirst
Du bist die Speerspitze unseres Vertriebsteams. Du identifizierst, kontaktierst und überzeugst Entscheider mit Fokus auf Enterprises – und legst den Grundstein für jeden Deal.
Outbound Power: Du identifizierst Entscheider bei Unternehmenskunden und Personaldienstleistern und kontaktierst sie aktiv, vor allem per Cold Calling.
Qualifizierung mit Tiefgang: Du verstehst in kurzer Zeit die Pain Points unserer Zielgruppe (Risiken, Zeitdruck, operative Ineffizienz) und zeigst auf, wie Pactos diese eliminiert.
Terminierung für High-Impact Demos: Dein Kernziel ist es, hochwertige Demos für unsere Gründer & das Team zu buchen – damit die Opportunity-Pipeline stetig wächst.
Markt-Insights liefern: Du erkennst Trends im Markt, Rückfragen und Objections – und gibst diese direkt ans Produkt & Go-to-Market (GTM) Team weiter.
Aufbauarbeit im Vertrieb: Du hilfst aktiv dabei, unsere Outbound-Playbooks, Messaging-Struktur und Prozesse im Enterprise-Sales kontiniuerlich zu verbessern und trackst Aktivitäten in HubSpot.
Was wir suchen
Leidenschaft für Vertrieb und keine Angst vor Kaltakquise – der Hörer ist dein Werkzeug
Starke kommunikative Fähigkeiten, schnelles Denken, souveränes Auftreten
Eine Hunter-Mentalität – ein „Nein“ ist für dich der Startpunkt, nicht das Ende
Du möchtest Strukturen mit aufbauen, nicht nur abarbeiten
Erste Erfahrung als SDR/BDR im B2B-Bereich ist ein Plus, aber kein Muss
Fließende Deutschkenntnisse, solide Englischkenntnisse
Was wir dir bieten
Ungedeckelte Provisionen – du bestimmst dein Einkommen maßgeblich selbst neben einem attraktiven Fixum
Early-Stage Ownership – du baust die Sales Engine mit uns auf und hast eine klare Entwicklungsperspektive
Enterprise Exposure – du arbeitest nur mit großen Accounts, nicht nur im KMU-Massenmarkt und baust dein Skillset im Software-Vertrieb
Direkte Zusammenarbeit mit Gründern & erfahrenen Sales-Kollegen
Moderne Sales-Tools (HubSpot, Sales Navigator, Aircall, Clay etc.)
Modernes Arbeitsumfeld mit einem klasse Büro im Zentrum Münchens mit üblichen Benefits (z. B. Essenszuschuss, Deutschlandticket-Zuschuss)
Ein Produkt, das einen echten Need löst – du verkaufst kein „Nice-to-have“
So bewirbst du dich
Schick uns einfach deinen Lebenslauf mit deinen bisherigen Vertriebserfolgen oder ein LinkedIn-Profil. Kein Motivationsschreiben notwendig.
Über uns
Pactos
is a venture-backed B2B SaaS start-up based in Munich. We’re revolutionizing the external workforce management with an AI-powered operating system. Used by leading corporates, Pactos help streamline the source-to-pay process with staffing providers in a compliant, scalable, and transparent way. Thus, our customers excel in operation efficiency and re-gain full workforce visibility.

title: Senior Business Development Representative
jd_text:
There are NO limits to your career: come shape the future and be part of a truly unique global culture at OutSystems!
There are NO limits to your career: come shape the future and be part of a truly unique global culture at OutSystems!
Execute outbound calling campaigns to targeted prospects to generate “sales ready” qualified leads and set appointments for the OutSystems sales team to close new business
Respond to inbound inquiries from prospective customers (phone, email, and web), attaining key data to qualify the prospect, generate “sales ready” leads, and set appointments for the OutSystems sales team to close new business
Learn and execute proven processes to generate new sales opportunities
Strategize with top-producing sales managers
Map prospective accounts around organizational structure, people and existing technology
Engage executives in targeted prospect accounts
Manage and maintain a pipeline of interested prospects
Leverage CRM tools to prospect into specific geographic territories and sectors
Qualifications / What You Need To Succeed
Minimum of 2 years Software/SaaS Business Development or related market/business experience
Understanding of the IT industry with a background of prospecting to IT decision makers
Outstanding communicator via phone and email to both technical and non-technical audiences
Ability to change priorities quickly, capacity to handle multiple tasks, perform at high velocity
Proficiency with Microsoft Office, WebEx or similar web conferencing product, and CRM or sales management tools (salesforce.com preferred)
Demonstrate strong desire to keep up-to-date on technology trends, changes, & best practices
The Longer Story
OutSystems enables enterprise teams to build AI-powered applications and agents that reduce manual work, streamline internal operations, and accelerate impact. A proven low-code foundation combined with agentic AI and AI app generation capabilities empowers teams to move up to 10x faster with the assurance of security, scalability, and governance built in.
As the future becomes agentic, our customers need us now more than ever. AI has opened the door to extraordinary possibilities—but inside the enterprise, things are moving fast and feeling chaotic. Some early adopters are making progress in production, but for many, AI tools are sprawling without governance, data isn’t ready, and talent isn’t there yet. Enterprises are still drowning in application backlogs and struggling with legacy systems. But with the right platform, AI doesn’t have to add to the chaos. It can become the breakthrough that brings clarity—and drives real, enterprise-wide impact. At OutSystems, we’ve built that platform, providing the tools necessary for enterprises to overcome these hurdles.
We are looking for passionate, talented, and motivated people to join us in helping our customers build, deploy, and scale apps and agents—fast, helping them accelerate innovation while enabling secure, governed human-AI collaboration.
OutSystems is a truly global company, with more than 850,000 developer community members, 1,700 employees, more than 500 partners, and thousands of active customers in over 75 countries and across 21 industries. Founded in 2001, OutSystems has offices in the United States, United Kingdom, the Netherlands, Portugal, Germany, the UAE, Japan, Hong Kong, Malaysia, Australia, India, and Singapore, and of course has a thriving, worldwide community of remote employees.
Amongst our customers are some of the world's most recognizable brands across diverse industries—brands like Toyota, Heineken, Bosch, KeyBank, and UCLA. These customers are the reason we have a 4.6 star rating on G2. Their success is ours, and their stories demonstrate tangible ROI and transformational impact. We are a 9x Gartner Magic Quadrant Leader for Low-Code Application Platforms and a multi-year leader in the Forrester Wave. We’re recognized not just as leaders but as visionaries with a strong ability to execute, now exte

title: Business Development Representative – Performance Management & C-Level Sales
jd_text:
Standort:
Hamburg (Büro bevorzugt) | remote innerhalb Deutschlands möglich
Start:
01.09. oder 01.10.2026
Business Development Representative (m/w/d)
Du bekommst C-Level-Vorstände ans Telefon, bevor der Wettbewerb überhaupt anruft.
Wer wir sind
ProgressMaker ist ein Startup im fünften Jahr mit einer klaren Mission: Wir sorgen dafür, dass Strategie und Transformation Realität werden – sichtbar, messbar, nachhaltig. Dafür verbinden wir Beratung, Enablement und unsere eigene KI-gestützte SaaS-Plattform. Unsere Kund:innen sind DAX-Konzerne und mittelständische Champions wie Volkswagen, Bosch, die Deutsche Bahn oder MANN+HUMMEL, die ernsthaft an ihrer Strategieumsetzung arbeiten wollen.
Jetzt bauen wir unser Angebot um ein neues Themenfeld aus: Performance-Management – kurzfristiger wirksam als klassisches Strategie- und Umsetzungsmanagement, aber mit derselben Handschrift. Ab Q3 vereinbaren wir dafür die ersten Vertriebstermine, ab Q4 2026 sollen daraus die ersten Deals werden. Auch hier gilt: Wir starten mit Beratung und Professional Services, das langfristige Ziel ist eine eigenständige Softwarelösung. Du bist von der ersten Minute an dabei.
Die Rolle
Als Business Development Representative bist du der erste Kontaktpunkt zwischen ProgressMaker und den Entscheider:innen, die wir erreichen wollen. Kein Umweg über Assistenz oder Mittelmanagement – dein Entry Point ist das C-Level direkt. Du vereinbarst hochwertige Vertriebstermine rund um unsere Themen Strategie & Umsetzung sowie das neue Feld Performance-Management, die Matthias als CEO oder unsere Account Executives übernehmen und zu Deals weiterentwickeln.
Du arbeitest eng mit Matthias sowie Hannes und Timo zusammen, unseren beiden BDRs, und wirst Teil eines kleinen, schlagkräftigen Sales-Teams mit direktem Draht zur Unternehmensspitze.
Deine Aufgaben
Cold Calling in hoher Taktzahl: Du sprichst täglich mit einer großen Anzahl an Vorständen und Geschäftsführer:innen aus Konzernen und Mittelstand
Termine vereinbaren für Vertriebsgespräche zu Strategie-/Umsetzungsmanagement und Performance-Management, die Matthias oder unsere Account Executives in Deals umwandeln
Interessent:innen für Kolbusas Vorstandsdialoge gewinnen – unser exklusives Austauschformat für Top-Entscheider:innen (kleinerer Teil deiner Rolle neben dem Kerngeschäft)
Sicherer Umgang mit unserem Tech-Stack: HubSpot, Notion, Nooks, Northdata & Co. für Sequenzen, Recherche und sauberes Reporting
Kontinuierlicher Ausbau unseres Leistungsangebots für Strategie, Umsetzung und Performance – mit Impulsen, die du direkt aus deinen Gesprächen mitbringst
Enge Abstimmung mit Matthias und den Account Executives, damit aus deinen Terminen echte Pipeline wird
Das bringst du mit
Erfahrung im B2B-SaaS-Vertrieb – Software, kein Consumer-Geschäft (B2C)
Routine im Gespräch mit C-Level: Ein Vorstandssekretariat bringt dich nicht aus der Ruhe, und du triffst in Sekunden den richtigen Ton
Nachgewiesene Cold-Calling-Stärke: Hohe Anrufzahlen pro Tag sind für dich Alltag, kein Ausnahmezustand
Affinität für Sales-Tech: HubSpot, Notion, Nooks, Northdata oder vergleichbare Tools sind dir vertraut – oder du eignest sie dir schnell an
Biss, Eigenantrieb und die Fähigkeit, mit Absagen produktiv umzugehen
Von Vorteil: erste Berührungspunkte mit Beratung, Strategie oder Performance-Management
Das bieten wir
Remote-First oder Büro Hamburg – du arbeitest im Flow, nicht im Meeting-Marathon
Ein großes Themenfeld mit echtem Gestaltungsspielraum
Top-Ausstattung: MacBook Pro, Claude u. a.
Zugang zu einem modernen Sales-Stack: HubSpot, Notion, Nooks, Northdata und damit Top Datenqualität (Direkte Telefonnummern) sowie bis zu 5 Anrufe gleichzeitig möglich
Direkte Zusammenarbeit mit Matthias als CEO und unseren Account Executives – kurze Wege, schnelle Entscheidungen
Ein neues Themenfeld, das du von Anfang an mitprägst – mit klarer Perspektive Richtung eigenständiger Softwarelösung
Basisgehalt von 55.000 €, zusätzlich vordefiniertes Vergütungsframework mit Accel

title: Business Development Lead
jd_text:
Job description
Zama is hiring a Sales, Growth & Strategy Lead for TokenOps, its newly acquired token operations platform (The Block, Business Insider). TokenOps will continue to operate independently, with the same CEO and team, and a sharper mission: bringing Fully Homomorphic Encryption to vesting, distributions, and treasury flows on public blockchains.
The Role: You’ll be Team Member #7 at TokenOps and will report directly to the CEO (Fabio). Around 80% of your time will focus on growth (sales, BD, marketing, acquisition). The remaining 20% will be dedicated to strategy and special projects, including new TokenOps/Zama product and commercial bets, and engaging key stakeholders and leadership within and outside Zama.
Growth, Sales & BD (80%)
Own the pipeline end-to-end: outbound, inbound, qualification, onboarding, activation, and expansion. You’ll take over from the previous BD lead and the CEO.
Evangelize the product and help close deals alongside the CEO.
Build strategic partnerships and co-marketing collaborations that expand TokenOps’ reach, including across the broader Zama ecosystem.
Hit revenue and pipeline targets and report on commercial metrics. Use data (and your gut) to tighten the funnel.
Run acquisition across both paid and organic channels. You should be comfortable running a Google, LinkedIn, or X ad campaign one day, and writing a blog post that ranks the next. We don’t want a pure brand person, and we don’t want a pure performance person.
Own the content engine and co-marketing: blog posts, social, newsletter, thought leadership, joint campaigns, guest posts, podcast swaps, and conference partnerships.
Drive SEO and GEO from zero. Traditional search engines and the new generation of answer engines (ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini) both matter. This includes keyword research, on-page work, link-building, content briefs, structured data, and prompt-friendly content that surfaces in AI answers.
Run our social presence (X, LinkedIn) and community touchpoints.
All of the above can be achieved solo (for initial experiments) or by progressively leading a team and/or freelancers/agencies (for scale).
Strategy & Chief of Staff (20%)
Partner with the CEO on strategic projects: market sizing, competitive analysis, pricing, GTM bets, and new product bets.
Prepare materials for Zama’s CEO and Board: quarterly updates, strategic reviews, and integration touchpoints. You’ll help translate what we’re doing on the ground into the language the parent company needs.
Prepare the CEO for partner meetings, conference talks, and ecosystem calls. Sometimes you’ll own the deck end-to-end.
Firefight. Whatever needs doing this week, you do it: recruiting pipeline, vendor selection, ops—you name it.
Bridge between commercial and product/engineering. Translate market signals into roadmap input.
Preferred experience
At least 2–5 years’ experience across some combination of growth, BD, or sales at a startup, management consulting, or founder/operator roles. Pattern-matching ability matters more than the exact path.
If you’re more junior / less experienced than the above, we still encourage you to apply if you have a PSD degree (Memo from Alan C. Greenberg, 1981). Top-class formal education/experience is a plus, but what we really care about is grit.
Hands-on with both paid and organic acquisition. You’ve run ad campaigns and written content that pulled traffic. Or you’ve done one and are eager to own the other.
A “not my job” attitude won’t fly here. Comfortable in a small, fast-moving startup without hierarchy. Self-directed, resourceful, able to operate with uncertainty.
Strong communication, structuring, and presentation skills. You can build a deck, write a memo, and run a meeting that ends with decisions.
AI-first ways of working. You build your own workflows around Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor, n8n, or whatever else gets the job done. You expect to be heavily augmented by AI, not outsourcing your judgment to it.
Prior work experi

title: Business Developer, VC-backed startup (German-speaking)
jd_text:
About BoWatt
BoWatt is building
agentic requirements and system engineering software for manufacturing
. We believe AI will reach
PhD-level engineering capability within the next 10 years
. This will unlock massive value across several complex manufacturing industries. Cars. Planes. Rockets. Factories. Chips. Medical devices. All will be engineered with agents.
We bootstrapped BoWatt to
€500k+ ARR
before raising our first round.
BoWatt is backed by
tier-1 investors,
including Atlantic Foodlabs, Buildery (CDTM affiliates), and Prequel Ventures, along with angels from Cohere and Luminovo.
You’ll join a small, exceptional team of startup builders, driven by innovation and execution.
Over half the team are former founders across DeepTech AI, psychedelics, and gaming.
We’ve studied and worked at top institutions and companies like
Stanford
,
TUM
,
Google
,
EA
, and
YC
-backed AI startups.
The role
Own outbound prospecting - cold email, LinkedIn, cold calling — to generate a qualified lead pipeline
Convert inbound leads into booked demos, fast and reliably
Build and refine our outreach playbook - messaging, sequences, positioning
Sit in on every demo to understand how we sell and what customers care about
Track your activity in the CRM and flag what's working and what isn't
Nurture long-term plays. Keep lower-priority accounts in motion with thoughtful follow-ups and signal-based reactivations
Work shoulder-to-shoulder with our Founder Steven — no layers, no bureaucracy
You
You are able to easily shake off rejection
You write clearly, speak confidently, and know how to get to the point
You're curious about what customers actually need - not just hitting dial quotas
You're energised by ambiguity, not slowed down by it
You want to grow into closing deals - the Account Executive path excites you
You are self-motived - hunger matters more than pedigree
Requirements
Prior experience in a startup or sales internship
4 days per week in the office in Munich (Meta Office, Balanstraße)
Fluent German required; English is our internal working language
Prior experience in manufacturing is a bonus
What we offer
Competitive salary based on experience (base salary of 55–75k € depending on experience, plus uncapped commission)
25 vacation days/per yr + plus public holidays
WellPass membership
Interview process
30min Screening call (Nat)
1.5hr Sales Interview (CEO + team member)
1hr Culture fit interview (CEO + CPO)
Offer and reference checks

title: Business Development Manager, DACH
jd_text:
At SiteMinder we believe the individual contributions of our employees are what drive our success. That’s why we hire and encourage diverse teams that include and respect a variety of voices, identities, backgrounds, experiences and perspectives. Our diverse and inclusive culture enables our employees to bring their unique selves to work and be proud of doing so. It’s in our differences that we will keep revolutionising the way for our customers. We are better together!
What We Do…
We’re people who love technology but know that hoteliers just want things to be simple. So since 2006 we’ve been constantly innovating our world-leading hotel commerce platform to help accommodation owners find and book more guests online - quickly and simply.
We’ve helped everyone from boutique hotels to big chains, enabling travellers to book igloos, cabins, castles, holiday parks, campsites, pubs, resorts, Airbnbs, and everything in between.
And today, we’re the world’s leading open hotel commerce platform, supporting 50,000 hotels in 150+ countries - with over 130 million reservations processed by SiteMinder’s technology every year.
As a Business Development Manager
you will be a key driver of new business growth across the DACH region, engaging and advising customers across various segments from small independent hotels and boutique properties to large hotel groups and travel tech partners. You will be joining and representing the leading AI technology platform redefining guest acquisition and hotel revenue management. You will be relied on not just to close new business but to strategically influence pipeline growth, long-term partnerships and commercial excellence.
We won't throw you into the deep end - you'll be trained on our products, industry and sales processes. We have a month-long sales training and onboarding program to make sure you are set up for success. You will be supported by a dedicated sales coach and a buddy, as well as your team leader, who will coach, guide and motivate you to achieve your professional goals.
We know that an attractive compensation package is a key motivator in sales. SiteMinder offers you not only this, but also a working environment that represents ambition, collaboration, self-development and customer-centricity. If you identify with our core values (
We Hustle, We Come Together, We Grow, We Make Things Simple
) and you are a sales hunter at heart and able to work at a fast pace, we want to hear from you!
What You'll Do...
Lead full-cycle sales and business development: identify, engage and convert new prospects into customers.
Strategic territory and pipeline growth: define and execute strategic qualification routines, build segmented pipelines from your territory, and prioritise opportunities that deliver high value.
Consistently achieving sales KPIs and targets: work towards daily, weekly and monthly targets. Accountability will be important to help you succeed.
Trusted advisor engagement: conduct insightful discovery and product presentations, consulting on clients’ business and commercial goals, and present proposals to clients in a professional and confident manner. Negotiate contracts as required, maintaining a balance between optimal service and value for the client and SiteMinder's profit margins.
CRM ownership & intelligence: maintain disciplined CRM practices to ensure pipeline, forecasts, reporting, and customer insights are accurate and actionable.
Collaborate across functions: partner with Onboarding, Marketing, Product and Customer Success teams to refine market messaging, feedback loops, and go-to-market strategies that will improve our competitiveness.
Representing the company: work with local industry groups and travel associations to promote SiteMinder’s products and brand. Attend key functions and events, support webinars and other tactical initiatives to raise brand awareness, requiring occasional travel to client locations to attend meetings with relevant managers.
What You Have...

title: Business Development Representative (DACH)
jd_text:
Why join Safeguard Global?
👀
We want to help you
“work in any way
” — that means making time for family, commitments, and life outside of work so that you can have the best of both worlds. When you own what you do and are driven to deliver, you have the flexibility to decide where and how you work. 🚀
Our roles aren’t just remote and hybrid first — they break free from the traditional ways of doing things, paving a way for a
job that works for you.
✅
Are you a
German-speaking sales professional
ready to make an impact? Join our
high-performing EMEA sales team
and play a key role in helping fast-growing companies expand their workforce across borders.
How you will make a difference:
As our
Business Development Representative (BDR)
at Safeguard Global, you’ll drive growth across the
DACH region and wider EMEA markets
. You’ll identify and qualify new opportunities, engaging with HR, Finance, and C-suite stakeholders to understand their challenges and connect them with our global employment solutions.
You’ll collaborate closely with Account Executives, Marketing, and Partnerships to
build pipeline, spark meaningful conversations, and generate new business opportunities
Prospect and qualify new leads through outbound calls, emails, social outreach, and inbound follow-up.
Engage German-speaking HR, Finance, and Operations leaders to understand their needs and showcase Safeguard Global’s solutions.
Collaborate with Account Executives to build account strategies and ensure seamless handoff of qualified opportunities.
Leverage marketing campaigns, events, and partner channels to drive top-of-funnel activity.
Maintain accurate CRM data in Salesforce and provide clear reporting on pipeline health and progress.
Hit and exceed KPIs for qualified meetings, opportunities, and revenue contribution.
Continuously improve your outreach through coaching, feedback, and collaboration with peers.
What will make you stand out:
Proven experience in business development, sales development, or inside sales, ideally in B2B SaaS, HR Tech, or global workforce solutions.
Hands-on experience in the DACH market, with a strong understanding of regional business culture and decision-making.
Native or fluent German speaker with strong English skills, both written and spoken.
Confident communicator with excellent interpersonal and presentation abilities.
Curious, driven, and resilient, motivated to achieve and exceed targets.
Skilled at building rapport quickly and managing multiple conversations simultaneously.
Practical experience with CRM tools (Salesforce preferred) and sales engagement platforms.
Based in Germany, combining remote work with events across Germany and occasional office visits to Barcelona.
Who we are and what we do:
Safeguard Global is….global! With offices worldwide and a workforce in 50+ counties,
we enable global expansion without the risk
by helping 1,500+ customers to hire, manage, and pay their employees in nearly 190 countries. It's all about people! Join us to meet diverse folks, explore new cultures, and connect with amazing talent from around the globe. 🌎
Our Global Benefits:
👨‍💻 Autonomy and Flexibility (work in any way): Remote first, with the flexibility to include life needs like school runs and gym breaks in your schedule, all while maintaining a high standard of work.
🌴 Generous leave: Enjoy a competitive leave package including paid bonding leaves for new additions to your family
🎁 Make a difference: Get 2 paid charitable days off to contribute to causes you believe in
💰 Corporate bonus/SIP: All Guardians are eligible for our annual bonus scheme or sales incentive plan.
✈️ International environment: Grow your network internationally and collaborate across the world. Interact, discover cultures, and tap into local expertise.
⭐ Human centered culture: We emphasize the people factor in everything we do. Our nurturing culture ensures your ideas reach our leaders and your contributions get the recognition they deserve.
📚 Learning:

title: 📍Business Development Manager
jd_text:
Business Development Manager (m/w/d)
Berlin · ab sofort · Vollzeit
NORITUAL LAB ist Deutschlands #1 Matcha-Brand. Bootstrapped auf 8-stelligen Umsatz in 2 Jahren. Jetzt skalieren wir B2B europaweit – Cafés, Gastro, Hospitality, Concept Stores. Der Markt wächst schnell. Wer jetzt reinkommt, baut mit auf.
Was du machst
Lead-Listen entwickeln & qualifizieren – von Null auf Pipeline
Cold Calls & personalisiertes Outreach: von Erstkontakt bis Close
Deals selbstständig steuern, CRM & Prozesse mitentwickeln – nicht nur bedienen
Wöchentlich testen, lernen, skalieren – eng mit dem B2B-Lead
Samples koordinieren, Onboarding neuer Kunden begleiten
Was wir suchen
Uni-Absolvent:in die durchstarten will – oder jemand mit erster Sales-Erfahrung im Outbound/B2B.
Was zählt:
Outbound-DNA – Telefon, Rejection, Iteration machen dir nichts aus
Zahlengetrieben: du denkst in Conversion Rates, nicht in "ich hab's versucht"
Souverän am Telefon & schriftlich – Deutsch & Englisch fließend
Unternehmerisches Denken: du siehst Chancen, testest schnell, lernst aus Daten
Plus:
Erfahrung in Food & Beverage oder Hospitality
Was du kriegst
60–70k
OTE
Echte Ownership – du baust B2B-Europa mit auf
Direktes Founder-Coaching & Playbooks, die wirklich funktionieren
✈ Japan-Trip möglich – bei starker Performance
Startup-Tempo: kurze Entscheidungswege, hohe Transparenz, sichtbarer Impact
Real Talk.
Das hier ist kein Consulting-Job mit Powerpoints und Frameworks. Du gehst raus, baust echte Kundenbeziehungen auf und wirst täglich mit Nein konfrontiert. Dafür lernst du in 12 Monaten mehr über Vertrieb, Marktaufbau und unternehmerisches Denken als in den meisten Einstiegspositionen in 3 Jahren. Wer später selbst gründen oder im Scale-up schnell wachsen will – das hier ist die bessere Schule.

title: Business Development Manager, Logistics
jd_text:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company,who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Business Development Manager, Logistics based in Germany.
This role is focused on driving commercial growth within the logistics and freight ecosystem by identifying, developing, and closing new business opportunities across a wide range of transportation modes. You will be responsible for expanding market presence by building strong client relationships and actively prospecting new customers in a highly competitive environment. The position requires a proactive “hunter” mindset, with a strong emphasis on cold outreach, pipeline management, and consultative selling. You will collaborate closely with operational teams to ensure seamless freight execution and a high-quality customer experience. This is a fast-paced, remote-first role where initiative, resilience, and commercial acumen directly translate into success. Your ability to understand customer logistics needs and turn them into long-term partnerships will be central to your impact.
Accountabilities
Develop and maintain strong business relationships with clients in the logistics and freight space, ensuring ongoing satisfaction and long-term partnership growth.
Identify and pursue new sales opportunities through cold calling, prospecting, and strategic outreach to expand the customer base.
Manage the full sales cycle, including lead generation, pipeline development, negotiation, and deal closure.
Meet and exceed sales activity targets, including call volume, pipeline progression, and revenue goals.
Collaborate closely with logistics coordinators to ensure smooth freight execution and alignment between sales commitments and operational delivery.
Continuously monitor market trends and client needs to identify new business opportunities and growth areas.
Requirements
3-4 years of experience in business development, sales, or account management within the logistics, freight brokerage, or 3PL industry.
Proven ability to develop and execute successful sales strategies in a competitive, target-driven environment.
Strong cold-calling, prospecting, and negotiation skills with a track record of closing deals.
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision while maintaining strong performance and accountability.
Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build trust and long-term client relationships.
Strong customer service orientation with a focus on delivering value and exceeding client expectations.
High level of resilience, adaptability, and comfort working in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.
Benefits
Competitive commission-based compensation structure.
Fully remote work flexibility, allowing you to work from anywhere.
Inclusive and collaborative team culture that values ideas, initiative, and ownership.
Opportunities for professional growth and career advancement within a growing organization.
Dynamic, performance-driven environment with strong support for high achievers.
How Jobgether Works
We use an
AI-powered matching process
to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.
We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!
Why Apply Through Jobgether?
Data Privacy Notice:
By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

Frankfurt am Main

Umzugsbereitschaft erforderlich
Erfahrungslevel:
Junior und Senior

Hybrid (3 Tage pro Woche vor Ort in Frankfurt, 2 Tage remote)

Schnellstmöglich
Über das Unternehmen
Unser Mandant ist ein moderner, stark wachsender Anbieter im digitalen Banking in Deutschland und baut aktuell ein neues Team im Bereich Investments / In App Brokerage auf. Ziel ist es, ein Brokerage-Angebot direkt in eine Banking-App zu integrieren und damit Kundinnen und Kunden den Handel mit Wertpapieren einfach, sicher und digital zugänglich zu machen.
Ihre Aufgaben
Je nach Erfahrungslevel übernehmen Sie Verantwortung in einem neuen, dynamischen Team und gestalten aktiv den Aufbau eines In App Brokers:
Entwicklung und Weiterentwicklung des Geschäftsmodells (z. B. Depotarten, Produkt-/Asset-Universum, Preismodelle)

Aufbau von Reportings, Dashboards und Management-Updates

Senior: Unterstützung bei Vertragsverhandlungen, Partner-Onboarding und Management bestehender Partnerschaften
Senior: fachliche Führung/Coaching von Junioren sowie Praktikant:innen/Werkstudent:innen
Ihr Profil
Abgeschlossenes Studium (Bachelor/Master)
Mindestens 2 Jahre relevante Berufserfahrung in vergleichbarem Umfeld (z. B. FinTech, Banking, Fondsgesellschaft, Strategieberatung)
Erste praktische Erfahrung in einem Digitalunternehmen (idealerweise FinTech), Banking (C&M), Fondsgesellschaft oder Beratung (z. B. Praktika/Werkstudententätigkeit)
Starkes Interesse an Kapitalmärkten, digitalen Finanzprodukten und Neobroker/Neobank-Trends
Sehr gute analytische Fähigkeiten, Zahlenaffinität, strukturierte Arbeitsweise und Eigeninitiative
Sicheres Auftreten und gute Kommunikation mit Stakeholdern
Erfahrung in der Zusammenarbeit mit externen Partnern/Dienstleistern (z. B. Steuerung/Onboarding)
Idealerweise Erfahrung in Vertragsverhandlungen und/oder regulatorischen Themen
Ausgeprägte Hands-on-Mentalität, Ownership und die Fähigkeit, Themen eigenständig voranzutreiben
Sprachen:
Deutsch Verhandlungssicher und Englisch auf sehr gutem Arbeitsniveau
Benefits
Attraktives Gehaltspaket bis zu 85.000 EUR jährlich.
Hybrides Arbeitsmodell: Hybrid (3 Tage pro Woche vor Ort in Frankfurt, 2 Tage remote)
Strukturiertes Onboarding mit unterstützendem Buddy-Programm.
Langfristige Perspektive in einem dynamisch wachsenden Umfeld.
Moderne Unternehmenskultur mit offener Kommunikation, Duz-Kultur und regelmäßigen Teamaktivitäten.
Umfassende Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten durch interne Trainings und Personalentwicklungsprogramme.
Zusätzliche Vorteile wie Mobilitätszuschüsse, zentrale Lage mit optimaler Verkehrsanbindung, moderne Arbeitsplätze und weitere attraktive Mitarbeitervorteile.
Für Kandidat:innen außerhalb der Region: Umzugsbereitschaft erforderlich

title: Business Development Manager – DACH – Germany based
jd_text:
About Group-IB
Founded in 2003 and headquartered in Singapore, Group-IB is a leading creator of cybersecurity technologies to investigate, prevent, and fight digital crime. Combating cybercrime is in the company’s DNA, shaping its technological capabilities to defend businesses and citizens and support law enforcement operations.
Group-IB’s Digital Crime Resistance Centers (DCRCs) are located in the Middle East, Europe, Central Asia, and Asia-Pacific to help critically analyze and promptly mitigate regional and country-specific threats. These mission-critical units help Group-IB strengthen its contribution to global cybercrime prevention and continually expand its threat-hunting capabilities.
Each of us can help make the world a safer place.
Join us!
The Role
As a Business Development Manager, you will be an essential part of the European Sales Team and, in particular, of the German Team, helping to generate great new business opportunities and promoting our solutions across Germany. You will be reporting directly to the Sales Manager.
By deep-diving into the industry, you will have a chance to advance your career in cybersecurity sales and deliver our solutions.
Tasks To Solve
Develop and manage existing customer accounts in Germany and hunt for new business.
Support existing and grow the right IT channel ecosystem.
Execute campaigns to target new sales opportunities through follow-up and outbound cold calls/emails.
Work closely with the Sales Teams, Partner Channel, and Marketing teams on lead management.
Develop our commercial business in your territory under the guidance of the relevant sales executives and ensure an accurate overview and forecasts.
Target, educate and persuade new customers to use Group-IB products.
Update and maintain clients’ database details within CRM diligently.
Qualifications
Hunter and results-driven individual seeking an opportunity to further develop their cybersecurity sales career.
Client-centric mindset and approach.
Good understanding of the indirect sales channels.
Excellent analytical, communication, and presentation skills.
Previous sales experience (5 to 10 years) in a cybersecurity vendor or system integrator.
Strong passion for cybersecurity and high-tech technologies
Fluency in German and English.
Location: based in Germany.
Why GROUP-IB?
Your happiness is important to us: We want every single team member to be happy.
Continuing professional development: At Group-IB, you can choose from various paths to growth: progress as an expert, advance to a management position, try your hand in another department, relocate abroad, or launch a new business area.
Group-IB.A team with extensive international expertise: Do you have experience but are looking for exciting challenges? By choosing us, you will be choosing complex tasks and continuously improving your skills in a fast-growing international company.
Globally recognized technologies: Group-IB’s members are located in 25 countries, and our products and services are sold in 60 countries. What’s more, Gartner, IDC, and Forrester have ranked our technologies among the best in their class. We work with over 450 international partners and about 500 clients.
A culture created by each of us: Group-IB’s employees speak many different languages and understand one another. We respect each other’s beliefs, share common values, and strive toward the happiness of every employee.
Economic stability: Group-IB’s sustainable growth helps rapidly develop careers that would take years to progress as far as most other companies.
What Else Should You Know
Flexible schedule: Group-IB does not have fixed working hours. You choose your schedule. We adhere to the principle advocated by Steve Jobs: “We have to work not 12 hours and head.”
Certificates and training courses: Group-IB specialists hold over 1,000 professional certificates, including CEH, CISSP, OSCP, GIAC, MCFE, BSI, as well as some rare ones that would be a source of pride for experts in forensics, pen

title: Country Manager
jd_text:
An established European healthcare solutions provider is expanding its presence in Germany and is seeking an entrepreneurial
Country Manager
to lead its commercial growth.
The company delivers innovative solutions that help hospitals increase clinical capacity, improve patient access, and optimise the use of diagnostic and treatment infrastructure. Working closely with healthcare providers, it offers flexible, long-term service solutions designed to support hospitals through capacity constraints, modernisation projects, and changing patient demand.
With operations across multiple European countries and significant investment supporting its continued growth, the business is entering an exciting phase of expansion in Germany.
This newly created leadership role offers the opportunity to shape market strategy, build strategic partnerships, and establish a strong local presence.
The Role
Reporting to the international leadership team, you will be responsible for driving the company's commercial development throughout Germany. This is a strategic, hands-on role combining business development, key account management, market development, and cross-functional collaboration.
You will identify new growth opportunities, develop long-term customer relationships, and position the organisation as a trusted healthcare solutions partner rather than simply an equipment supplier.
Key Responsibilities
Develop and execute the commercial growth strategy for Germany.
Identify and secure new business opportunities within the healthcare sector.
Build long-term partnerships with hospitals, healthcare networks, outpatient providers, and diagnostic organisations.
Lead complex commercial negotiations from initial engagement through contract signature.
Grow existing customer relationships while developing new strategic accounts.
Analyse market developments and identify emerging opportunities.
Collaborate with international operations, service, engineering, and leadership teams to ensure successful project delivery.
Represent the company at relevant industry events and conferences.
Candidate Profile
We are looking for an experienced commercial leader with:
A successful background in MedTech, diagnostic imaging, healthcare services, healthcare infrastructure, or capital equipment.
Experience managing regional or national sales activities within Germany.
Strong consultative selling and strategic business development skills.
An established network within German hospitals, healthcare providers, or diagnostic organisations.
A solid understanding of healthcare purchasing processes and decision-making structures.
Experience with service-based business models, managed services, leasing, or long-term healthcare partnerships would be advantageous.
Personal Qualities
Entrepreneurial mindset with a passion for building new markets.
Commercially driven and results-oriented.
Strategic thinker with a pragmatic, hands-on approach.
Excellent relationship-building and stakeholder management skills.
Resilient, self-motivated, and comfortable working independently.
Fluent German and strong English language skills.
Willingness to travel across Germany.
What's on Offer
A unique opportunity to build and lead the German business for a growing international healthcare organisation.
High level of autonomy and ownership.
Direct collaboration with senior international leadership.
Competitive salary, bonus, car allowance, and home-office package.
Excellent long-term career development within an expanding European business.

title: Business Development Manager Yacht Insurance (m/f/x)
jd_text:
Looking for more than just a job? Join forces with a dynamically growing company with an exciting product and a worldwide customer base.
Since 1999, we have been insuring high-quality sailing and motor yachts – from daysailers to superyachts. Thanks to our expertise and customer focus, we have established ourselves as one of the leading solution providers worldwide.
We don't manage the relationships with yacht owners ourselves, but through a worldwide network of strong partners with whom we work closely and trustingly. These include yacht managers, boat dealers, insurance brokers, shipyards, and many others. Our partners' success is therefore not a side issue, but a cornerstone of our strategy. The more successful our partners are, the more successful we are.
This principle guides our daily priorities. Our team currently consists of thirty customer-focused and highly motivated employees. To meet the steadily growing demand for our insurance solutions and seize further growth opportunities, we are looking for the following addition to our team:
Tasks
Build win-win partnerships with the international yachting industry
Identify and approach potential partners through various channels
Onboard and train newly acquired partners
Monitor and manage existing partnerships
Represent Sealogy at national and international yacht shows and events

Successfully completed degree or vocational training
Network and many years of experience in the international yachting industry
Interest in and enthusiasm for the world of yachting
Fluent spoken and written in English. Additional languages are an advantage

Benefits
An exciting and varied role in a dynamic environment
Attractive development opportunities with corresponding effort and commitment
Fair compensation with exciting growth opportunities
Attractive working conditions with regular participation in great events
The chance to help shape a company and its processes and structures

We look forward to hearing from you!

title: Key Account Manager – Packaging (m / f / d)
jd_text:
Purpose of the Role:
Purpose: Initiative and independent support of the Key Accounts in technical and commercial matters within a defined sales territory/market segment.
Goal: Realization of sales targets in the Industry & Consumer Goods segment; in particular, planned and rational acquisition and customer consultation to increase sales per customer and generate an appropriate contribution margin.
Responsibilities: Customer support, market observation and reporting on specific circumstances in the sales territory / market segment.
Main Responsibilities / Tasks:
Planning of customer visits and their careful preparation according to the specific circumstances of individual customers.
Customer visits for consultation and sales (identifying the customer's problem solution with the company’s own offering).
Preparation of sales quotations according to given guidelines.
Competent conduct of sales negotiations.
Forwarding of customer complaints to the Sales Management.
Targeted use of application technology in consultation with the Sales Manager.
Assistance in the execution of tests, market actions, new product launches, trade fairs, etc.
Ongoing maintenance of work documentation (customer files & SSS).
Forwarding of customer observations, wishes and suggestions to the Sales Manager Industry & Consumer Goods and Product Managers.
Ongoing reporting on competitor observations (e.g., pricing policy, sales promotion activities, advertising, product range, sales conditions, etc.) to the Sales Manager.
Cooperation with the internal sales department.
Special Tasks:
Cultivating contacts with relevant professional organizations and authorities.
Preparation of monthly reports and, if necessary, conducting market analyses for the entity in Germany as well as for the headquarters.
Attending and contributing to trade fairs, seminars, etc..
Implementation of the marketing concept.
Responsibilities:
Responsible for achieving sales targets in the sales territory/market segment.
Responsible for adherence to the cost budget.
Competencies:
Creation and independent organization of one's own workstation.
Co-decision-making in the development of sales guidelines and measures in the sales territory/market segment.
Pricing authority within defined guidelines.

title: Senior Business Development Manager
jd_text:
Senior Business Development Manager
Remote - Germany
The world of payment processing is rapidly evolving, and businesses are looking for loyal and strategic partners, to help them grow.
Meet Nuvei, Nuvei is the global fintech building the infrastructure for every payment, everywhere. Its modular, flexible, and scalable technology enables leading companies to accept next-generation payments, offer all payout options, and benefit from card issuing, banking, risk, and fraud management services. Connecting businesses to their customers in more than 200 markets, with local acquiring in 52 markets, 150 currencies, and over 720 alternative payment methods, Nuvei provides the technology and insights for customers and partners to succeed locally and globally through one integration.
At Nuvei, we live our core values, and we thrive on solving complex problems. We're dedicated to continually improving our product and providing relentless customer service. We are always looking for exceptional talent to join us on the journey!
Your Mission
We are looking for a
Senior Business Development Manager
to join our fast-growing DACH Sales team.
Reporting to our VP Sales DACH,
you will be supporting the team on accelaerating the distribution of Nuvei's products with a key focus on the DACH market.
Responsibilities
Establish an extensive pipeline of sales opportunities, manage the development of the pipeline, and accurately forecast sales to senior management
Develop new sales strategies and identify high-value prospects - focus on selling Nuvei's products to Ecom/Digital goods/Retail merchants
Present large-scale technical products and innovative solutions to prospects at C-level
Negotiate contracts in a complex selling environment between various stakeholders. (business, legal, procurement, finance, etc.)
Directly identify and develop new sales partnership programs with merchants
Actively understand the prospects business and global payments footprint, their strategic growth plans, and competitive landscape
International mindset and ability to coordinate cross countries' projects and intercept new opportunities from partners and industry regulatory changes
Strong pipeline management via Salesforce to ensure accurate forecast of performance and deliver consistent results among Tier1 prospects
Requirements
Minimum 5 years' experience in Sales within global enterprises, additionally having expertise in the payments industry with a relevant network in DACH
Proven success in complex c-level negotiations, including all technical, compliance, legal and commercial aspects
Successful track record of achievement within the targeted field of expertise
Ability to build internal and external relationships to gain and share information such as industry trends for example
Creative problem solver and ability to manage stressful situations whilst juggling multiple challenges
Good organizational skills and willingness to travel
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Fluent in German and English. Other languages - an advantage
Experience with Salesforce
Highly motivated team player
Please submit your CV in English*
Benefits
30 Days annual leave
100 EUR monthly health allowance, Alternatively, if enrolled in private health insurance, the Employer will cover up to 50% of the cost capped at EUR 497.32 (gross) per month
50 EUR per month monthly allowance to set up your work from home set up such as wifi and office supplies
Nuvei is an equal-opportunity employer that celebrates collaboration and innovation and is committed to developing a diverse and inclusive workplace. The team at Nuvei is comprised of a wealth of talent, skill, and ambition. We believe that employees are happiest when they're empowered to be their true, authentic selves.
So, please come as you are. We can't wait to meet you
Our recruitment process may use automated tools, including AI, to support application management and candidate shortlisting.
The annual salary range for this role in the p

title: Business development Manager - Hotels
jd_text:
Major Global Luxury Hotel Group role as a Senior Business Development Manager.
B2B business development role targeting Travel Agents across leisure agencies and key Tour Operators to promote a portfolio of luxury hotel brands, as the preferred hotel chain to their own clients.
Hotel brand side or travel agency industry experience required.
euros 75k. Plus bonus.
Must be Germany based within 1 hour of an airport.
WFH / remote role, ~ 30% travel to meeting your agents F2F for in-person visits and territory trade show and hosted events.
The Senior Business Development Manager role works on their own portfolio of travel agencies in their region and is part of a wider European team, which is part of a global team.
From analysis of various agency and tour operator account metrics, the BDMs tailor their contact frequency and develop business relationships with the front-line travel agents, through calls (F2F and virtual), webinars, training sessions, workshops and by delivering and presenting relevant sales materials to promote awareness and encourage maximum sales of the hotel brand's resorts & all-inclusive properties.
The role records and up-dates all activities in Salesforce.
The Senior Business Development Manager works with the very personable Team Lead and Global Director in a small team of 5 who have twice weekly ‘huddles’ to share experiences, tactics and performance.
You will report back on KPIs, data, and ROI – with support from senior management.
The Global Director provides a lot of commercial direction, and then it is up to the BDMs to execute the strategies with their list of accounts.
They are looking for good analytical and commercial awareness to evaluate the Hotel Group’s market share and you'll need to be someone who enjoys presenting and engaging with clients.
You’ll need to prioritize agencies based on analysis of market share and room night data, and formulate the sales strategy for each agency. Using relevant data and reports you’ll plan and structure the content of the calls and presentations with agency activity based on relevant data and information.
Remote working close knit team, with some travel to meet your portfolio of agencies.
Please send your CV to me, Graham with a view to being briefed further on this major hotel group Senior Business Development Manager role.
Senior Business Relationship Manager

title: Strategic Operations Manager Germany - Airports Lead
jd_text:
Strategic Operations Manager, DACH Mobility - Airports Lead
Location:
Berlin, Germany (Hybrid)
About The Role
Airports are the "front door" to the Uber experience. For a traveler landing in a new city, that first ride is a critical moment of truth. In the DACH region (Germany, Austria, and Switzerland), our Airports and Travel Hubs represent one of our most complex and high-stakes strategic frontiers.
As the
Strategic Operations Manager for Airports
, you will be the architect of this experience. You aren't just managing a category; you are navigating the intersection of digital marketplaces and physical infrastructure. You will lead the strategy for some of the world's busiest hubs, managing high-level partnerships with airport authorities, negotiating commercial deals, and ensuring that when a rider steps off a plane, their journey with Uber is flawless.
This is a high-visibility role that requires a unique blend of "big picture" strategic thinking and "boots-on-the-ground" execution.
What You'll Do
Own the Partnership Strategy: Build and nurture senior-level relationships with airport authorities and travel hubs. You'll lead joint business planning, negotiate commercial agreements, and ensure Uber is the partner of choice for DACH's major gateways.
Drive Growth & Performance: You own the P&L for the Airports portfolio. You will monitor marketplace health, track KPIs, and stack-rank opportunities to maximize rider acquisition, revenue, and reliability.
Bridge the Physical & Digital: Uber doesn't just live in an app. You will optimize on-the-ground operations from signage and wayfinding to the design of pickup zones ensuring riders and drivers can find each other effortlessly in high-pressure environments.
Scale Through Analytics: Use data to solve complex problems. Whether it's modeling the impact of a new fee structure or optimizing throughput during peak holiday seasons, your decisions will be backed by rigorous analysis.
Lead Cross-Functional Impact: Act as the "General Manager" for your domain, collaborating with Product, Marketing, Legal, and Business Development to launch new features and regional campaigns.
Basic Qualifications
3+ Years of Experience: Background in technology, management consulting, or high-growth operational environments.
Operational Excellence: You are a process-driven project manager who can turn an ambiguous business goal into a structured Standard Operating Procedure (SOP).
Analytical Rigor: You are comfortable with data and can translate complex datasets into actionable recommendations.
Strategic & Tactical Versatility: You can "see the forest and the trees" designing a 3-year roadmap in the morning and conducting an on-site signage audit in the afternoon.
Collaboration & Communication: Ability to influence stakeholders across different cultures and functions.
Language: Business-level proficiency in German (B2+) and English is required.
Preferred Qualifications
Data Tools: Proficiency in SQL (or a proven aptitude to learn it within your first 30 days) is highly preferred, as data-driven storytelling is core to the role.
Industry Context: Previous experience in the travel, aviation, or logistics industries is a plus.

title: Key-Account-Manager:in
jd_text:
Unser Mandant ist ein familiengeführtes, innovatives und global agierendes europäisches Pharmaunternehmen mit über 60 Jahren wissenschaftlicher Exzellenz. Mit einer modernen Unternehmenskultur und einem klaren Fokus auf Forschung und Entwicklung treibt das Unternehmen den medizinischen Fortschritt konsequent voran.
Aktuell befindet sich das Unternehmen in einer entscheidenden Wachstumsphase: der Aufbau einer Onkologie-Pipeline mit einem ersten Produktlaunch in Deutschland im Jahr 2026. Diese Therapie besitzt hohes Potenzial und verspricht einen starken medizinischen Impact.
Wenn Sie neugierig sind, Gestaltungsfreude mitbringen und aktiv einen neuen Markt aufbauen möchten, finden Sie hier die ideale Umgebung, um Ihre Ideen einzubringen und gemeinsam mit uns Zukunft zu gestalten.
Account Manager Onkologie (m/w/d) – Region Duisburg, Düsseldorf, Essen
Ihre Aufgaben
Aufbau und Pflege nachhaltiger Beziehungen zu onkologischen Stakeholdern über alle verfügbaren Kanäle
Erfolgreicher Launch eines neuen onkologischen Produktes durch Umsetzung der Produktstrategie im Gebiet
Entwicklung individueller Account-Pläne und enge Zusammenarbeit in einem cross-funktionalen Team
Analyse des regionalen Marktes, Identifikation von Trends und Ableitung strategischer Maßnahmen
Effektive Nutzung digitaler Omni-Channel-Tools zur lokalen Vertriebsunterstützung
Netzwerkmanagement, einschließlich der Identifikation relevanter Ärztenetze und Patientenflüsse
Repräsentation unseres Mandanten bei regionalen Veranstaltungen, Kongressen und Fortbildungen
Aktiver Beitrag zu einer Unternehmenskultur, die Integrität und hohe ethische Standards lebt
Das bringen Sie mit
Hochschulabschluss in Biowissenschaften oder vergleichbare Qualifikation
Mehrjährige Erfahrung im pharmazeutischen Außendienst, idealerweise in der Onkologie
Nachweisbare Vertriebserfolge und Zugang zu onkologischen Entscheidern
Erfahrung in cross-funktionaler Zusammenarbeit und projektorientiertem Arbeiten
Sehr gutes Verständnis des deutschen Gesundheitssystems
Mut, Neues auszuprobieren, sowie Motivation, einen neuen Markt aktiv zu entwickeln
Analytisches und strategisches Denken sowie eigenständige, strukturierte Planung
Starke Kommunikations- und Präsentationsfähigkeiten, persönlich und digital
Gute IT-Kenntnisse und wünschenswerterweise Englischkenntnisse
Freude daran, ein onkologisches Portfolio nachhaltig auszubauen
Das bietet Ihnen unser Mandant
Arbeiten außerhalb von Big Pharma mit echten Gestaltungsmöglichkeiten
Die Chance, eine innovative Therapie in einem hochrelevanten onkologischen Indikationsgebiet aufzubauen
Ein dynamisches, wertschätzendes Team ohne Silodenken
Flache Hierarchien und kurze Entscheidungswege
Hohe Eigenverantwortung und Raum für eigene Ideen
Attraktives Gehaltspaket inklusive leistungsbezogenem Bonus
Dienstwagen zur privaten Nutzung
Umfassende Benefits, darunter Altersvorsorge, 30 Urlaubstage, zusätzliche freie Brückentage sowie freie Tage zwischen Weihnachten und Neujahr
Haben Sie Interesse?
Dann freuen wir uns auf Ihre Kontaktaufnahme und Ihre Bewerbung. Gestalten Sie eine wichtige onkologische Therapieentwicklung aktiv mit und werden Sie Teil eines Unternehmens, das einen echten medizinischen Unterschied machen möchte.

title: Business Development Manager (m/w/d) Trade 85.000€
jd_text:
Business Development Manager (m/w/d) Brokerage / Investments - unbefristet - Festanstellung - 85.000€
Standort:
Frankfurt am Main
Für Kandidat:innen außerhalb der Region:
Umzugsbereitschaft erforderlich
Erfahrungslevel:
Junior und Senior

Hybrid (3 Tage pro Woche vor Ort in Frankfurt, 2 Tage remote)
Wichtig:
Wenn Sie nicht in der Nähe von Frankfurt leben, setzen wir Umzugsbereitschaft voraus.
Start:
Schnellstmöglich
Über das Unternehmen
Unser Mandant ist ein moderner, stark wachsender Anbieter im digitalen Banking in Deutschland und baut aktuell ein neues Team im Bereich Investments / In App Brokerage auf. Ziel ist es, ein Brokerage-Angebot direkt in eine Banking-App zu integrieren und damit Kundinnen und Kunden den Handel mit Wertpapieren einfach, sicher und digital zugänglich zu machen.
Ihre Aufgaben
Je nach Erfahrungslevel übernehmen Sie Verantwortung in einem neuen, dynamischen Team und gestalten aktiv den Aufbau eines In App Brokers:
Entwicklung und Weiterentwicklung des Geschäftsmodells (z. B. Depotarten, Produkt-/Asset-Universum, Preismodelle)

title: Business Development Manager Service (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Job Description
Aufgaben - Das erwartet Sie bei uns:
Ableitung und Umsetzung strategischer Maßnahmen zum Wachstum des RRPS-Service-Geschäft für das Geschäftssegment Stationary mit den Vertriebseinheiten.
Identifizierung von Umsatzpotenzialen für neue und bestehende Service-Produkte anhand des Marktmodells.
Wirtschaftliche Bewertung und Erarbeitung von Business Cases zur Unterstützung von Entwicklungsprojekten in enger Abstimmung mit Controlling.
Kommerzielle Produktentwicklung in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Service Engineering.
Unterstützung und Durchführung von Sonderprojekten für das Service-Geschäft.
Gestaltung von Marketingkonzepten in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Communication und Marketing.
Erstellung von Entscheidungsvorlagen und Präsentationen für das Management und strategische Gremien.
Proaktiver und kontinuierlicher Austausch und enge Zusammenarbeit mit den Vertriebsorganisationen, Matrix-Funktionen sowie konzernweiten Stakeholdern.
Qualifikationen - Was Wir Uns Wünschen
Master- oder Bachelorabschluss im Bereich Betriebswirtschaftslehre, Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen, Maschinenbau oder vergleichbare Qualifikation.
Sehr gute analytische und konzeptionelle Fähigkeiten, um komplexe Sachverhalte aufarbeiten, verstehen und verarbeiten zu können.
Schnelle Auffassungsgabe und ausgeprägte Hands-on-Mentalität, um Projekte erfolgreich umzusetzen.
Ausgeprägte soziale Kompetenzen und gute Team- sowie kommunikative Fähigkeiten.
Verständnis und hohe Affinität für Service-Geschäft.
Berufserfahrung in einer Funktion mit Bezug zur Geschäftsentwicklung (zum Beispiel als Business Developer in einem Industrieunternehmen oder als Consultant in einer Unternehmensberatung).
Warum Rolls-Royce Power Systems – Das Bieten Wir Ihnen
Gesunde und faire Arbeitsbedingungen durch Tarifbindung (Metall- und Elektroindustrie)
Betriebseigene Krankenkasse BKK MTU für unsere Mitarbeitenden und Familienangehörigen
Umfangreiches betriebliches Gesundheitsmanagement inklusive Firmenfitnessprogramm (EGYM Wellpass)
Attraktive betriebliche Altersvorsorge – Mit uns für das Alter sparen!
Berufsunfähigkeitsversicherung – Absicherung für alle Fälle!
Mobilitätsunterstützung im Alltag – JobTicket und Jobrad
Corporate Benefits Programm – Attraktive Mitarbeiterrabatte auf zahlreiche starke Marken
Wir sind ein Arbeitgeber, der sich für Chancengleichheit einsetzt. Wir setzen uns für die Entwicklung einer vielfältigen Belegschaft und eines integrativen Arbeitsumfelds ein. Wir glauben, dass Menschen mit unterschiedlichen Hintergründen und Kulturen uns unterschiedliche Perspektiven geben. Und je mehr Perspektiven wir haben, desto erfolgreicher werden wir sein. Durch den Aufbau einer Kultur des Respekts und der Wertschätzung geben wir jedem, der hier arbeitet, die Möglichkeit, sein volles Potenzial auszuschöpfen.
Ihr Ansprechpartner
Ana De Los Rios
careers@ps.rolls-royce.com
Hinweis:
Bitte bewerben Sie sich ausschließlich online über unser Karriereportal. Bewerbungen auf anderen Kanälen (insb. auch E-Mail) können nicht berücksichtigt werden.
Type of Contract
Permanent

title: Senior Business Development Manager: new business
jd_text:
Granular Energy
Mission & Vision
Granular is a fast-growing climate tech startup paving the way towards a carbon-free grid.
We provide a portfolio management platform and trading services to utilities, suppliers and energy traders. The platform helps solve the complex issue of hourly matching between clean energy generation and consumption, at scale. You can find out more about the 24/7 energy space in this article.
Initially founded in 2021, our team is now around 30 people. We have clients in the US, Europe and Asia, and our team is primarily located in western Europe.
This is your opportunity to work on a product that's creating a pathway to a fully clean energy grid. Your efforts will have a direct and sizeable contribution to the climate transition.
Remote-first
Granular Energy is a remote-first organisation. We are flexible on locations that are within a train ride from Paris. There will be regular team calls in the morning, and three all-hands gatherings each year (most recently Brussels, London and Amsterdam). Travel to industry events and customer meetings, primarily in the UK and Europe, will also be part of the role.
What's the mission of this role?
This role exists to identify and help to validate the next wave of products at Granular Energy, beyond our core enterprise platform.
Our core platform (EAC management and hourly matching) has reached strong product-market fit, with customers across Europe, the US and Asia. We now want a dedicated person who can run the full zero-to-one cycle on adjacent opportunities: a battery-options and virtual-toll marketplace, ETRM extensions for trading desks, ESNA-related extensions, and other adjacencies that the energy transition will surface.
You will have significant autonomy on what to pursue, and a direct line to the rest of the commercial, product and engineering teams for prototyping and when an opportunity is ready to scale.
Who we are looking for!
Core responsibilities
Support sales of our core platform
Identify and evaluate new commercial opportunities adjacent to the core platform
Run primary market research through expert and customer interviews
Be involved in developing prototypes using AI tooling
Run small-scale market tests and
Close first paying customers on test products (from existing or new logos)
Develop business cases for promising opportunities and present them to the executive team
Hand validated opportunities cleanly to the wider commercial and product teams for scaling
Work with our tech team to build minimum-viable prototypes using AI tooling and demo them with prospects
Essential Experiences, Competencies And Knowledge
Significant experience in the power market (typically 5+ years), from one of: energy consulting, trading (utility, IPP or fund), or strategy / corporate development inside an energy company
Demonstrated track record of identifying and pursuing new commercial opportunities; entrepreneurial mindset, comfortable in ambiguous early-stage work
Strong network in the UK and / or European power market across suppliers, traders, generators, optimisers and regulators
Strategic thinker who can move quickly from market signal to thesis to validated test
Hands-on technical capability; comfortable using modern AI tooling (Claude, Cursor, Replit and similar) to build prototypes and demos independently
Strong commercial instincts and willingness to do early sales independently
Stellar communication and organisational skills
Bonus points for
Experience in startup and / or remote environments
Direct experience in flexibility, battery, EAC or hourly matching markets
Product or product management background
Existing network at any of European Tier 1 utilities, IPPs, energy traders, corporate energy consumers
What's in it for you
Transparent salaries based on seniority and location (see "compensation" section)
Remote first: WFH, or your local co-working space
