title: Product Manager
jd_text:
This is a role that
TechTree is recruiting for on behalf of one of our clients.
TechTree is an AI-driven recruitment platform working with high-growth companies.
When you apply, our AI Agent matches you
not just to this role
, but to other relevant opportunities across our network, so
one application can unlock multiple roles.
Product Manager — Own the core product line end-to-end at Europe’s #1 farm-level data platform. Berlin, on-site.
A well-funded agritech company is the #1 primary data collection platform for dairy in DACH, AI-enabled at the core. They raised >€10M in their first two years and signed some of the largest F&B players in Europe. Today the platform is the foundation for agricultural decarbonization across the continent — and the next chapter means new commodities and new geographies.
You will own the core product line end-to-end: from discovery to delivery to ongoing maintenance. You decide what gets built, when, and why. You lead the engineering and design squad. And you go deep on methodology — calculators, data sources, agricultural emission factors — becoming one of the most knowledgeable people in Europe on how farm-level emissions get calculated.
What You’ll Own
→ Own the product line end-to-end — discovery, delivery, maintenance; you decide what gets built
→ Lead an engineering and design squad — routines, rituals, prioritization are on you
→ Prioritize across competing demands — customer escalations, expansion bets, methodology updates
→ Go deep on emission methodology — calculators, data sources, agricultural emission factors
→ Drive expansion into new markets and commodities
→ Ship — time-to-customer-value beats perfection
What We’re Looking For
→ 2–3 years of product management experience at an early-stage B2B SaaS company that shipped fast
→ Strong prioritization — you hold competing demands in your head and communicate decisions clearly
→ Comfortable leading engineering and design
→ Some technical understanding — you can reason about systems, data models, and feasibility tradeoffs
→ Builder mentality — you’d rather have something live and imperfect than perfect and unshipped
→ AI-fluent — modern AI tools are part of your daily craft
→ Based in Berlin, on-site
Bonus:
genuine interest in farmers, agriculture, or the food system
What is TechTree?
TechTree combines AI agents with a human support team to give candidates a better, more transparent hiring experience.
Our AI Agent
gets to know your background, experience, and what you’re optimising for. It continuously matches you to live roles across our network where there’s
genuine alignment, not just keyword overlap.
Our Human Team
is there to support you through the process — answering questions, adding context, and making sure you’re never left guessing or ghosted.
We only post real jobs.
Every role on TechTree is a live, active hire.

title: Product Manager - Business Automation
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
We are on the lookout for a Product Manager to join our Enterprise Technology Services team on our journey to always deliver amazing experiences. This role would support senior leadership in driving high-visibility Agentic AI initiatives, bridging technical product management and strategic project execution. You will lead the delivery of AI solutions tailored for People & Culture, Legal, and other departments. The ideal candidate navigates ambiguity effectively and transitions seamlessly between strategic oversight and detailed technical or operational deep dives. Your objective is to identify manual bottlenecks, deploy automation at scale, and measure the resulting operational impact.
Define and own the strategic product roadmap for internal AI and automation tools, ensuring alignment with broader business objectives.
Assess and synthesise broad organisational challenges to identify root causes, dependencies, and automation opportunities.
Lead the end-to-end product lifecycle of AI projects, such as large scale AI driven agents, Slack-integrated agents, enterprise search, and n8n workflows.
Define, track, and iterate on core success metrics, including financial savings, hours saved, ticket deflection rates, and AI adoption rates.
Facilitate cross-functional collaboration among engineering, operations, and senior stakeholders to manage dependencies and mitigate risks.
Develop structured communications, status updates, and decision-support materials to maintain executive visibility.
Qualifications
Proven background in technical program management, product management, or strategic operations within a large enterprise.
Pragmatic AI Advocate including daily usage of AI tools at expert level (e.g., Claude, Gemini) : deep understanding and a practical ability to prototype, build and automate using agentic AI focuses on business impact
Experience managing integration of enterprise platforms such as Slack, Google Workspace, Workday, Atlassian, n8n and other enterprise grade stacks.
Strong stakeholder management skills, including the ability to influence without direct authority and ask tactful yet challenging questions.
Demonstrated ability to define and own product KPIs (e.g., SLA adherence, user satisfaction, financial impact) and translate ambiguous problems into clear, prioritised engineering backlogs.
Systems Thinker: Adept at synthesising large amounts of information to navigate complex, ambiguous technical dependencies and undocumented workflows.
Nice to have
Positive Team Player: You naturally "bring good vibes" to the group. You focus on what is possible and actively contribute to a supportive, healthy workplace.
High Adaptability: You absorb rapid organisational change and shifting requirements with an open mind, free from premature assumptions or strong judgements.
Inclusive Mindset: You champion diversity of thought and background, ensuring our internal AI solutions are built equitably for a global workforce and you work well within a culturally diverse team.
Humble Learner: You stay humble and objective when evaluating feedback, prioritising continuous improvement and team success over personal ego.
Additional Information
Ensuring you and all our Heroes are looked after, happy, and healthy is always on the menu. Because if you’re in good shape, then we’re in good shape.
Make the most

title: Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Company Description
Everyone’s story matters. Come shape your story with us at Riverty.
But where does that take you?
To one of our 30 hybrid workspaces – designed for exchanging ideas, learning from others, and shaping the way we work. An international community of over 4,000 people, representing almost 80 nationalities across 12 countries. United by one mission: Combining empathy, advanced technology and data-driven insights to keep people and businesses in flow. With payments made for them. So that they don’t have to worry about it.
And there’s more.
We are part of the family-owned Bertelsmann group. Established. Corporate. In a fast-paced industry. We enable flexible payments in various industries, simplifying the financial management of known brands and helping people repay debt to build financial confidence. In short: shaping FinTech.
Job Description
We are looking for a
Product Manager Conversational AI (m/f/d)
(unlimited, fulltime) Join our team at our locations in Berlin, Verl or Baden-Baden - flexible working conditions available
Millions of people each year end up in difficult financial situations. How they are treated in those moments matters, and technology can make it better.
As a Product Manager for Conversational AI within our BU Collection, you'll define how AI-powered experiences across chat, voice, and digital portals help people resolve debt in ways that are faster, clearer, and less stressful.
This is a builder role: you'll work hands-on with a cross-functional team spanning Platform Engineering, AI Engineering, Operations, Data, Compliance, and Corporate AI leadership, bringing structure, experimentation, and momentum to a domain that's still taking shape.
Your Responsibilities Will Be
Define and drive the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for Conversational AI across all consumer interaction channels
Analyze and prioritize key journeys (payment, installment, dispute, status inquiries) to maximize digital self-service and conversion
Bring clarity and ownership into a fragmented domain by establishing a structured product setup
Design and run experiments to validate use cases quickly, ensuring only the highest-impact bets get built without waiting for perfect conditions on interlinked systems
Ensure smooth end-to-end execution from discovery to launch and performance optimization
Define and track key performance metrics to drive continuous improvement across live channels
Coordinate cross-functionally with Platform Engineering, AI Engineering, Operations, Data, Compliance, Customer Service, and Corporate AI
Qualifications
What you bring:
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Product Management, Information Systems, AI, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience
3–5+ years of product management experience in AI-driven products, B2C digital platforms within fintech or other regulated industries
Strong stakeholder management skills, with experience navigating complex international organizations
Ability to bring structure and prioritization to ambiguous environments with fragmented ownership
Affinity for conversational AI technologies (LLMs, NLU, chatbots, voicebots) and backend/platform constraints
Business fluent English is required; German skills are a plus
Additional Information
Disclosure requirements pertaining to the collection of your personal data:
Responsible for processing the information provided in your application is the company specified in the job advertisement, with its registered office as indicated. The company processes your data for the purpose of establishing an employment relationship on the basis of Art. 6 (1) b GDPR / Section 26 (1) sentence 1 BDSG.

You can contact the company’s Data Protection Officer at the above-mentioned postal address.

title: AI Adoption Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Du bekommst Energie, wenn du dich zu einer Kolleg:in setzt, ihr bei der Arbeit zuschaust und genau die Aufgabe entdeckst, die im Kleinen jede Woche Zeit kostet. Bei Candis suchen wir eine Person als AI Adoption Product Manager (m/w/d), die aus diesem Instinkt Tools macht, die Menschen wirklich gerne nutzen, und die dabei hilft, dass wir KI als die Chance sehen, die sie sein kann.
Warum brauchen wir diese Rolle?
Die KI-Adoption bei Candis läuft langsamer, als sie könnte. Unsere Teams sind im operativen Alltag stark ausgelastet, und die meisten Menschen haben keine Zeit, einen Schritt zurückzutreten und zu fragen, was automatisiert werden könnte oder wo ein kleines KI-Tool helfen würde. Hinzu kommt: Wenn unser AI Solutions Team ein Tool entwickelt hat, ist eigentlich niemand dafür verantwortlich. Wir verfolgen nicht systematisch, wer es nutzt, welche Probleme auftreten oder ob es auf weitere Bereiche ausgeweitet werden sollte. Wir wissen nicht, was wir nicht wissen, und man kann nichts adoptieren, von dem man nichts weiß. Genau diese Lücke soll diese Rolle schließen, von der ersten Idee bis zur tatsächlichen Nutzung im Alltag.
Warum ist diese Rolle spannend?
Das ist ein echter Spielplatz für jemanden, der mitgestalten will, wie ein ganzes Unternehmen mit KI arbeitet. Du bekommst kein enges Briefing und kein festes Backlog. Du entscheidest mit, welche Probleme es wert sind, gelöst zu werden, entwickelst Ideen, baust MVPs gemeinsam mit dem AI Solutions Team und begleitest sie bis zur echten Nutzung. Dein Team besteht nicht nur aus dem AI Solutions Team, sondern im Alltag aus dem ganzen Unternehmen. Jede Abteilung ist offen für Entdeckungen, und jeder Erfolg wird schnell sichtbar. Es ist eine klassische Startup-Rolle in dem Sinn, dass du einen Titel bekommst, der eine Verschmelzung aus drei zentralen Disziplinen ist: Product Management, Change Management und AI Solutions Engineering.
Was sind die größten Herausforderungen?
Die größte Herausforderung ist die Bandbreite an Menschen, die du mitnehmen musst, und das Tempo, mit dem du zwischen ihnen wechselst. Ein Gespräch führst du vielleicht mit einer Designer:in, das nächste mit jemandem aus der Verwaltung, der noch nie über Automatisierung nachgedacht hat, und jedes Mal passt du deinen Ansatz an, ohne deine Neugier und Empathie zu verlieren. Gleichzeitig brauchst du die Zuversicht, eine Entscheidung zu treffen und andere davon zu überzeugen, sowie die Widerstandsfähigkeit, damit umzugehen, wenn die Adoption langsamer läuft als erwartet oder Tools nicht genutzt werden. Ein großer Teil dieser Rolle bewegt sich in Unsicherheit, denn wir wissen selbst nicht alles, was du herausfinden sollst. Du musst dich also auch wohlfühlen, wenn du ohne fertigen Plan weitermachst.
Was möchten wir in 6 Monaten erreicht haben?
In den ersten 30 Tagen lernt diese Person zunächst, was das AI Solutions Team bereits gebaut hat und wie das aktuelle Projektboard priorisiert ist. Im Rahmen des eigenen Onboardings führt sie Einzelgespräche mit Kolleg:innen: Nachdem sie die verschiedenen Abteilungen kennengelernt hat, stellt sie Fragen dazu, wie diese KI heute nutzen und wo es hakt. Gleichzeitig beginnt sie, Beziehungen im ganzen Unternehmen aufzubauen. Auch wenn das nebensächlich klingt, ist es einer der wichtigsten Teile der Rolle
Bis Tag 60 möchten wir ein ausführliches Discovery-Gespräch mit mindestens einer Person aus jeder Abteilung sehen, eine Einarbeitung durch das AI Solutions Team in ein bestehendes Tool mit einer kurzen Adoptionsprüfung, sowie ein oder zwei vorgestellte Ideen für die nächsten Schritte, basierend auf dem bisher Gelernten.
Bis Tag 90 sollen ein oder zwei Tools live sein und sichtbar positive Wirkung zeigen.
Innerhalb des ersten Jahres möchten wir eine spürbare Reduktion manueller, wiederkehrender Arbeit im gesamten Unternehmen erreichen. Einen echten Wandel im Mindset, sodass KI-Adoption als Chance und nicht als Bedrohung wahrgenommen wird. Der Erfolg wird gemessen an ungefähr fünf Proj

title: Senior Product Manager – Retail Media Platform (m//f/d)
jd_text:
About Redcare Pharmacy:
As Europe’s No.1 e-pharmacy Redcare Pharmacy is powered by passionate teams and cutting-edge innovation. We strive to create a healthy collaborative work environment where every employee feels valued and inspired to contribute to our vision “Until every human has their health”. If you’re seeking a career that offers purpose and aligns with your values join us and start your #Redcareer today.
About the role:
This is a unique opportunity to shape one of Europe’s fastest-growing retail media platforms at the intersection of health, pharmacy, and e-commerce. You will take ownership of core data and sponsored product capabilities in a high-impact environment, where your work directly influences commercial outcomes and platform scalability.
Working closely with Product, Data, Engineering, and Commercial teams, you will help define how a modern retail media platform leverages first-party data, trusted customer relationships, and advanced experimentation to drive measurable advertiser value.
Job Description
About your tasks:
Own and evolve our Sponsored Products ecosystem, including auction mechanics, bidding logic, and relevance models to continuously improve performance and ad quality
Drive the development of retail media data products, such as first-party audience segments, attribution models, and measurement frameworks in collaboration with Data & AI teams
Shape the advertiser self-service platform, enabling seamless campaign creation, optimisation, and reporting for both marketplace sellers and programmatic buyers
Build and scale experimentation frameworks, including A/B testing, incrementality testing, and platform intelligence, to unlock continuous learning and revenue growth
Partner cross-functionally with Retail Media, Marketing, and Category teams to align product development with business needs and maximise commercial impact
Qualifications
About you:
You bring 7+ years of product management experience with strong exposure to Retail Media, AdTech, or e-commerce monetisation
You have deep understanding of auction dynamics, advertiser ROI, and data-driven product design, and have owned data products end-to-end
You are technically fluent, comfortable discussing data pipelines, model design, and experimentation frameworks with engineers and data scientists
You work in a structured, independent, and highly data-driven way, navigating complex stakeholder environments and identifying dependencies across teams
Additional Information
About your benefits:
In order to provide you with the best possible support for your individual needs - whether it‘s living a healthy life, having enough time for your family, or developing your skills - we offer a wide range of different, site-specific benefits:
Welcome days: We want you to feel at home with us from the very first day. Therefore, we welcome you with our comprehensive onboarding program.
Work from Home: If your job does not require you to be present in the office, we can arrange the place you work from individually - even for up to 20 days a year anywhere in the EU.
Mobility: We provide our employees with a fully costed Deutschland Ticket which can be used at any time. Click here to learn more.
Personal development: Grow! We support and encourage your individual development through various in- and external trainings.
Vacation: 30 days paid vacation per annum (plus another day on Rosenmontag) and the option to take unpaid vacation of up to 10 days (after one year working with us).

Hamburg, DE

About Us
PLIXXENT is represented at seven different locations in Europe (Germany, Denmark, the Netherlands, Belgium, Italy, Spain and Great Britain), has around 320 employees and an annual turnover of over 300 M€.
PLIXXENT offers innovative technologies based on polyurethane. As a specialist for standard solutions and tailor-made systems, we accompany our customers along the entire value chain - from the initial consultation to after-sales support. Well-founded know-how in the development, production and processing of polyurethanes, flexibility in the procurement of raw materials and a European team with decades of experience make us a reliable and powerful partner - and an attractive employer with a wide range of career opportunities.
PLIXXENT is looking for a new team member to join our
Product Management
team, with a primary focus on
Flexible Moulded Foam
.
In this role, you will take responsibility for managing and developing products within this area, working closely with cross-functional teams to support our business objectives and customer needs.
If you are seeking a new challenge and would like to take the next step in your career, we look forward to receiving your application.
Location:
The position can be based at any of our PLIXXENT locations.
YOUR NEW CHALLENGE
Managing the portfolio
Strategic development
Prioritization of development & projects
Discontinuation of products
New product approvals
Cost Management
Raw Material Strategy (exchangeability, new RM, Focus clusters etc.)
Formulation Benchmarking
Market Analysis on Competitive Offering
Where to produce?
Pricing
Price guidance to Sales
Target & Bottom Price definition
Market Price tracking
YOUR COMPETENCIES AND QUALITIES
Bachelor or Master degree in chemistry, chemical engineering or similar
Passion for Polyurethane is a plus
Proactive behaviour
Good analytical skills
Excellent communication
Fast pace in thinking and acting
Ability to take different perspectives
Logical thinking
Empathy
Good written and spoken English skills
Additional languages are a plus
What You Can Expect
At PLIXXENT, you’ll step into a modern workplace with international flair, where innovation meets collaboration. Here, you’re not just part of a team - you’re empowered to shape ideas, take responsibility, and make a real impact. Together, we tackle exciting projects and challenges in an open, supportive culture that values creativity and teamwork.
Why Join Us? We believe work should inspire you. That’s why we offer attractive compensation package, flexible working hours, mobile working options for greater freedom and other benefits.
At PLIXXENT, you’ll find more than just a job - you’ll discover perspective, benefits, and an environment where you can truly make a difference.
More Than a Job: Your Future at PLIXXENT
PLIXXENT respects the principle of equality when hiring people. We do not discriminate based upon religion, race, national origin, gender, color, sexual orientation, disability

title: Product Manager, Search Ranking
jd_text:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.
Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.
Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.
About The Team And The Role
eBay’s Buyer and Seller science team is central to eBay’s business. Joining means being part of a dynamic and innovative group focused on delivering great experiences to millions globally. As a member of the search ranking team, you will help shape the strategic direction of our search products. You will focus on improving search result rankings and developing new search technologies. You will collaborate with experts who apply advanced data science methods to build relevant search experiences. We seek people who excel in fast-paced, cooperative settings and who are dedicated to providing excellent customer experiences. Our culture values creativity, bravery, and teamwork, offering many opportunities for personal and professional growth. Join us and contribute to shaping ecommerce’s future!
We are looking for a highly motivated and experienced Product Manager to join the search ranking team and enhance how search results are ranked. As the ranking Product Manager, you will lead initiatives that improve the overall search experience for customers and support revenue growth for the company. The candidate must be comfortable with ambiguity and experienced in defining requirements, coordinating across teams, and communicating with executives. You will collaborate with product managers, engineers, applied researchers, and analytics teams to develop solutions. The candidate should be skilled at making decisions based on data and analyzing large datasets.
What You Will Accomplish
Accomplishing ranking strategy and owning roadmap for search ranking.
Data-driven product prioritization with a balance of near-term deliverables and longer-term investment in technology platforms
Optimally communicate strategies & trade-offs up to senior leaders and down to development teams
Putting the customer first, understand market-specific nuances and identify science algorithm improvements to address unique market-specific needs
Driving collaboration across multiple teams to ensure teams are aligned and are working towards solving customer problems
Manage A/B testing to find launch candidates that boost metrics
What You Will Bring
5+ years of solid experience in product management. Ideally have prior experience developing customer-focused platform products that provide value to both sides of a marketplace (buyers and sellers)
A demonstrated history of creating fresh, inventive, and captivating customer experiences guided by customer data from multiple sources (market research, A/B testing, user research)
A customer and Search Engine obsession with a passion for everything related to technology, and technology-based innovation to build great experiences for the customers
Strong quantitative skills with a data-first approach; ability to use data and metrics to back up assumptions, recommendations, and drive actions
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Technically savvy (prefer a technical role in past, or Computer Science Degree)
Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience required; Master’s Degree or equivalent experience preferred
Additional Details
eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual or

title: Product Manager - Payments
jd_text:
About IXOPAY
IXOPAY is the enterprise-grade global payment infrastructure platform built for the era of agentic commerce. We equip merchants and enterprises with AI-driven intelligence, payment orchestration, advanced tokenization, and the tools to optimize every stage of the payments journey.
From intelligent routing and compliance to customizable modules and enterprise-scale orchestration, IXOPAY helps businesses integrate faster, improve payment performance, and expand globally with confidence.
At IXOPAY, our people are our greatest strength. Our collaborative culture is built on shared values that guide how we work, innovate, and support our customers.
About The Role
We're looking for a Product Manager to help shape the future of IXOPAY's Payments Intelligence platform.
In this role, you'll work closely with Engineering, Design, Sales, Customer Success, and enterprise customers to build data-driven products that help merchants optimize payment performance, improve conversion rates, manage risk, and make smarter business decisions. You'll balance customer needs, market trends, and business priorities to deliver innovative solutions that have measurable impact.
What You'll Do
Own and prioritize the product backlog for Payment front-end related products and AI Payments Intelligence.
Define product requirements, user stories, and roadmaps based on customer feedback, market trends, and business value.
Partner with Engineering to deliver features including observability, AI-powered insights, anomaly detection, dashboards, and workflow automation.
Collaborate with Design to create intuitive user experiences for analytics, reporting, configuration, and payment management.
Analyze payment and transaction data trends to identify opportunities, validate product decisions, and define meaningful KPIs.
Work closely with customers and internal stakeholders to understand challenges, gather feedback, and continuously improve the platform.
Support go-to-market activities by creating product documentation, training materials, and enablement resources.
Stay current with emerging trends in payments, AI, analytics, and regulatory requirements to inform produc
What You'll Bring
Required
5+ years of Product Management or Payments Management experience with focus on data analytics or payment setups
Strong understanding of payment ecosystems, including payment orchestration, acquiring, payment gateways, PSPs, or financial operations.
Experience defining product roadmaps, managing backlogs, and delivering products using Agile methodologies.
Strong analytical skills with experience using data to guide product decisions and measure outcomes.
Experience working with cross-functional teams including Engineering, Design, Sales, and Customer Success.
Excellent communication, stakeholder management, and prioritization skills.
Preferred
Experience working for a merchant, payment service provider, acquirer, or payments technology company.
Experience with business intelligence or analytics platforms such as Grafana, Tableau, or Metabase.
Experience building AI-powered or data-intensive products.
Familiarity with UX design processes and creating user experiences for analytics or enterprise applications.
Location
This is a remote-friendly position based in Germany or European Union . We welcome applications from candidates across Germany , with a preference for those located in or near Munich. You'll have the flexibility to work remotely or from our Munich office.
Why Join IXOPAY?
At IXOPAY, you'll help define the future of enterprise payments by building products that combine orchestration, AI, and data intelligence into a single platform. You'll work on meaningful industry challenges, collaborate with experienced teams, and have a direct impact on product strategy and customer success.
We Offer
Competitive salary and benefits
Professional growth and development opportunities
A collaborative, international team environment
Flexible remote working with the opti

title: Product Manager (m/w/d) | Remote
jd_text:
Wir bei
Insurgo
sind nicht nur ein Technologie-Unternehmen. Wir sind Innovatoren, die den Weg für digitale Lösungen in der Versicherungswirtschaft ebnen. Mit einem engagierten Team von 10 Experten arbeiten wir daran, die Geschäftsprozesse unserer Kunden zu revolutionieren.
Aufgaben
Als Product Manager gestaltest du unsere Software aktiv mit und bringst Struktur, Klarheit und Nutzerorientierung in unsere Produktentwicklung. Du arbeitest eng mit Entwicklung, Support und Geschäftsführung zusammen.
Produkt-Roadmap:
Du pflegst und priorisierst die Produkt-Roadmap und richtest diese an unseren Zielen aus
Anforderungsmanagement:
Du nimmst Kundenfeedback und interne Anforderungen auf, bewertest sie und überführst sie in klare Tickets und Spezifikationen.
Visualisierung & Konzeption:
Du hast die Fähigkeit, Produktideen nicht nur zu beschreiben, sondern visuell aufzubereiten. Du erstellst Mockups und User Flows, um Konzepte greifbar zu machen.
Schnittstellenfunktion:
Du bist die Verbindung zwischen Technik, Support und Business und sorgst dafür, dass alle Beteiligten auf einem Stand sind.
User Research:
Du führst qualitative User-Interviews durch und analysierst diese, um sicherzustellen, dass wir nah am Kundenbedarf entwickeln.
Qualifikation
Das bringst du mit:
Erfahrung im Produktmanagement:
Du hast bereits Erfahrung als Product Manager oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle gesammelt.
Technisches Verständnis:
Du besitzt die Fähigkeit, dich schnell in komplexe technische Sachverhalte einzuarbeiten und auf Augenhöhe mit Entwicklern zu sprechen.
Design-Affinität:
Du hast ein gutes Auge für Design und bist sicher im Umgang mit gängigen Prototyping- und Design-Tools.
Strukturierte Arbeitsweise:
Du bist stark in der Organisation von Wissen und Anforderungen (Erfahrung mit modernen Dokumentations-Tools ist vorausgesetzt).
Mindset:
Du arbeitest verantwortungsvoll, ergebnisorientiert und hast eine ausgeprägte Hands-on Mentalität.
Pluspunkt:
Erfahrung in der Versicherungsbranche ist von Vorteil.
Benefits
Ein ambitioniertes Team aus A-Playern
Moderner Arbeitsplatz mit Top-Ausstattung
Kompetitives Gehalt und Erfolgsvereinbarung
Offene Kommunikation und ergebnisorientiertes Arbeiten
100€ Arbeitgeberfinanzierte Altersversorgung
50€ Gutscheinkarte jeden Monat - steuerfrei zur freien Verfügung
Teamevents, die in Erinnerung bleiben
Sicherer und unbefristeter Arbeitsvertrag
Klingt interessant? Dann freuen wir uns auf Deine Bewerbung! Bitte vergiss nicht Deine Gehaltsvorstellung und Deinen frühstmöglichen Starttermin mit anzugeben.

title: Product Charging Solutions Manager
jd_text:
Chery Automobile is a leading Chinese auto company and an international brand whose business covers more than 80 countries and regions.
For our Engineering Center in Raunheim, near Frankfurt, we are looking for a
Product Charging Solutions Manager
We welcome you to join a growing team of extraordinarily talented and enthusiastic people, developing tailor-made cars for the European market.
We want to work with a flexible and enthusiastic individual wo will be required to support the development of our European vehicles locally as well as globally - jointly with our teams in our headquarter in China.
Responsibilities:
1. Responsible for planning the full product line of residential AC charging stations, public DC ultra-fast chargers, and commercial and industrial charging equipment in Europe, covering the localized needs of Germany, the UK, and other EU countries.
2. Research competitors, distribution channels, user habits, installation standards, and property access requirements in the European charging market to produce quarterly market insights.
3. Define interaction requirements for charging hardware, backend cloud systems, and mobile apps, balancing cost, energy efficiency, outdoor protection, and aesthetic integration with home interiors.
4. Oversee the entire new product lifecycle—from requirements definition, R&D, and certification through mass production to overseas market launch—managing NPI milestones and localization adaptation.
5. Collaborate with European charging station operators and residential customers to identify pain points in equipment operation, installation, and O&M, and drive improvements to the hardware and software user experience. 6. Develop Go-to-Market (GTM) strategies to support overseas marketing, trade shows, and channel product training
Qualifications:
1. Bachelor’s degree or higher from an accredited institution in Power Electronics, Energy and Power Engineering, Industrial Design, or related fields
2. At least 5 years of experience as a product manager for AC/DC charging stations, with a proven track record of successfully launching products in the EU market
3. Familiarity with European charging station safety standards, grid connection regulations, IP outdoor protection ratings, and residential installation regulations
4. Understanding of home energy storage + charging station integration systems; mastery of the basic logic of BMS and PCS in energy replenishment equipment.
5. Fluent in English with the ability to independently handle external business communications; proficiency in German is a plus; proficient in using Axure, XMind, and the Office suite.
6. Capable of cost estimation, competitive benchmarking, and product iteration planning; experience with on-site assignments or business travel overseas is preferred.
Join our dynamic team and be a part of our company's growth and success!

title: Technical Product Manager (all genders)
jd_text:
Welcome to Glow25!
We are a dynamic, fast-growing beauty and pro-aging company based in Berlin.
Our heartfelt mission:
Glow moments that last — for our community and for us as a team.
Work at Glow25 should inspire, excite, and be fun. That’s why we’re looking for smart, empathetic people who take ownership and grow with us.
Join Glow25 and help us redefine beauty. We’re excited to meet you!
These are our current challenges and future goals:
We run Shopify across DACH and NL (FR next), plus the ERP, stock, subscriptions, and B2B surfaces around it. The WebEx Product Team owns how all of that comes together on the site.
We're looking for someone who knows Shopify cold from a technical angle without being a developer. You understand the architecture, the limits, and when something needs native config, an app, or a custom build. Your job is to be the technical point person for the implementation and operational questions this team handles every day, and to keep us honest about what's actually worth building.
This is an individual contributor role reporting to the Head of WebEx. Roughly 60% operational ownership, 40% roadmap and strategy.
This is where we need your support:
Be the technical point person.
Own the implementation questions stakeholders bring to WebEx. Scope them, decide the right Shopify approach (native vs. app vs. custom), and see them through.
Own operational understanding of the shop experience end to end.
Understand how our interconnected systems fit together (ERP, CS, Data, and the rest) and how a change in one ripples through the others.
Support adjacent teams.
Learn our subscriptions platform well enough to back the CRM team on operational questions. Partner with Data and Infrastructure on tracking across the Shopify instances.
Keep the customer experience front of mind.
Bring a product lens to how the site actually feels to shoppers, and let UX and customer impact shape what we scope and prioritize.
Stay on top of performance, functionality, and flows.
Be aware of site performance (Lighthouse, render times) as we add to the site, and spot functionalities and workflows, internal or customer-facing, that can be smoothed with Shopify, third-party apps, or automation tooling like n8n.
Protect the roadmap.
Help the team separate genuine impact from stakeholder gut feeling, and spend limited capacity on what moves the business.
You have the following experience:
Deep, hands-on Shopify knowledge from a technical angle: architecture, platform limits, how apps and custom apps work, and how the pieces fit in a more complex setup. Not a developer, but able to hold a serious technical conversation with one.
A working command of web performance (Lighthouse, render time): what the metrics mean and how to improve them.
A practical, everyday use of AI. Not expert level, but you know what it can do for you and you use it. Familiarity with workflow automation (n8n or similar) is a plus.
A security mindset: GDPR, DPAs, and sound data handling factored into how you scope work.
The judgment and spine to take ownership of ambiguous asks and to push back on low-value priorities while staying collaborative.
You do speak fluent English. German is a plus.
That's something you can look forward to:
Work flexibly, in the way that suits you best.
Work when and where you’re most productive – with flexible hours and smart time tracking.
Home office? Done right.
We’ve got you covered: receive a one-time €500 allowance to set up your perfect workspace.
Fully equipped from day one.
Mac or Windows – you choose what works best for you.
Glow Card – your monthly treat.
Get €50 every month to spend however you like – that’s €600 a year, just because.
Learning is part of the deal.
Whether it’s our Glowcademy, dedicated learning time, or personal development support – we invest in you, not just in numbers.
Let’s Glow!
Enjoy 40% off our entire product range – yes, everything.
Take a break – you’ve earned it.
30 vacation days + 1 personal holiday + up

title: Senior Product Manager — AI-Native, Product & Product Ops  (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Your tasks
Shape the product. Scale the way we build.
This is a role for builders. You'll combine strategic product ownership with hands-on execution, establish scalable product operations, and help our team unlock the full potential of AI. Join us if you're passionate about creating products, improving systems, and leaving things better than you found them.
Own a product area end-to-end
Own the roadmap and strategy for one of our most important product areas, aligned with company goals and clearly communicated to leadership
Prototype with AI tools to test ideas in hours instead of weeks
Run rigorous discovery, then own delivery from start to finish:clear specifications, well-defined tickets, QA, and an ambitious-but-realistic rollout
Make decisions with data: set KPIs, form and test hypotheses, run your own basic analyses and prioritize using a value-vs-effort approach
Champion an accessibility-first mindset so inclusivity is designed in from the start, not bolted on
Support by setting up our product process & structure (Product Ops)
Partner with the VP Product to design and roll out our product operating model — how we plan,prioritize, discover, build and review
Establish and improve the team's rituals and cadences (roadmap planning,prioritization, reviews, retros) so they're lightweight and actually used
Stand up product metrics and reporting: a shared, reliable view of what we're shipping and the impact it has
Assess and improve product tooling, and smooth the hand-offs between product, engineering,design and go-to-market
Own product documentation and knowledge management — reference documents, spec standards, and a single source of truth the whole team can rely on
Raise the bar
Support by setting upstandard for discovery, specs and delivery, and mentor other PMs on their craft
Hands-on experience building AI powered features, plus a solid working understanding of modern AI — what these models are good at, where they fail, and how to evaluate, prompt, constrain and monitor them
Your profile
You're a senior or lead-level PM witha track record of owning a product area and driving measurable impact, ideally in B2B SaaS
You are AI-native in how you work: you use LLMs, agents and AI tooling daily and have strong intuition for what they can and can't do today
You prototype with AI tools to test ideas
You've shipped at least one AI-powered feature or product and understand the unique product challenges
A genuine "systems" instinct —you've introduced or meaningfully improved how a product team plans,prioritises,measures or documents its work, and you make process serve the work rather than the other way around.
Strong at both ends of the job: real discovery and disciplined delivery.
Data-literate — you set KPIs, reason about metrics, and pull basic analyses yourself
Excellent communication and stakeholder management;you're comfortable operating as a trusted partner to product leadership and keeping engineering, design and leadership aligned
You thrive in a fast-moving, scale-up environment where structure is something you help create
Fluent English. German nice-to-have
Nice to have
Familiarity with digital accessibility (WCAG) and the relevant legislation (BFSG, European Accessibility Act)
Exposure to Product Operations as a discipline, or experience being the person who set up product processes at a growing company
Experience with developer-facing, governance,complianceor audit products
Why us?
Prospects for entrepreneurial success – You are part of our expansion and can grow with us.
A meaningful mission – Your contribution improves the digital participation of millions of people every day.
30 days of vacation, flexible working hours, and a supportive, ambitious team.
For a relaxed start to the holiday season: One day of special leave in total for Christmas Eve and New Year's Eve, with the remaining time covered by the company.
Creative freedom – We value your ideas and sales expertise.
Benefits - Our benefits contribute to your

title: Lead Product Manager, B2B Trade Platform
jd_text:
About Andercore
Andercore is the AI-native supplier of industrial materials for energy, infrastructure, and construction in wholesale and beyond.
We trade with global suppliers and distribute to European customers on our own account. Our AI runs the full trade and distribution end-to-end: sourcing, quality, pricing, sales, logistics, and embedded financing. For the customer, it feels like buying from their preferred local supplier; for our partners, it is the most convenient and safe way to do business across borders. Behind it, our software and agentic AI do the heavy lifting that used to take an asset-intensive supply chain with four or five intermediaries and weeks of manual coordination.
Where We Are Today
Strong triple-digit-million euro turnover
Seven European markets live
80+ people across Berlin (HQ), offices in London, Mumbai, and Shanghai
$40M Series B just closed, $75M raised to date from Atomico, Project A, and Inven Capital, institutional financing from international banks.
We are building the world's first and last industrial-grade AI operating system for materials, redefining how global trade works in one of the largest and most essential industries on earth.
About This Role
We're hiring a Director Product Management to own a core product area end to end. This is a hands-on leadership role: you'll set direction and ship it yourself, reporting directly to the founder.
What You Will Own
A P&L-relevant product area (logistics automation, pricing, or embedded finance) from discovery to shipped outcomes
Concrete targets, not vibes: we run on automation and retention metrics, and your area will have a number attached
Discovery with real users: traders, logistics operators, and buyers, weekly and in person
Prioritization and delivery with your engineering squad, including the calls on what not to build
Post-launch iteration driven by usage data and margin impact, not opinion
What You Bring
5+ years in product management with at least 2 at a B2B scale-up, owning a product area through significant growth
Shipped products in an operations-heavy domain: logistics, marketplaces, supply chain, fintech, or similar
Fluency with data: you pull your own numbers, define your own metrics, and change course when they tell you to
A track record of fast MVP cycles: built, measured, iterated or killed, and you can walk us through the decisions
Experience shipping AI-powered features to production is a strong plus
Fluent English; German is a plus, not a requirement
What this role is not
Not a people-management role on day one. You'll lead through the product, with a path to building a team as the org scales.
Not a strategy-deck job. If your best work lives in slides rather than shipped product, this isn't the fit.
Who You Are
You are slef-motivated, iterating fast, and eager for product success
You are a self-starter who takes ownership of a problem and makes sure it’s solved
You are comfortable with giving and receiving feedback for continuous improvement
A lifelong learner, someone who can dive into a domain quickly and become an expert
Data-informed, business-oriented and relentless in execution
We are an equal-opportunity employer and welcome applicants from all backgrounds, regardless of race, ethnicity, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, or any other characteristic. We believe that diversity drives innovation, creativity, and collective strength.

The company is an AI-native supplier of industrial materials, with a strong presence in European markets and a growing team of 80+ people.

title: Product Manager (f/m/d) - AI
jd_text:
Remote in our day-to-day work. On-site when it matters.
We work remote by default – focused, efficient, and with full ownership. For larger features, architectural decisions, and real brainstorming sessions, we come together in Berlin – fast, hands-on, and without unnecessary meeting overhead.
We use AI to accelerate – not to replace thinking.
We design the system, steer the output, and take responsibility for what we ship.
Fast where it makes sense. Careful where it matters.
Your mission
Own the product vision end-to-end.
Shape AI agent products that don’t exist yet – from discovery through to delivery.
Translate customer needs
into a clear, scalable, and profitable product direction. Build your own vision on top of it.
Bridge business and technology.
Turn customer and stakeholder input into product strategy and technical requirements that engineering actually wants to build.
Talk to customers directly.
Understand their real problems, not just their stated ones. Distill ambiguity into actionable direction.
Work closely
with data scientists, engineers, marketing, and leadership. Keep up with a team that operates at a high level.
Solve problems. Close gaps.
Don’t wait for permission or a Jira ticket – take ownership and move things forward.
Your skills
Be technically fluent in AI/ML.
Understand LLMs, GenAI, and Agentic AI well enough to form strong opinions about product and system design.
Understand the stack.
Know how hardware (GPUs, compute), models, and applications connect – and where decisions have impact.
Put the customer first.
Run discovery, extract real needs, and turn complexity into clarity and direction.
Operate with high agency.
Take initiative, make decisions, and drive work forward with minimal guidance.
Build, don’t just plan.
Get hands-on – prototype, write specs, test assumptions, and validate with users.
Bring experience from AdTech, media, or advertising.
Understand programmatic ecosystems, real-time systems, and business models.
Have 3–6+ years in product management
, ideally with exposure to AI/ML products or complex technical platforms.
Why you’ll love working with us
Ownership over tickets:
You’re trusted with real responsibility, not just tasks. No unnecessary bureaucracy, no micromanagement – we rely on you to take things forward.
Build > Talk:
We test what works – not what sounds good. Fail fast, learn faster.
High standards, low ego:
We take our work seriously, but not ourselves. Direct feedback, honest collaboration, no drama.
Stay sharp:
Hackathons, conferences, community – we invest in your growth and keep you at the cutting edge.
And yes – the fundamentals are covered too:
30 days of vacation + Dec 24 & 31 off, Smart Fridays (4 days week possible), mobility (Germany ticket & JobRad), sports & health offerings, mental health support, corporate benefits, RTL+ access, and more.
Your CV is just the starting point.
Skip the cover letter. Tell us about a product problem you solved that you’re proud of, and why you think AI agents are about to change the advertising and media industries.
Welcome to smartclip! We’re just your average group of techies, you know, trying to keep the internet open and defend the European TV industry from Silicon Valley’s giants. Join us!
About smartclip
smartclip is the adtech development unit of RTL Group — Europe’s leading free-to-air broadcaster group. Our proprietary advertising technology is custom-built for the needs of European broadcasters and publishers — enabling media owners to implement smarter monetisation strategies. We are committed to delivering the most innovative ad experiences spanning in-stream, out-stream, addressable TV, connected TV, audio, and gaming — ultimately empowering brands with true cross-screen storytelling opportunities on all devices.
For more information: Please visit www.smartclip.tv and follow us on LinkedIn.
Location:
Hamburg, Berlin, Gütersloh or Remote (ideally max. 3 hours travel time to Berlin)

The company offers a dynamic work environment, with a mix of remote and on-site work, and a range of benefits, including 30 days of vacation and opportunities for professional growth.

title: Staff Product Manager
jd_text:
P-1345
You will be the Product Manager for the Databricks Runtime team in
Berlin
. The team works at the intersection of query processing, storage, infrastructure and data governance, all key components of the Databricks data intelligence platform. As the Product Manager, you will develop a deep understanding of modern cloud infrastructure, data governance solutions, and open source components, and gather requirements from customers. You will launch features that will impact how customers process petabytes of data every day and make it simpler for them to draw value from their data.
This role requires coordinating all product activities from vision to implementation, including engaging customers to understand their needs, developing long-term product strategy, defining product roadmaps, working with engineering to build those products, and coordinating with various internal stakeholders (both pre- and post-launch) to ensure product success.
The Impact You Will Have
Own the end-to-end product management process
Gather customer requirements, design features and collaborate with engineering to deliver a polished market-ready product
Drive launches with product marketing through blog posts, release notes, webinars
Define and measure OKRs for your products
Drive the prioritization and development of features, measurably making it simpler and more cost effective for customers to govern and process petabytes of data.
What We Look For
6+ yrs of experience
Bachelors degree in Computer Science or similar field
Experience in Product Management (inbound, outbound, cross-functional processes)
Technical skills
Solid understanding of cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP)
Strong data analysis and operationalization skills (SQL, rollups, building operational dashboards)
About Databricks
Databricks is the data and AI company. More than 10,000 organizations worldwide — including Comcast, Condé Nast, Grammarly, and over 50% of the Fortune 500 — rely on the Databricks Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics and AI. Databricks is headquartered in San Francisco, with offices around the globe and was founded by the original creators of Lakehouse, Apache Spark™, Delta Lake and MLflow. To learn more, follow Databricks on Twitter, LinkedIn and Facebook.
Benefits
At Databricks, we strive to provide comprehensive benefits and perks that meet the needs of all of our employees. For specific details on the benefits offered in your region click here.
Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
At Databricks, we are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive culture where everyone can excel. We take great care to ensure that our hiring practices are inclusive and meet equal employment opportunity standards. Individuals looking for employment at Databricks are considered without regard to age, color, disability, ethnicity, family or marital status, gender identity or expression, language, national origin, physical and mental ability, political affiliation, race, religion, sexual orientation, socio-economic status, veteran status, and other protected characteristics.
Compliance
If access to export-controlled technology or source code is required for performance of job duties, it is within Employer's discretion whether to apply for a U.S. government license for such positions, and Employer may decline to proceed with an applicant on this basis alone.

The company, Databricks, is a data and AI company that provides a Data Intelligence Platform to unify and democratize data, analytics, and AI.

title: Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Werde Teil unseres Teams
Festanstellung, unbefristet, Vollzeit (40 Std./Woche), ab sofort, München | Nürnberg
Du möchtest Produktmanagement ganzheitlich denken und dabei über den Tellerrand einzelner Produktbereiche hinausblicken? Bei Memodo betreust du internationale Hersteller über den gesamten Produktlebenszyklus hinweg und verbindest verschiedene Produktwelten miteinander. Dabei gestaltest du aktiv unser Portfolio mit und arbeitest eng in interdisziplinären Teams zusammen.
Das bieten wir dir
Sinnvolle Arbeit mit Zukunft
: Gestalte die Energiezukunft in einem nachhaltigen Unternehmen einer zukunftsfähigen Branche aktiv mit.
Flexibilität für dein Leben
: Flexible Arbeitszeiten, 30 Urlaubstage pro Jahr sowie zusätzlich jeweils ein halber freier Tag an Heiligabend und Neujahr. Darüber hinaus ermöglichen wir bis zu zwei Monate Workation innerhalb der EU.
Gesundheit, Mobilität & Vorsorge
: Zuschuss für Sport (EGYM) oder das Deutschlandticket sowie eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge.
Exklusive Rabatte
: Attraktive Vergünstigungen bei nachhaltigen Unternehmen über FutureBens oder vielfältige Angebote über Corporate Benefits.
Starkes Miteinander
: Eine offene, unterstützende Arbeitsatmosphäre in einem engagierten und leidenschaftlichen Team.
Gemeinsam Erfolge feiern: Regelmäßige Company-Events, Pasta-Lunch sowie Freizeitangebote im Büro.
Top Ausstattung
: Moderne Hardware für Office & Homeoffice, ergonomische Arbeitsplätze und ein strukturiertes Onboarding vor Ort in München.
Das erwartet dich
Du betreust internationale Hersteller ganzheitlich über den gesamten Produktlebenszyklus – von der Markteinführung bis zum Phase-Out.
Als zentrale Ansprechperson entwickelst du bestehende Herstellerpartnerschaften weiter und bringst Markt- sowie Kundenfeedback aktiv ein.
Gemeinsam mit dem Category Lead gestaltest du ein zukunftsfähiges Produktportfolio.
Du betreust ein eigenes Lieferanten-Portfolio und entwickelst daraus konkrete Maßnahmen, um relevante Categories trend- und zukunftsgerichtet aufzustellen.
Markt- und Wettbewerbsentwicklungen behältst du im Blick und leitest daraus Impulse für unser Sortiment ab.
Mit Produktinformationen, Schulungen und Webinaren unterstützt du Vertrieb, Marketing und weitere interne Teams.
Du koordinierst category-übergreifende Themen und sorgst für einen transparenten Informationsaustausch zwischen den beteiligten Fachbereichen.
Auf Herstellerterminen, Events und Messen repräsentierst du Memodo und pflegst dein Netzwerk.
Das bringst du mit
Ein abgeschlossenes Studium im Bereich Wirtschaft, Wirtschaftsingenieurwesen, Ingenieurwesen oder eine vergleichbare Qualifikation.
Erste Berufserfahrung im Product Management, Produktmanagement, Category Management oder einer vergleichbaren Funktion.
Freude daran, komplexe Zusammenhänge zu analysieren und bereichsübergreifende Lösungen zu entwickeln.
Eine strukturierte, eigenverantwortliche Arbeitsweise sowie ein ausgeprägtes Organisationstalent.
Kommunikationsstärke und Spaß an der Zusammenarbeit mit internen sowie externen Partnern.
Technisches Interesse – idealerweise im Umfeld erneuerbarer Energien oder erklärungsbedürftiger Produkte.
Sehr gute Deutschkenntnisse (C1) sowie gute Englischkenntnisse (B2).

title: Associate Product Manager (m/f/d)
jd_text:
Interested in a career that matters?
The
Associate Product Manager (APM)
supports the Product Portfolio Manager within the Product Management Team (PMT) , the role is anchored in Global Marketing. The role focuses on ensuring operational excellence across the product lifecycle by coordinating cross-functional activities, supporting product portfolio management, and driving timely execution of product-related processes in alignment with the company's product range and industry strategies.
In this role you’ll make an impact by:
Support PMT Operations: Participate in PMT meetings, support agenda and target follow-up, and drive cross-functional actions to ensure timely execution.
Manage Product Establishment & Sampling – Coordinate product establishments, sampling activities, approvals, documentation, and stakeholder communication.
Maintain the Product Portfolio – Support product range analysis, product pruning, Product Change Notifications (PCNs), Product Data Sheets, and portfolio management tools.
Coordinate Cross-Functional Activities – Work with Engineering, Supply Chain, Marketing, and other stakeholders to resolve operational issues and support business requests.
Support Market & Product Development – Coordinate Near-to-Market activities, manage product variant requests, and support execution of new product initiatives.
Drive Operational Excellence – Contribute to business growth and continuous improvement by supporting quality, cost, supply, capacity, and customer-focused product management activities.
You will thrive in this role if you bring some of the following:
Bachelor's degree in Engineering or a related technical discipline.
Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in Engineering, Supply Chain, Marketing, or Product Management-related roles.
Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to coordinate and influence cross-functional teams.
Structured, analytical, and detail-oriented, with solid project coordination and problem-solving skills.
Proactive, curious, and accountable, demonstrating ownership and the ability to drive tasks through to completion.
Working Model:
This role follows a hybrid working model, with 3 days per week in the office and up to 2 days working remotely, subject to business needs. The role also requires regular travel to our Denmark headquarters. Travel is expected approximately 6–12 times per year (typically monthly to every other month), with each visit usually lasting up to one week. Travel frequency is generally higher during the first year and may reduce over time
Our purpose guides the way
In Novonesis, we know that solutions rooted in biology can help solve humanity’s biggest challenges. Since we began more than a century ago, this has been our guide. It’s how we've gotten so far. And it’s how we'll impact the future. Now, more than ever, the world needs change. And with biosolutions, the possibilities for transformation are endless. If our purpose resonates with you, we encourage you to apply.
Opportunities for everyone
We are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunity for all applicants. Therefore we ask you to apply without a cover letter or photo and instead include a few sentences in your CV explaining your motivation for applying.
We make all employment decisions based on business needs and welcome candidates with a wide range of backgrounds, including ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, or veteran status.
Explore Other Openings
If this position isn’t the right fit, you may still find other opportunities with us. You’re welcome to explore open roles or create a job agent to stay connected with future opportunities at novonesis.com/Career

The role requires a Bachelor's degree in Engineering or a related technical discipline and minimum 2 years of relevant experience. The company follows a hybrid working model with regular travel to the Denmark headquarters.

title: Innovation Lead (Intrapreneur)
jd_text:
Celonis is the global leader in Process Intelligence and the pioneer of Process Mining technology. As one of the world’s fastest-growing enterprise SaaS companies, we are changemakers pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. We invest heavily in advanced AI capabilities—specifically our Process Intelligence Graph—to turn data insights into immediate business action. We believe there is a massive opportunity to unlock global productivity and sustainability by placing intelligence at the core of every business process. Join our mission to make processes work for people, companies, and the planet.
About the team:
Celonis Labs is the innovation engine of Celonis: an independent, rapid-innovation unit within Celonis focused on pioneering entirely new business models and novel platform capabilities. Merging the radical agility, clean-slate freedom, and speed of a pre-seed startup with the massive market leverage of a global tech giant, we turn wild hypotheses into high-impact, market-ready realities. This is a unique environment where high-agency builders have the mandate, resources, and speed required to turn blank-canvas ideas into next-generation enterprise solutions.
The role
As
Innovation Lead
, you will shape the direction of innovation across Celonis Labs, creating and championing a diverse portfolio that spans exploratory prototypes, new business models, and partner solutions. Your mandate is to scan the horizon for disruptive high-potential opportunities and build compelling cases that get stakeholders and teams alike excited to build the next big thing.
But getting people excited is only the spark.
Bringing a founder’s mindset, you have the radical autonomy to hunt for paradigm-shifting concepts, backing your vision with intellectual rigor and sound hypotheses.
The work you’ll do
Source and curate a rich pipeline of disruptive ideas, applying first-principles thinking to isolate high-potential opportunities past the “cool idea” phase and pitch them to win the backing required to activate them as active Labs projects
Rigorously pressure-test your core assumptions around market feedback, technical feasibility, and strategic fit to either ruthlessly prune weak ideas early, or seamlessly transition your concepts into execution
Command executive attention and skip the slide-deck graveyard. Leverage AI capabilities and rapid mock-ups to bring abstract concepts to life instantly, turning a theory into a tangible, interactive experience that cuts through the noise and commands attention from our customers
Go straight to the decision-makers within our massive global network of Fortune 500 customers to build your own high-trust advisory circle. Cultivate a tight feedback loop for radical ideas with these visionary allies, establishing the high-level buy-in and trusted sounding board needed to shape and validate disruptive concepts in absolute confidence
The Qualifications You Need
Hands-on startup experience and a proven track record of building and launching products from scratch within the B2B software domain—whether as a venture-backed founder, co-founder, founder's associate, or intrapreneurial product manager
A demonstrated ability to bring abstract concepts to life alongside cross-functional teams rallying them behind a shared vision through product empathy and collaborative drive
Strong trend acumen and technical literacy that gives you a natural foresight for emerging technological shifts and a deep empathy for software architecture
High comfort with radical ambiguity, thriving in unstructured, rapidly shifting environments where, instead of waiting for a map, you think like a founder and are excited by the opportunity to draw the map yourself
An impatient bias for action and a can-do attitude, demonstrating exceptionally high agency and leading by example to actively getting things done and drive momentum
Exceptional communication and persuasion skills, with comfort pitching and building C-level relationships in both Engl
summary: Celonis is seeking an Innovation Lead to shape the direction of innovation across Celonis Labs, creating and championing a diverse portfolio of exploratory prototypes, new business models, and partner solutions. The role requires a founder's mindset, radical autonomy, and the ability to hunt for paradigm-shifting concepts. The ideal candidate will have hands-on startup experience, a proven track record of building and launching products from scratch, and strong trend acumen and technical literacy.

title: Innovation Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Gestalte Zukunft – mit Sinn, Software und echtem Innovationsgeist.
Unsere Kund:innen leisten in der Sozialwirtschaft jeden Tag Großartiges - von Sozialarbeiter:innen über Pflegekräfte bis hin zur Einrichtungsleitung. Die Hürden, die ihnen dabei begegnen, willst du aus dem Weg räumen. Aber du weißt, dass gute Absichten allein nichts verändern. Was verändert, ist eine Idee, die fliegt, ein Geschäftsmodell, das trägt und Lösungen, die in der Praxis wirklich helfen.
Du denkst nicht nur in Möglichkeiten. Du denkst in Hypothesen. Du fragst nicht nur "Was wäre, wenn?", sondern auch "Wie finden wir heraus, ob es wirklich funktioniert?"
Wenn das deine Art zu denken ist – dann lies weiter.
Worum es geht:
Wir sind ein Softwareunternehmen, das die Sozialwirtschaft neu erfinden will. Nicht weil es einfach ist. Sondern weil gerade die menschlichste Branche unserer Gesellschaft zu einer ihrer innovativsten werden kann. Wir glauben daran. Und wir arbeiten daran.
Als Innovationsmanager:in bist du die Person, die fragt: Was wäre, wenn? Und Antworten auf diese Frage findet. Du entwickelst Ideen, die heute noch niemand vermisst – aber morgen alle brauchen werden. Du durchdenkst, ob und wie daraus echte Lösungen werden können. Du arbeitest hypothesenbasiert: Du weißt, dass nicht jede Idee fliegt. Aber du weißt auch, wie man herausfindet, welche es tut.
Du managst keine Menschen. Du managst Innovationen – von der ersten Hypothese bis zum MVP, das Wirkung zeigt.
Was dich erwartet:
Ein Team, das genauso denkt wie du: neugierig, ambitioniert, unbequem wo nötig. Ein Unternehmen, das die Frage "Wie können wir unsere Wirkung skalieren?" nicht beantwortet, weil es muss, sondern weil sie es will. Und einen Markt, der dringend Menschen braucht, die bereit sind, ihn neu zu denken.
Du wirst scheitern. Nicht weil wir das wollen – sondern weil Innovation ohne Scheitern keine ist. Was zählt, ist was du daraus machst.
Was du mitbringst:
Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Studium: Die Fachrichtung ist uns weniger wichtig als die Art, wie du denkst. Idealerweise bringst du Erfahrung aus dem B2B-Softwareumfeld mit: Wir suchen niemanden, der schon alles kennt, aber versteht, wie Softwarelösungen entstehen, vermarktet und skaliert werden. Du weißt, was ein Geschäftsmodell trägt – und was es zum Scheitern bringt. Du hast Ideen nicht nur entwickelt, sondern sie durch die unbequemen Fragen geführt: Wer zahlt dafür? Warum? Und lässt sich das skalieren?
Erfahrung im Bereich Produktmanagement, Business Development, Gründung oder Strategie ist ein Plus, aber kein Muss. Was zählt, ist die Kombination aus unternehmerischem Denken und dem echten Willen, etwas zu bewegen.
Verhandlungssicheres Deutsch ist Voraussetzung.
Warum wir?
Weil du bei uns innovative Ideen zu realen Lösungen werden lassen kannst, die Menschen und Organisationen stärkt, die unsere Gesellschaft tragen. Dafür bieten wir dir Gestaltungsspielraum, Vertrauen und ein motiviertes Team, das Innovation lebt. Flexible Arbeitszeiten, Remote-Optionen und ein offenes Miteinander gehören dabei für uns zum Alltag.
Bereit loszulegen?
Dann lass uns gemeinsam die Sozialwirtschaft revolutionieren. Schick uns deinen Lebenslauf und sag uns in 1-2 Sätzen, welche Innovation in der Sozialwirtschaft du für längst überfällig hältst. Den Rest besprechen wir persönlich.

title: Innovation Lead (Intrapreneur)
company: Process Analytics Factory - PAFnow by Celonis
jd_text:
Celonis is the global leader in Process Intelligence and the pioneer of Process Mining technology. As one of the world’s fastest-growing enterprise SaaS companies, we are changemakers pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. We invest heavily in advanced AI capabilities—specifically our Process Intelligence Graph—to turn data insights into immediate business action. We believe there is a massive opportunity to unlock global productivity and sustainability by placing intelligence at the core of every business process. Join our mission to make processes work for people, companies, and the planet.
About The Team
Celonis Labs is the innovation engine of Celonis: an independent, rapid-innovation unit within Celonis focused on pioneering entirely new business models and novel platform capabilities. Merging the radical agility, clean-slate freedom, and speed of a pre-seed startup with the massive market leverage of a global tech giant, we turn wild hypotheses into high-impact, market-ready realities. This is a unique environment where high-agency builders have the mandate, resources, and speed required to turn blank-canvas ideas into next-generation enterprise solutions.
The role
As
Innovation Lead
, you will shape the direction of innovation across Celonis Labs, creating and championing a diverse portfolio that spans exploratory prototypes, new business models, and partner solutions. Your mandate is to scan the horizon for disruptive high-potential opportunities and build compelling cases that get stakeholders and teams alike excited to build the next big thing.
But getting people excited is only the spark.
Bringing a founder’s mindset, you have the radical autonomy to hunt for paradigm-shifting concepts, backing your vision with intellectual rigor and sound hypotheses.
The work you’ll do
Source and curate a rich pipeline of disruptive ideas, applying first-principles thinking to isolate high-potential opportunities past the “cool idea” phase and pitch them to win the backing required to activate them as active Labs projects
Rigorously pressure-test your core assumptions around market feedback, technical feasibility, and strategic fit to either ruthlessly prune weak ideas early, or seamlessly transition your concepts into execution
Command executive attention and skip the slide-deck graveyard. Leverage AI capabilities and rapid mock-ups to bring abstract concepts to life instantly, turning a theory into a tangible, interactive experience that cuts through the noise and commands attention from our customers
Go straight to the decision-makers within our massive global network of Fortune 500 customers to build your own high-trust advisory circle. Cultivate a tight feedback loop for radical ideas with these visionary allies, establishing the high-level buy-in and trusted sounding board needed to shape and validate disruptive concepts in absolute confidence
The Qualifications You Need
Hands-on startup experience and a proven track record of building and launching products from scratch within the B2B software domain—whether as a venture-backed founder, co-founder, founder's associate, or intrapreneurial product manager
A demonstrated ability to bring abstract concepts to life alongside cross-functional teams rallying them behind a shared vision through product empathy and collaborative drive
Strong trend acumen and technical literacy that gives you a natural foresight for emerging technological shifts and a deep empathy for software architecture
High comfort with radical ambiguity, thriving in unstructured, rapidly shifting environments where, instead of waiting for a map, you think like a founder and are excited by the opportunity to draw the map yourself
An impatient bias for action and a "can-do" attitude, demonstrating exceptionally high agency and leading by example to actively getting things done and drive momentum
Exceptional communication and persuasion skills, with comfort pitching and building C-level relationships in both Eng

title: Intrapreneur (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Gestalte Zukunft – mit Sinn, Software und echtem Innovationsgeist.
In der Sozialwirtschaft leisten unsere Kund:innen jeden Tag Großartiges. Und du willst den Arbeitsalltag von Sozialarbeiter:innen und Pflegekräften verbessern. Aber du weißt, dass gute Absichten allein nichts verändern. Was verändert, ist eine Idee, die fliegt. Ein Geschäftsmodell, das trägt. Eine Lösung, die Menschen in der Praxis wirklich hilft – nicht nur auf dem Papier.
Du denkst nicht nur in Möglichkeiten. Du denkst in Hypothesen. Du fragst nicht nur "Was wäre, wenn?", sondern auch "Wie finden wir heraus, ob es wirklich funktioniert?"
Wenn das deine Art zu denken ist – dann lies weiter.
Worum es geht:
Wir sind ein Softwareunternehmen, das die Sozialwirtschaft neu erfinden will. Nicht weil es einfach ist. Sondern weil gerade die menschlichste Branche unserer Gesellschaft zu einer ihrer innovativsten werden kann. Wir glauben daran. Und wir arbeiten daran.
Als Intrapreneur:in agierst du als Innovationsmanager:in, die:der fragt: Was wäre, wenn? Du entwickelst Ideen, die heute noch niemand vermisst – aber morgen alle brauchen werden. Du durchdenkst, ob und wie daraus echte Lösungen werden können. Du arbeitest hypothesenbasiert: Du weißt, dass nicht jede Idee fliegt. Aber du weißt auch, wie man herausfindet, welche es tut.
Du managst keine Menschen. Du managst Innovationen – von der ersten Hypothese bis zum MVP, das Wirkung zeigt.
Was dich erwartet:
Ein Team, das genauso denkt wie du: neugierig, ambitioniert, unbequem wo nötig. Ein Unternehmen, das die Frage "Wie können wir unsere Wirkung skalieren?" nicht beantwortet, weil es muss, sondern weil sie es will. Und einen Markt, der dringend Menschen braucht, die bereit sind, ihn neu zu denken.
Du wirst scheitern. Nicht weil wir das wollen – sondern weil Innovation ohne Scheitern keine ist. Was zählt, ist was du daraus machst.
Was du mitbringst:
Du hast ein abgeschlossenes Studium – die Fachrichtung ist uns weniger wichtig als die Art, wie du denkst. Du bringst Erfahrung aus dem B2B-Softwareumfeld mit – nicht weil wir jemanden suchen, der alles schon kennt, sondern weil du verstehst, wie Softwarelösungen entstehen, vermarktet und skaliert werden. Du weißt, was ein Geschäftsmodell trägt – und was es zum Scheitern bringt. Du hast Ideen nicht nur entwickelt, sondern sie durch die unbequemen Fragen geführt: Wer zahlt dafür? Warum? Und lässt sich das skalieren?
Erfahrung im Bereich Produktmanagement, Business Development, Gründung oder Strategie ist ein Plus – aber kein Muss. Was zählt, ist die Kombination aus unternehmerischem Denken und dem echten Willen, etwas zu bewegen.
Warum wir?
Weil du bei uns innovative Ideen zu realen Lösungen werden lassen kannst, die Menschen und Organisationen stärkt, die unsere Gesellschaft tragen. Dafür bieten wir dir Gestaltungsspielraum, Vertrauen und ein motiviertes Team, das Innovation lebt. Flexible Arbeitszeiten, Remote-Optionen und ein offenes Miteinander gehören dabei für uns zum Alltag.
Bereit loszulegen?
Dann lass uns gemeinsam die Sozialwirtschaft revolutionieren. Schick uns deinen Lebenslauf und sag uns in 1-2 Sätzen, welche Innovation in der Sozialwirtschaft du für längst überfällig hältst. Den Rest besprechen wir persönlich.

title: (Senior) Principal Innovation Management (f/m/d)
jd_text:
Purpose of the role
Responsibility for owning NXP’s Innovation Management function by driving selected innovation initiatives, partnerships, and operational activities. This role plays a key part in translating strategy into execution and establishing a strong foundation for the team.
Your Responsibilities
Drive innovation initiatives within defined ownership areas (e.g. startups, Long-Term Innovation programs, invention disclosures), ensuring execution and measurable impact
Support ecosystem engagement, including startup scouting, partnerships, and collaboration with universities and research institutions
Enable innovation governance and operations, including Innovation Boards, reporting, and coordination of key processes and stakeholders
Manage innovation projects end-to-end, from ideation and scoping to proof-of-concepts and scaling
Analyze market, technology, and ecosystem trends to generate insights and support strategic decision-making
Prepare high-quality materials, business cases, and presentations for senior stakeholders
Track and report on innovation KPIs, ensuring transparency and progress visibility across initiatives
Facilitate cross-functional collaboration and ensure alignment across teams and stakeholders
Requirements
Experience in innovation, strategy, consulting, R&D, or program management (≈5–12 years depending on level)
Strong execution and delivery mindset with the ability to manage initiatives end-to-end and drive outcomes
Ability to translate complex topics and trends into structured insights and actionable recommendations
Collaborative and influential working style across technical and business stakeholders
Strong prioritization skills and ability to operate effectively in ambiguous, fast-moving environments
Excellent communication skills, including clear storytelling and stakeholder alignment
High curiosity, learning agility, and interest in emerging technologies and innovation ecosystems
More information about NXP in Germany...
