title: Business Development Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Wer wir sind
RockQ Technologies (ehemals NC-Vision) ist ein in Stuttgart ansässiger Softwarepionier, der sich auf die digitale Transformation der Fertigungsindustrie spezialisiert hat. Wir bieten eine hochmoderne, KI-gestützte No-Code-Plattform, die die Lücke zwischen der operativen Realität und digitaler Innovation schließt.
Indem wir Fachexperten ermöglichen, komplexe Unternehmensanwendungen durch visuelle Modellierung – ganz ohne klassische Programmierung oder spezielles IT-Know-how – zu entwickeln, unterstützen wir Unternehmen dabei, Altsysteme zu modernisieren, Künstliche Intelligenz zu integrieren und intelligente Lösungen weltweit zu skalieren.
Wir suchen einen
Business Development / Portfolio Manager
(m/w/d), der gemeinsam mit unserem Technologie-Team die erfolgreiche Positionierung unseres innovativen Lösungsangebots am Markt vorantreibt.
In dieser Position sind Sie verantwortlich für die Umsatzsteigerung durch die Entwicklung und Umsetzung einer wirkungsvollen Go-to-Market-Strategie für industrielle Softwarelösungen. Sie übernehmen die Verantwortung für den gesamten Vertriebsprozess – von der Identifizierung potenzieller Zielkunden über die Generierung von Nachfrage und das Management der Vertriebspipeline bis hin zur Verhandlung und dem erfolgreichen Abschluss von Verträgen. Dabei stellen Sie eine hohe Kundenzufriedenheit sowie den langfristigen Mehrwert unserer Lösungen sicher.
Ihre Aufgaben
Go-to-Market-Strategie und Marktentwicklung
Identifikation potenzieller Zielkunden.
Segmentierung der Zielgruppen und Planung geeigneter Vertriebs- und Akquiseaktivitäten.
Entwicklung und Umsetzung einer zielgerichteten Go-to-Market-Strategie.
Steigerung der Bekanntheit unseres Leistungsportfolios durch die Teilnahme an Branchenveranstaltungen sowie den Aufbau relevanter Netzwerke.
Nutzung von Social Media zur Ansprache potenzieller Kunden und zur Generierung von Nachfrage.
Umsatz- und Pipeline-Management
Verantwortung für die Erreichung der Umsatzziele mit Bestands- und Neukunden.
Generierung neuer Geschäftsmöglichkeiten durch Proof of Value (PoV) sowie weitere geeignete Vertriebs- und Lead-Generierungsmaßnahmen.
Aktives Management der Vertriebspipeline sowie Sicherstellung einer hohen Datenqualität.
Kontinuierliche Optimierung von Vertriebsprozessen und Hinterfragen bestehender Vorgehensweisen zur Sicherstellung der Zielerreichung.
Regelmäßiges Reporting über den Status der Vertriebspipeline.
Erstellung zuverlässiger Umsatzprognosen sowie Sicherstellung einer hohen Forecast-Genauigkeit.
Vertrieb und Vertragsmanagement
Eigenverantwortliche Führung kommerzieller Verhandlungen mit dem Ziel einer optimalen Profitabilität.
Verantwortung für die Verhandlung und den erfolgreichen Abschluss von Verträgen.
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit internen Fachbereichen zur Erstellung wirtschaftlich attraktiver und wettbewerbsfähiger Angebote.
Aufbau und Pflege von Partnerschaften mit externen Vertriebspartnern, Distributoren und Systemintegratoren zur Erweiterung der Vertriebskanäle.
Ihr Profil
Mindestens 4 Jahre Berufserfahrung im B2B-Vertrieb von Softwarelösungen.
Idealerweise Erfahrung in Unternehmen, die Softwarelösungen für die Fertigungsindustrie anbieten.
Deutsch auf muttersprachlichem Niveau sowie sehr gute Englischkenntnisse.
Ausgeprägte Kommunikations- und Beziehungsmanagementfähigkeiten sowie die Fähigkeit, erfolgreich mit Ansprechpartnern auf unterschiedlichen Entscheidungsebenen zusammenzuarbeiten.
Ausgeprägtes kommerzielles Verständnis, unternehmerisches Denken sowie eine ziel- und ergebnisorientierte Arbeitsweise.
Was wir bieten
End-to-End-Verantwortung
– Sie übernehmen die vollständige Verantwortung für den gesamten Lebenszyklus von MES-Lösungen: von der Analyse der Geschäftsanforderungen über Design und Implementierung bis hin zur kontinuierlichen Optimierung und Weiterentwicklung der Produkte.
Hoher geschäftlicher Einfluss
– Ihre Entscheidungen tragen direkt zur Steigerung der Produktionseffizienz, zur digitalen Transformation und zu

title: Head of Commercial Partnerships – Retail (DACH)
jd_text:
Head of Commercial Partnerships – Retail (DACH)
Location: Berlin, Germany
Job Type: Full-Time, Permanent
Workplace: Hybrid
Are you a strategic commercial leader with a passion for retail, e-commerce and business growth?
An exciting opportunity has arisen for an experienced
Head of Commercial Partnerships
to lead the commercial growth strategy across the DACH region for one of the world's most innovative technology platforms.
This is a senior leadership role where you'll drive high-value retail partnerships, lead a high-performing commercial team and shape the future of digital commerce across Germany, Austria and Switzerland.
The Role
As Head of Commercial Partnerships, you'll be responsible for developing and executing the commercial strategy across the DACH region, building long-term relationships with leading retail and grocery brands while delivering ambitious revenue growth.
Key responsibilities include:
Leading commercial partnerships across the DACH region.
Developing and executing sales strategies to achieve revenue and growth targets.
Building, coaching and developing a high-performing commercial team.
Identifying new business opportunities and expanding strategic retail partnerships.
Negotiating complex commercial agreements with senior stakeholders.
Working cross-functionally with Operations, Strategy, Product, Marketing and Merchandising teams.
Managing large-scale commercial projects from planning through to execution.
Monitoring market trends and identifying opportunities for business expansion.
Using data and commercial insights to drive strategic decision-making.
Representing the business within the retail and e-commerce industry.
About You
You'll be an experienced commercial leader with a proven track record of delivering growth in a fast-paced environment.
You'll ideally have:
At least 6 years' experience in Sales, Business Development or Commercial Partnerships.
A minimum of 3 years' experience leading high-performing sales or commercial teams.
Strong experience within Retail, E-commerce, Consumer Goods (CPG) or a related sector.
A proven track record of delivering commercial growth and strategic partnerships.
Experience working within complex, matrix organisations.
Excellent project management and stakeholder management skills.
Strong commercial acumen with an analytical, data-driven approach.
Experience using Salesforce or similar CRM platforms.
Fluency in both
German and English
. (Preferred)
What's on Offer
Join one of the world's leading technology businesses.
Hybrid working.
Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package.
Opportunity to lead commercial strategy across the DACH region.
Work with some of the region's largest retail and grocery brands.
Excellent career development and progression opportunities.
A collaborative, innovative and high-performance working environment.
If you're an experienced commercial leader looking to make a significant impact within a global organisation at the forefront of retail innovation, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Account Executive Europe (m/f/d) Business Development & Closing (remote)
jd_text:
Your Mission
We are looking for an
Account Executive (m/f/d)
who will actively help further establish MakerVerse as a leading company in the on-demand manufacturing sector in Europe.
In this role, you will connect companies with our AI-powered platform, which enables solutions such as additive manufacturing, CNC machining, and injection molding. You will work closely with our Customer Acquisition Managers (CAMs), take on qualified leads, and guide them from the initial specific request through to successful contract closure, building sustainable, trusting customer relationships along the way.
This position is aimed at sales-oriented personalities who enjoy tapping into new business opportunities, building lasting customer relationships, and actively driving revenue and making a real impact in a dynamic environment.
This role is fully remote - you can work from anywhere in the European Union. Once a month, during the third week (Tuesday to Thursday), we come together at our Berlin office for team collaboration, meetings, a shared breakfast, and team activities. It’s a great opportunity to connect in person, exchange ideas, and strengthen our team culture.
Your Impact
Sales Responsibility: You will take on qualified sales opportunities and guide customers from their initial requirements through to a confirmed order.
Solution-Oriented Consulting: You will develop and present customized, technically oriented proposals tailored to the needs of our customers across various manufacturing technologies.
Customer Relationship Management: You will build and maintain strong customer relationships by serving as a trusted point of contact throughout the entire project lifecycle.
Customer Development: You will identify opportunities to expand existing customer relationships and generate follow-up orders.
Sales Collaboration: You will work closely with Customer Acquisition Managers (CAMs) to successfully convert pipeline opportunities into revenue.
Interdisciplinary Collaboration: You will collaborate with internal teams to ensure the smooth implementation of customer projects, resolve challenges, and identify new business opportunities.
Your DNA
Closing skills: At least 2 years of experience in B2B sales with a proven track record.
Consultative mindset: You enjoy understanding your clients’ challenges and developing tailored solutions, rather than focusing solely on high outreach volumes.
Communication skills: You excel with strong presentation and negotiation skills.
CRM experience: You are proficient in using CRM systems such as HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar tools to manage pipelines and create forecasts.
Team player with ownership: You enjoy collaborating across functions with other teams while taking ownership of your deals.
Technical Interest: Curiosity or interest in manufacturing technologies such as CNC machining, additive manufacturing, or injection molding is a major advantage.
Languages: Fluent German and English is required, as a significant part of our customer base is DACH-based. French or any additional language skills are a plus.
Join Us & Raise the Bar
At MakerVerse, we don’t just execute - we innovate. We don’t just meet expectations - we exceed them. If you’re ready to take on responsibility, take on meaningful challenges, and grow as part of a strong team, we look forward to receiving your application.
At MakerVerse, we live by these principles:
We Own It. We Deliver It. We take responsibility, act with purpose, and go the extra mile to make an impact.
We Execute Relentlessly. We Solve Big Challenges. We embrace a “doer” mentality and focus on solutions, not problems.
Our Customers’ Success = Our Success. We make an impact and prioritize what drives our customers’ success.
We Win Together. We Set the Bar High. We challenge and support one another to give our best and thrive as a high-performing team.
Curious about our
employee benefits
? Find more information at www.makerverse.com/careers
About Us
The future of Digital

title: Business Development Manager
jd_text:
Ready to take your career global?
Make your mark at one of the biggest names in payments. We’re looking for a
Business Development Manager
to join our ever evolving Enterprise Sales team and help shape the future of global commerce.
What you’ll own
You will drive growth across the DACH region, partnering with global and emerging eCommerce brands to unlock new opportunities through Global Payments solutions.
Your work will directly influence how we expand our market share across EMEA, contributing to a core pillar of our future growth strategy.
Drive new business growth by identifying, engaging and converting high-value prospects into long-term clients across the DACH region
Build a strong pipeline of opportunities, ensuring consistent progress toward ambitious revenue targets
Own the full sales cycle, from initial outreach through to deal closure, delivering measurable commercial outcomes
Create strategic client partnerships by understanding business needs and positioning solutions that deliver long-term value
Influence senior stakeholders, shaping decisions and securing buy-in for high-impact payment solutions
Build and maintain trusted relationships with founders, entrepreneurs and eCommerce leaders, positioning Global Payments as a strategic partner
Identify new growth opportunities within the market, unlocking additional revenue streams and strengthening competitive positioning
What you’ll bring
Proven success in business development, with a strong track record of acquiring new clients and driving revenue growth
Experience engaging senior stakeholders and influencing commercial decisions
Strong communication and relationship-building skills, with the ability to build trust quickly
Fluency in German and English, with confidence operating in an international environment
Commercial mindset, with the ability to identify opportunities and convert them into measurable outcomes
High levels of self-motivation, resilience and drive to succeed in a target-driven environment
It’s a bonus if you have
Experience in payments, ePayments, financial services or digital sales
Experience selling complex or software-based solutions
An existing network of senior or C-level contacts within eCommerce or related industries
About The Team
Our inclusive and global teams win together every day. We’re proud to have the best minds in the industry, who you can learn from as you grow your career. The people, the energy, the connections
– it’s unmatched. Come and be part of an ever-evolving company and get dynamic opportunities that go beyond borders.
What makes a Globalpayer?
Globalpayers think like a client, act like an owner and win as one team. We’re curious and innovative – always finding better ways to deliver impact. We empower each other to make decisions, and it’s our passion that drives excellence in everything we set out to do.
Does this sound like you? Then you sound like a Globalpayer. Apply now to take your career global.

title: Business Development Manager – Norddeutschland
jd_text:
Während viele Unternehmen in der aktuellen Konjunkturlage zögern, investiert die Hydrotechnik GmbH gezielt in Wachstum, Marktausbau und leistungsfähige Strukturen.
Mit rund 150 Mitarbeitenden, einem Jahresumsatz von ca. 40 Mio. EUR und Sitz in Limburg an der Lahn entwickeln und fertigen wir seit über 60 Jahren hochpräzise Lösungen zur Analyse und Optimierung von Fluidsystemen.
Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams suchen wir ein
Business Development Manager – Norddeutschland
Standort:
Norddeutschland
Zeitpunkt:
ab sofort
Art der Anstellung:
Vollzeit/unbefristet
Ihre Aufgaben:
Sie entwickeln den Markt Norddeutschland systematisch und nachhaltig – mit klarem Fokus auf Wachstum, Abschlussstärke und Kundenwert.
Aktive Marktentwicklung und Neukundenakquise im industriellen Umfeld
Aufbau langfristiger Kundenbeziehungen (Maschinenbau, Anlagenbau, Industrie)
Identifikation neuer Anwendungen und Projekte
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Technik, Produktmanagement und Vertrieb
Verantwortung für Umsatz, Pipeline und Marktdurchdringung
Ihr Profil:
Mehrjährige Erfahrung im technischen B2B-Vertrieb
Kenntnisse Hydraulik und/oder Sensorik
Hoher Eigenantrieb und ausgeprägte Abschlussorientierung
Sehr gutes technisches Verständnis
Strukturierte, selbstständige Arbeitsweise
Wir bieten - mehr als nur einen Job
Klare Wachstumsziele statt Vertriebsverwaltung
Kurze Entscheidungswege und direkte Wirkung
Leistungsorientierte Vergütung
Langfristige Entwicklungsperspektive
Deine Chance
Während andere bremsen, investieren wir.
Wenn Sie Leistung bringen wollen, Verantwortung suchen und aktiv an Wachstum mitarbeiten möchten, freuen wir uns auf Ihre Bewerbung. karriere(at)hydrotechnik.com
Ihr Ansprechpartner bei der Hydrotechnik ist
Elisa Heuser
+49 6431 4004-727

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Product Manager
REMOTE (EU Based)
Full-time
Who we are
At Jaipur Robotics, we’re building AI-powered solutions that transform the waste industry — turning complex data into automation, efficiency, and environmental impact. As a fast-growing, VC-backed clean-tech startup based in Switzerland, we work with leading waste operators across Europe and are now expanding our product and customer-facing teams.
What we offer
We’re looking for a
Product Manager
who can combine product ownership, data analysis, and close customer engagement.
This is not a traditional desk-based product role. You will spend significant time working directly with customers, visiting industrial plants, understanding how our products are used in real operating environments, and identifying where they can create greater value.
You will act as the bridge between customers, engineering, AI, operations, and commercial teams. You will critically evaluate customer requests, distinguish between individual preferences and scalable product needs, and translate the most valuable opportunities into clear product priorities and specifications. The role combines elements of
product management, product analytics, and customer success
. You will be responsible not only for defining what should be built, but also for ensuring that customers understand, adopt, and benefit from the product.
Key Responsibilities
Build strong relationships with existing customers and develop a deep understanding of how they use Jaipur Robotics’ products.
Visit customer sites regularly to observe workflows, gather feedback, and identify improvement opportunities.
Evaluate customer requests critically, distinguishing scalable product needs from one-off customizations.
Consolidate feedback across customers and translate recurring needs into clear product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria.
Define and prioritize the product roadmap with engineering, AI, operations, commercial teams, and leadership.
Use customer feedback, product analytics, SQL, and statistical analysis to guide decisions and measure feature impact.
Monitor adoption, engagement, retention, customer satisfaction, and operational value after release.
Support product launches, onboarding, training, troubleshooting, and feature validation.
Communicate product decisions and trade-offs clearly to customers and internal teams.
Build scalable product and customer-success processes as the company expands.
Requirements
Degree in engineering, computer science, automation, data science, electronics, industrial engineering, or a related technical field.
2+ years of experience in product management, solutions engineering, product engineering, technical customer success, or a similar customer-facing role.
Strong analytical skills, including hands-on experience with SQL, relational databases, statistics, and data analysis.
Experience with product analytics or BI tools such as PostHog, Metabase, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Power BI, or Tableau.
Ability to turn complex customer and operational problems into clear, structured product requirements.
Strong judgment, communication, and customer relationship skills.
Comfortable collaborating with software, AI, field, IT, and industrial teams.
High ownership, independence, and ability to work effectively in an early-stage environment.
Willingness to travel frequently across Europe and work in industrial environments.
Fluent English.
Nice to Have
Experience with AI, computer vision, industrial automation, IoT, edge computing, or data-intensive products.
Background in industrial B2B SaaS, technical customer success, or product roles involving direct customer engagement.
Experience with manufacturing, utilities, waste management, infrastructure, or other industrial sectors.
Familiarity with Python, APIs, data pipelines, cloud platforms, and analytics engineering.
Experience in an early-stage startup or scale-up.
Knowledge of German, French, Italian, or Spanish.
Based in Europe with convenient access
summary: Jaipur Robotics is seeking a Product Manager to combine product ownership, data analysis, and customer engagement, acting as a bridge between customers, engineering, AI, operations, and commercial teams. The role involves building strong relationships with customers, evaluating customer requests, and defining product priorities. The company is a fast-growing, VC-backed clean-tech startup based in Switzerland, focusing on developing AI-powered solutions for the waste industry.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Product Manager
REMOTE (EU Based)
Full-time
Who we are
At Jaipur Robotics, we’re building AI-powered solutions that transform the waste industry — turning complex data into automation, efficiency, and environmental impact. As a fast-growing, VC-backed clean-tech startup based in Switzerland, we work with leading waste operators across Europe and are now expanding our product and customer-facing teams.
What we offer
We’re looking for a
Product Manager
who can combine product ownership, data analysis, and close customer engagement.
This is not a traditional desk-based product role. You will spend significant time working directly with customers, visiting industrial plants, understanding how our products are used in real operating environments, and identifying where they can create greater value.
You will act as the bridge between customers, engineering, AI, operations, and commercial teams. You will critically evaluate customer requests, distinguish between individual preferences and scalable product needs, and translate the most valuable opportunities into clear product priorities and specifications. The role combines elements of
product management, product analytics, and customer success
. You will be responsible not only for defining what should be built, but also for ensuring that customers understand, adopt, and benefit from the product.
What we offer
Build strong relationships with existing customers and develop a deep understanding of how they use Jaipur Robotics’ products.
Visit customer sites regularly to observe workflows, gather feedback, and identify improvement opportunities.
Evaluate customer requests critically, distinguishing scalable product needs from one-off customizations.
Consolidate feedback across customers and translate recurring needs into clear product requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria.
Define and prioritize the product roadmap with engineering, AI, operations, commercial teams, and leadership.
Use customer feedback, product analytics, SQL, and statistical analysis to guide decisions and measure feature impact.
Monitor adoption, engagement, retention, customer satisfaction, and operational value after release.
Support product launches, onboarding, training, troubleshooting, and feature validation.
Communicate product decisions and trade-offs clearly to customers and internal teams.
Build scalable product and customer-success processes as the company expands.
Requirements
Degree in engineering, computer science, automation, data science, electronics, industrial engineering, or a related technical field.
2+ years of experience in product management, solutions engineering, product engineering, technical customer success, or a similar customer-facing role.
Strong analytical skills, including hands-on experience with SQL, relational databases, statistics, and data analysis.
Experience with product analytics or BI tools such as PostHog, Metabase, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Power BI, or Tableau.
Ability to turn complex customer and operational problems into clear, structured product requirements.
Strong judgment, communication, and customer relationship skills.
Comfortable collaborating with software, AI, field, IT, and industrial teams.
High ownership, independence, and ability to work effectively in an early-stage environment.
Willingness to travel frequently across Europe and work in industrial environments.
Fluent English.
Nice to Have
Experience with AI, computer vision, industrial automation, IoT, edge computing, or data-intensive products.
Background in industrial B2B SaaS, technical customer success, or product roles involving direct customer engagement.
Experience with manufacturing, utilities, waste management, infrastructure, or other industrial sectors.
Familiarity with Python, APIs, data pipelines, cloud platforms, and analytics engineering.
Experience in an early-stage startup or scale-up.
Knowledge of German, French, Italian, or Spanish.
Based in Europe with convenient access to majo

The role involves defining product priorities, working with customers, and analyzing data to guide decisions. The company is a fast-growing, VC-backed clean-tech startup based in Switzerland, working on AI-powered solutions for the waste industry.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
HCP Portal Product Manager / Product Owner (Pharma) – 12 Months
About the role: A global Nutrition/healthcare business is building a new digital engagement platform for Healthcare Professionals (HCPs) — doctors, nurses, and clinical specialists — and needs an experienced Product Manager/Owner to take it from concept to live MVP within 6 months.
This is a hands-on, delivery-focused product ownership role, not a strategy or advisory position. You will own the roadmap, backlog, and delivery outcome end-to-end, directing a cross-functional team (Martech/Platform Owner, IT/vendors, Marketing, Medical/Regulatory) to launch a functional HCP portal and drive real user adoption.
Key responsibilities:
Own the HCP portal product end-to-end – define MVP scope, roadmap, and backlog
Define priority HCP use cases and journeys (education, patient support, services)
Drive MVP delivery within 6 months, working with Martech, IT/vendors, Marketing and Medical/Regulatory teams
Navigate MLR (Medical/Legal/Regulatory) compliance requirements throughout
Launch the portal, drive go-to-market activation, and monitor adoption/engagement
Define the roadmap for scaling to a multi-market omnichannel platform
Must-have experience:
Proven track record delivering HCP portals / HCP engagement platforms in pharma or healthcare
End-to-end digital product launch experience (not advisory-only)
Strong understanding of CRM-driven engagement (e.g. Salesforce) and omnichannel/digital customer journeys
Familiarity with pharma regulatory constraints (MLR)
Product ownership mindset – comfortable driving delivery in ambiguous, cross-functional environments
Nice-to-have:
Nutrition sector experience
Exposure to DAM/content workflows, personalisation/segmentation, or AI in customer engagement
To apply, please submit your CV along.

The ideal candidate has experience in delivering HCP portals or engagement platforms in pharma or healthcare, with a strong understanding of CRM-driven engagement and pharma regulatory constraints. The role requires a hands-on, delivery-focused approach, with the ability to navigate MLR compliance requirements and drive cross-functional teams.

title: (Senior) Product Manager (all genders)
jd_text:
Across Europe, fleets are under enormous pressure. Fuel costs are rising. Regulatory requirements are tightening. The shift to low-emission vehicles is accelerating. And yet around 70% of European businesses still manage their fleets manually, with spreadsheets, disconnected tools, and processes that were never built for this moment.
Shiftmove
exists to make this simple.
We are a profitable B2B SaaS company and the fastest-growing fleet management platform in Europe, born from the merger of Avrios and Vimcar, and now serving tens of thousands of corporate customers across Germany, France, Spain, Switzerland, Poland, and beyond. Most recently, we acquired Optimum and Ocean in France, expanding our footprint and doubling down on one of Europe's largest fleet markets. Our AI-powered platform connects real-time vehicle data with intelligent software so that businesses can procure smarter, control costs, stay compliant, and lead the transition to cleaner mobility, all from one place.
About the role:
As our
(Senior)
Product Manager,
you will take over one of the most strategically important product domains at Shiftmove -
the platform and ecosystem layer,
aka the connective tissue between Avrios, Vimcar, Optimum, Océan, and external partners.
You will own the product direction for this layer: deciding what becomes a reusable platform capability, what should stay market-specific, and where the team focuses first. You will work directly with Engineering, senior leadership, and cross-functional stakeholders across Germany and France to bring clarity to an ambiguous domain and turn complex problems into focused product direction.
This is a build and define phase, with a large scope and direct impact on Shiftmove's ability to expand across Europe, grow cross-sell revenue, and establish the platform foundation behind the leading fleet mobility ecosystem on the continent.
What you'll do:
Platform & Ecosystem Strategy: Own the product direction for our platform and integration scope, connecting our products, external partners, market-specific systems, and customer workflows across Europe
Ambiguity into Direction: Create structure where ownership, priorities, and system boundaries are not yet fully defined. Decide what becomes a reusable platform capability, what should stay market-specific, and where the team focuses first
Discovery and Delivery: Lead discovery and delivery for core platform capabilities: shared workflows, reusable integration patterns, APIs, data flows, and interoperability between products
European Market Expansion: Build internationalisation capabilities that help our core products scale beyond one market at a time
Cross-Product Portfolio: Shape initiatives that bring Avrios, Vimcar, Optimum, and Océan closer together, reducing friction for customers and unlocking stronger cross-sell opportunities across the portfolio
Stakeholder Alignment: Work closely with Engineering, GTM, Sales, Customer Success, country teams, and senior leadership to align priorities and move complex topics forward across products and markets
Focus and Ownership: Protect the team from reactive request intake by setting clear priorities, defining ownership boundaries, and keeping focus on long-term platform value over one-off solutions
What the first 6 months might look like:
Months 1 to 2: Focus on onboarding, learning about our product portfolio, stakeholders, and ways of working.
Months 3 to 4: Build trusting relationships with your team, translating your learnings into a focused product strategy and roadmap, and working on first challenges
Months 5 to 6: You're driving your topics, delivering reusable integration and internationalisation capabilities that make our platform more scalable across markets.
What you'll need to be successful:
Scaleup Mindset: You possess a fierce ownership mentality, thrive in ambiguity, and enjoy building structure where there isn't a playbook.
B2B Product Background: You have a solid track record in B2B product manag
summary: The role of Senior Product Manager at Shiftmove involves leading the product direction for the platform and ecosystem layer, connecting real-time vehicle data with intelligent software to help businesses manage their fleets. The ideal candidate will have a solid track record in B2B product management and thrive in ambiguity. title: Senior Product Manager, Payments
jd_text:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Senior Product Manager, Payments based in Germany.
This role offers the opportunity to lead product strategy and execution for large-scale payment experiences used by merchants and shoppers worldwide.
You will own a key area within a payments ecosystem where reliability, trust, and seamless user experiences are critical.
Working closely with engineering, design, support, and strategic partners, you will transform complex challenges into impactful product solutions.
The position combines strategic thinking with hands-on execution, requiring strong ownership from discovery through delivery.
Your decisions will directly influence revenue growth, conversion rates, merchant satisfaction, and the overall quality of payment experiences.
You will thrive in a distributed, collaborative environment where clear communication, autonomy, and product excellence are highly valued.
Accountabilities
The Senior Product Manager, Payments will take ownership of a defined product area, driving strategy, prioritization, and execution while collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver reliable and scalable payment solutions.
Define and execute product direction for a key payments area, establishing priorities, goals, success metrics, and measurable outcomes.
Improve merchant and shopper experiences across critical payment journeys, including onboarding, checkout, refunds, disputes, payouts, reporting, and platform reliability.
Identify opportunities to reduce friction, improve product quality, lower support demand, and increase user trust through continuous optimization.
Use data insights, customer feedback, support trends, and business signals to determine priorities and make informed product decisions.
Collaborate with payment partners to improve integrations, manage dependencies, deliver enhancements, and resolve technical or operational challenges.
Work closely with engineering and design teams on complex product problems, balancing user needs, technical constraints, and business objectives.
Maintain strong stakeholder alignment through clear documentation, communication of progress, risk management, and transparent decision-making.
Create and refine product documentation, including requirements, briefs, workflows, and technical discussions, while supporting execution throughout the product lifecycle.
Requirements
The ideal candidate is an experienced product leader with strong payments expertise, excellent product judgment, and the ability to operate effectively in complex, distributed environments.
7+ years of experience in product management, including senior individual contributor experience owning complex products or problem areas.
4+ years of experience working in payments, fintech, or related financial technology environments, ideally with products operating at significant scale.
Proven ability to lead cross-functional initiatives through strong communication, prioritization, and execution skills.
Strong product sense and the ability to make effective decisions in ambiguous situations without needing complete information.
Experience working on high-trust, mission-critical systems where reliability and user confidence are essential.
Ability to collaborate deeply with engineers and designers, understanding technical challenges and making thoughtful product tradeoffs.
Strong analytical mindset with experience using metrics, dashboards, experimentation, and data insights to guide decisions.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, especially in remote and asynchronous work environments.
Familiarity with open-source ecosystems, commerce platforms, or related technologies is considered a plus.
Benefits
Competitive salary range of $170,000-$240,000 USD, with compensation adjusted and paid according to local currency.
Fully remote opportunity with the flexibility to work from anywhere.
Straightforward

title: Product Manager, Consumer Platform  (f/m/x)
jd_text:
Our Tech department is the driving force behind one of the most successful start-ups in Europe since 2012. Based in Berlin, Germany, we have an innovative and driven team that uses cutting-edge technologies to redefine the used-car market.
As a Product Manager, you will join our  Customer Journey team, work closely with senior management of the Marketing department to contribute to and implement a product strategy that drives customer acquisition, retention and conversion. Together with our agile development teams and stakeholders across Europe, you will work to deploy state-of-the-art technology and bring the industry to a new level.
Our offer:
Educational budget for your personal growth
Your life-work balance and home office are part of our culture, you can work from home up to 5 days a week
Team and company events like Hackathons, International Nights, Company Parties, Football and more
More than 100 different nationalities make up for a truly international and diverse working environment
Job Description
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to enhance our customer facing products, focusing on lead generation, customer engagement, and retention.
Work closely with senior management of the Marketing department to develop a long-term product strategy that drives customer acquisition, retention and conversion.

Strong analytical skills, with the ability to use hard data and metrics to back up assumptions, design experiments, and interpret results.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to convey ideas to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Experience leading agile software development teams, with evidence of creative thinking, independent problem-solving, and team motivation.
Additional Information
Join us, building the best way to buy and sell cars!
You do not meet 100% of the requirements? Take your shot and apply anyway! We offer room for growth and challenges to learn from.

Marija Dimitrova
At AUTO1 Group we live an open culture, believe in direct communication, and value diversity. We welcome every applicant; regardless of gender, ethnic origin, religion, age, sexual identity, disability, or any other non-merit factor.

title: GTM Product Manager
jd_text:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a GTM Product Manager based in Germany.
This is an opportunity to lead the evolution of critical go-to-market technology solutions that enable business growth, operational efficiency, and customer success. In this role, you will own the strategy, roadmap, and lifecycle of internal products that power revenue teams across multiple functions. You will combine product thinking, technical expertise, and business insight to transform complex operational needs into scalable solutions. Working closely with teams across sales, marketing, customer success, engineering, data, and automation, you will shape the future of revenue technology ecosystems. This position is ideal for a strategic product leader who enjoys solving complex problems, driving innovation, and leveraging AI to build smarter, more efficient business capabilities.
Accountabilities
Own the vision, strategy, roadmap, and lifecycle of one or more go-to-market products and the business capabilities they support.
Define product priorities based on business impact, stakeholder needs, customer requirements, and strategic objectives.
Build and maintain scalable technology solutions by treating internal systems as products rather than standalone tools.
Drive technology decisions, including evaluating build versus buy approaches, automation opportunities, AI adoption, and long-term platform evolution.
Partner with cross-functional teams including Revenue Operations, Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, Business Systems, Data, AI, and Engineering to deliver impactful solutions.
Translate complex business requirements into scalable product strategies, balancing immediate needs with long-term product direction.
Establish product success metrics, governance processes, documentation standards, release planning, and adoption strategies.
Continuously improve the go-to-market technology ecosystem by identifying opportunities for simplification, optimization, and innovation.
Requirements
Proven experience owning business systems, technology products, or product portfolios within a go-to-market, revenue operations, or similar business environment.
Strong understanding of GTM architecture, integrations, data models, and end-to-end revenue processes.
Experience defining product strategies, roadmaps, priorities, and measurable success metrics for internal technology products.
Ability to evaluate technical solutions and determine when capabilities should be delivered through configuration, automation, AI solutions, custom development, or third-party platforms.
Hands-on experience using AI tools to improve workflows, build internal solutions, and optimize operational processes.
Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to communicate effectively and align teams around product decisions.
Experience working with GTM technologies such as CRM platforms, sales engagement tools, marketing automation systems, business intelligence solutions, APIs, integration platforms, or similar technologies.
Strong analytical, strategic, and problem-solving skills with a focus on delivering measurable business outcomes.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English.
Ability to work effectively in a remote, asynchronous, and globally distributed environment.
Experience working remotely is a plus but not required.
Benefits
Fully remote work environment with the flexibility to work from anywhere.
Flexible working hours within an asynchronous work culture.
Competitive salary package with compensation based on role, location, experience, and market conditions.
Stock options and opportunities to participate in company ownership.
Flexible paid time off to support work-life balance.
Paid parental leave.
Mental health support and wellbeing resources.
Dedicated learning and development budget to support professional growth.
Home office budget and IT equipment support.

title: Product Manager for Plastic Modular Belts
jd_text:
Role description
As a Product Manager (m/f/x), you are responsible for the Plastics division, build trusting and long-term customer relationships, and identify individual needs with a strong sense of insight. In close and collaborative partnership with the management team, you develop strategic approaches and implement them in a goal-oriented manner to ensure sustainable revenue and long-term business success.
Key responsibilites
The fact that our customers are satisfied and accustomed to high quality is thanks to you: you maintain personal customer relationships and build new ones. You also know how to analyze specific customer needs and present tailored solutions. We place our trust in you from the very beginning.
You take on product responsibility for the Plastics division.
You make a significant impact by developing appropriate market strategies in direct cooperation with our Head of Business Unit and by monitoring the sales market and competitors. In doing so, you create transparency and structure.
Through your personal commitment, you achieve the planned sales and profit targets in your sales territory - and it shows.
What we look for
You have a degree in industrial engineering or a qualification as a technician/master craftsman (m/f/x) (or comparable), at least one year of experience in a similar position, and are looking for a new long-term challenge where you can learn a lot. Then you’ve come to the right place.
In addition, you enjoy working in a conceptual and strategic way and have a solid technical understanding, combined with an affinity for IT (MS Office, CRM systems, etc.). Great – this know-how will help you and us successfully master upcoming challenges.
You also have very good language skills in German and English - then you are an excellent fit for us!
What we offer
We want you to feel comfortable. Through intensive structured onboarding and training, you will quickly get to know your tasks and our company. You will become part of a strong team.
We value your work. You will receive attractive, performance-based compensation along with many additional benefits such as employee savings schemes, employer contributions to pension plans, additional private health insurance, meal subsidies in our canteen, free parking, bike leasing, free beverages, and more.
You will receive everything you need: office equipment including laptop, mobile phone, and tablet, as well as a neutral company car that you can also use privately.
This is how we keep things moving. You will be able to take on exciting challenges in a company with flat hierarchies and short decision-making paths.

title: Technical Product Manager (all genders)
jd_text:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Technical Product Manager (all genders) based in Germany.
Join a fast-growing digital product environment where you'll play a key role in shaping and optimizing a complex e-commerce ecosystem built around Shopify. This position combines operational ownership with strategic product planning, ensuring scalable, high-performing solutions that enhance the customer experience. Working closely with cross-functional teams, you will evaluate technical opportunities, guide implementation decisions, and help prioritize initiatives that deliver meaningful business impact. This is an individual contributor role offering significant autonomy, collaboration, and the opportunity to influence both product direction and operational excellence in a fully remote setting across Europe.
Accountabilities
Serve as the technical product expert for Shopify, evaluating implementation requests and determining the most appropriate solution using native functionality, third-party applications, or custom development.
Oversee the operational understanding of the end-to-end e-commerce platform, ensuring seamless integration across systems such as ERP, customer service, subscriptions, analytics, and infrastructure.
Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to support operational processes, improve tracking capabilities, and ensure reliable platform performance.
Drive customer-centric product decisions by balancing technical feasibility, user experience, and business priorities when defining solutions.
Monitor website performance, identify opportunities for optimization, and recommend improvements through platform capabilities, automation tools, and workflow enhancements.
Contribute to product roadmap planning by assessing stakeholder requests, challenging low-impact initiatives, and focusing development efforts on high-value opportunities.
Requirements
Strong technical understanding of Shopify, including platform architecture, ecosystem capabilities, application integrations, and limitations, with the ability to communicate effectively with engineering teams despite not being a developer.
Experience working with complex e-commerce environments involving multiple integrated business systems and cross-functional stakeholders.
Knowledge of web performance principles, including Lighthouse metrics, rendering performance, and optimization best practices.
Practical experience using AI tools to improve day-to-day productivity, with familiarity with workflow automation platforms such as n8n considered an advantage.
Understanding of data privacy, GDPR compliance, secure data handling, and related operational considerations.
Excellent analytical thinking, ownership mindset, prioritization skills, and confidence to challenge assumptions while maintaining collaborative relationships.
Fluent English communication skills; German is considered a plus.
Benefits
Fully remote position available across Europe with flexible working hours.
One-time €500 home office allowance to create a productive workspace.
Choice of Mac or Windows equipment from day one.
Monthly €50 flexible spending allowance.
Continuous learning opportunities, dedicated development time, and professional growth support.
40% employee discount across the product portfolio.
30 vacation days, one additional personal holiday, New Year's Eve off, plus up to 10 unpaid leave days when needed.
Comprehensive health insurance benefits with reimbursements of up to €600 annually for eligible healthcare expenses.
Pension contribution after the probation period, including additional employer matching.
Access to exclusive discounts through corporate partner programs.
Company merchandise and a collaborative, people-focused culture.
Wellness benefits through a choice of fitness and wellbeing membership programs.

title: Data Product Manager, Dynamic Assortment - (Quick Commerce)
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
We are on the lookout for a
Product Manager
to join the Dynamic Assortment tribe within the Quick Commerce team on our journey to always deliver amazing experiences.
Be part of redefining how customers experience quick commerce. You’ll help build technology that scales our non-food offerings, reaching new market segments and driving revenue growth. By innovating within our Quick Commerce Team, you’ll make Delivery Hero the go-to platform for a broad range of products, helping us grow faster and deliver more value to customers around the world.
The Dynamic Assortment team operates as a specialized data team within the QC Assortment domain. Our mission is to maximize customer lifetime value and drive profitable growth by transforming our static product catalog into an adaptive and dynamic one. We develop and refine sophisticated data models that intelligently curate and personalize the product assortment, enhancing the customer experience and optimizing business outcomes. Our work is a critical enabler of the Quick Commerce strategy to make every product "easy to list, effortless to discover, and inspiring to convert."
We are seeking a strong Product Manager to own and deliver the data products that power our intelligent digital shelf. You will work closely with data science, engineering, and cross-domain stakeholders to translate our vision into a tangible product roadmap. You will be at the forefront of transforming our static catalog into an adaptive, personalized experience by leveraging unified demand intelligence, deploying advanced machine learning models, and embedding a culture of continuous experimentation and optimization.
This role requires a blend of strategic thinking, technical depth, and a passion for data. You will be responsible for the entire lifecycle of our data products, from ideation and model development to experimentation and performance monitoring, directly influencing key growth metrics such as conversion rates, average order value, and customer retention.
Drive Product Vision & Roadmap: Translate the team's vision into a clear, actionable product roadmap. Define and prioritize features and initiatives based on business impact, customer value, and technical feasibility.
Lead Data Model Development: Partner with data scientists and engineers to develop, refine, and deploy machine learning models for intelligent product curation, personalization, and substitution.
Own Experimentation & Optimization: Embed systematic experimentation and rapid iteration into the product development workflow. Design, run, and analyze A/B tests to validate hypotheses and drive measurable improvements in algorithm performance and business KPIs.
Deliver on Key Initiatives: Lead the execution of strategic projects, such as implementing experimentation with customer-facing Category Tree structure, driving the Category Recommendation engine, and developing models for automated merchandising and cross-listing.
Cross-Functional Stakeholder Alignment: Partner with stakeholders across Merchandising, Catalog, Data Analytics, Platforms Product, and commercial teams to align on strategies and ensure our data products meet business needs. Articulate complex data concepts to non-technical audiences to build consensus and drive decision-making across our global platforms.
Own Metrics & Performance: Est

The ideal candidate will have a blend of strategic thinking, technical depth, and a passion for data. The company is a leading local delivery platform operating in around 65 countries worldwide, with a strong focus on innovation and customer experience.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
The future looks like you
With over 1,300 stores in 17 European countries and more than 25,000 employees, C&A is one of Europe’s leading fashion retailers. Every day, C&A welcomes millions of visitors to its stores in Europe and to its online shop. We are building on more than 180 years of experience in the fashion industry and continuously modernize the company to become an omnichannel fashion retailer.
C&A is seeking an experienced, data driven, and highly customer focused Product Manager to join the Product Management team for our Fit for Future (F4F) Programme.
Digital Product Managers are responsible for leading the development and execution of our product roadmap, driving product innovation and ensuring the successful launch and adoption of products. Product Managers act as the voice of the customer within C&A, ensuring that we build and develop elegant solutions that enhance the customer experience. They are essential for driving customer-centricity within the organization.
The Product Manager will play a key role in the largest digital transformation in the history of C&A!
Your Responsibilities
Drive the vision of your products and understand how it is embedded in the overall product and the digital strategy so that it can be translated into prioritized and actionable plans.
Understand and define customer needs to set clear goals and KPIs for the product to ensure that the product creates and delivers tangible value for both our customers and our business alike. Coordinate the documentation of business requirements, user stories, and to-be-processes in scope.
Validate ideas and assumptions in a short feedback loop to avoid risks earlier in the development process and build products that delight our consumers. Review / challenge business requirements when needed to ensure adoption of industry standards.
Figure out solutions to complex problems by analyzing and utilizing data to inform product decisions and drive growth.
Own the prioritization of your backlog, the discovery of strategic opportunities, enabling and empowering your team by providing product roadmaps and deliver consistently with a 'can do' attitude within a context of high alignment and high autonomy.
Maintain a constant communication flow and updates about status of the development and initiatives to main stakeholders, collaborate with cross-functional teams, including designers, developers, and business offering transparency and enthusiastically communicate achievements.
What You Bring
Proven ability to utilize advanced analytical skills to drive evidence-based product development, understanding the importance of regularly measuring and analyzing product performance
A bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as business administration, marketing, computer science, engineering, or a related field
Relevant experience in a similar product management role, with a demonstrated understanding of the techniques and methods of modern product discovery and product delivery
Fluent in English spoken and written
Exceptional communication skills, with a proven ability to engage with technical and business stakeholders in a constructive and collaborative way
Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects and products simultaneously
Willingness to roll up sleeves
Extreme attention to detail
Excellent business judgment and good instinct
What We Offer
This is an opportunity for you to join us on a truly exciting transformation journey, where you’ll be able to leave footprints that last and contribute significantly to our vision. As well as knowing that you played a key part in the evolution of one of Europe’s most recognizable retail brands, you will also enjoy:
Countless learning and development opportunities via our C&A academy
Access to a Partner Discount Scheme
An employee discount of 20% in C&A stores
30 days' vacation plus an additional 3 days off
Corporate pension scheme
Mental health and wellness program

title: Product Manager - Data-Viz Startup - B2B SaaS
jd_text:
At Vizzlo, we create tools that make data visualization accessible to everyone.
Creating high-quality, on-brand charts, visuals, and business graphics used to be hard. Vizzlo changes that. We offer the only tool on the market that integrates with Google Slides, PowerPoint, and more. Whether for presentations, business documents, or the Web—with Vizzlo, anyone can visualize their ideas in seconds.
We are a small team of enthusiasts in a SaaS startup. We’re proud of our hands-on culture, we talk plainly to each other, and improve anything we’re working on until it’s perfect.
We are looking for a hands-on and product-obsessed
Product Manager
to join our team remotely within Germany or in our offices in Leipzig. This is a full-time role ideal for someone who geeks out about data visualization, isn't afraid of Markdown specs and nerdy engineers, and is ready to do whatever it takes to ship an awesome product.
Tasks
As our Product Manager, you own the "what" and the "why" behind everything we ship. You talk to customers, dig through usage data, write crisp specs, and sit down with our engineers to turn ideas into features people love. In a small team, no task is "not your job"—and you wouldn't have it any other way.
Your responsibilities are:
Product Roadmap
: Define and prioritize what we build next based on customer feedback, market insights, and business goals.
Specs & Documentation
: Write clear, detailed specs in Markdown—because that's how we roll—so engineering knows exactly what to build and why.
Customer Discovery
: Talk to customers. A lot. Understand their workflows, pain points, and what "mind-blowing" means to them.
Cross-Team Collaboration
: Work
daily
with engineering and design to ship iteratively and fast.
AI Exploration
: Evaluate where AI can genuinely make our product smarter—and where it's just hype.
Data-Driven Decisions
: Track product metrics, run experiments, and use data to validate ideas (or kill your darlings).
Get Shit Done
: Do whatever it takes to move the product forward—whether that's coordinating a launch, triaging a bug, answering a support ticket, or making coffee for the team.
Requirements
Excellent written and spoken English skills
(a must); Good German skills are a plus.
2+ years of experience in product management, ideally in a SaaS or B2B environment.
A genuine interest in data visualization, charts, and making complex information look great.
The ability to write specs that engineers actually enjoy reading. Markdown fluency is a must.
No fear of working closely with nerdy engineers—in fact, you consider yourself one of them.
Curiosity about AI/ML and a pragmatic sense for where it can improve a product.
A bias for action: you'd rather ship something imperfect and iterate than debate for weeks.
Bonus: you've used R, PowerBI, Tableau, D3.js, played with data viz tools, or have opinions about bar charts vs. pie charts.
Benefits
Full-time position with a competitive remuneration package.
Start-up environment where your talents and motivation can make a real impact.
International team of highly motivated doers.
Fully remote position (within Germany), with ultra-nice offices in Leipzig (Plagwitz) for optional in-person collaboration.
Need something else? Ask!
summary: Vizzlo is seeking a Product Manager to join their team, responsible for defining and prioritizing the product roadmap, writing specs, and collaborating with engineers to ship features. The ideal candidate has experience in product management, a genuine interest in data visualization, and excellent written and spoken English skills. The company offers a competitive remuneration package, a startup environment, and the opportunity to work with a highly motivated team.

title: Senior Product Manager - Marketplace
jd_text:
Your Mission
With the power of AI, we're set to revolutionize the way general contractors and subcontractors find and match with each other on our marketplace. As our Senior Product Manager Marketplace, you'll actively shape this product vision — in a small team with big leverage and direct impact on a product used by thousands.
Your Responsibilities
You own the marketplace dynamics between General contractors and Subcontractors on our platform.
You ensure a healthy balance between supply and demand, and build mechanisms that keep liquidity strong on both sides.
You own search, matching and recommendation mechanisms that bring Subcontractors and projects together in a way that increases relevance and bid rate.
You analyze marketplace metrics and translate them into hypotheses and priorities.
You identify AI use cases for matching and recommendations and implement them together with our designers and engineers.
What You Bring
3–5 years of product management experience with two- or multi-sided marketplaces.
Demonstrated experience thinking through supply-demand dynamics: how to create enough supply, enough demand, and how to balance the sometimes conflicting interests of both sides.
Experience with search or matching algorithms, or working closely with data and engineering teams on ranking or recommendation logic.
Strong analytical skills and a solid understanding of marketplace metrics such as liquidity, conversion, and network effects.
You use AI tools such as LLM-based assistants, MCPs, and no-code prototyping routinely in your day-to-day work.
Fluent in written and spoken German and English.
Your Benefits
Help shape a product that solves real-world problems in the construction industry through AI and is revolutionizing the market.
Work in an open-minded, dynamic, and international team.
Remote or from our modern office in Berlin Friedrichshain.
Regular company and team events (offline and online).
Permanent contract and modern equipment.
Open feedback culture with regular 1:1s and development conversations.
About Us
Cosuno – Revolutionizing construction through technology.
We are Cosuno – a fast-growing tech startup that is making the construction industry more efficient and transparent with our digital platform for tenders and procurement processes. Our goal: to solve the most complex challenges in the industry with innovation and simplicity.
Why Cosuno?
Artificial intelligence is the key to our solution: it analyzes millions of price data points and helps construction companies create precise and efficient bids. This not only saves our customers time but also helps them avoid unnecessary costs.
But for us, it's not just about technology – it's about the people who drive it. At Cosuno, you will work with creative minds who are reshaping the construction industry. We believe in diversity because we know that the best ideas come from different perspectives. An integrative and inclusive work environment is a matter of course for us.
Join us – Build the future of construction.

The ideal candidate has 3-5 years of product management experience with two- or multi-sided marketplaces and demonstrated experience with supply-demand dynamics and search or matching algorithms. The company, Cosuno, is a fast-growing tech startup that aims to revolutionize the construction industry through technology.

title: Product Manager (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Zur Verstärkung unseres Teams suchen wir Dich zunächst auf zwei Jahre befristet als
Product Manager (m/w/d)
DEINE AUFGABEN
Du entwickelst gemeinsam mit dem Product Lead die digitalen Produkte und User Journeys Deiner Marke weiter – von der Idee über das Konzept bis zum Live-Betrieb und zur kontinuierlichen Optimierung
Du übersetzt die Marken- und Produktstrategie in konkrete Initiativen, Features und Experimente
Du analysierst Nutzerbedürfnisse und -verhalten anhand von Daten, Research und Feedback und leitest daraus Hypothesen sowie Produktmaßnahmen ab
Du erstellst und pflegst Epics und User Stories in enger Zusammenarbeit mit Tech, Business und Redaktion
Du steuerst Discovery- und Delivery-Prozesse - von MVPs über A/B-Tests bis hin zu iterativen Verbesserungen - und optimierst definierte User Journeys kontinuierlich
Du behältst relevante Produkt-KPIs wie Reichweite, Engagement, Conversion Rates und Feature Adoption im Blick und reportest die Ergebnisse für Deine Marke
DEIN PROFIL
Du verfügst über mehrjährige Erfahrung im Digital Product Management oder in einer vergleichbaren Rolle, z. B. als Product Owner oder im Bereich Produktentwicklung bzw. Growth/Conversion Management
Du denkst konsequent aus Nutzerperspektive und arbeitest mit Hypothesen, MVPs und iterativen Produktentwicklungsprozessen
Du hast Erfahrung in der Datenanalyse (z. B. Web-/App-Analytics und Dashboards) und nutzt Kennzahlen als Grundlage für Entscheidungen
Du verstehst digitale Geschäftsmodelle und weißt, welche Rolle Produkte für Reichweite, Engagement und Monetarisierung spielen
Du bringst eine hohe Affinität zu Marken und Content mit und bist souverän in der Kommunikation mit vielen Stakeholdern
Du arbeitest strukturiert, selbstständig und pragmatisch:„Done & gelernt“ ist Dir lieber als „perfekt & nie live“
Deine Benefits
Innovation trifft Tradition: Du gestaltest die digitale Transformation unserer Mediengruppe mit
Entwickle Dich mit uns: Wir ermöglichen Dir die Teilnahme an individuellen Coachings, Workshops oder (Online)-Trainings im Rahmen unserer FUNKE Akademie
Flexibel arbeiten: Wir leben ein hybrides Arbeitsmodell, weil uns Deine Work-Life-Balance wichtig ist
Teamwork: Dich erwarten ein dynamisches Team, eine offene Feedbackkultur, kurze Kommunikationswege und ein herzliches Miteinander
Corporate Benefits: Wir bieten Dir ein Benefit-Portal mit attraktiven Rabatten und Abos
Halte Dich fit und gesund: Profitiere von unseren Kooperationen mit verschiedensten Fitnessstudios und lasse Dich für Deine mentale und körperliche Gesundheit individuell beraten
Haben wir Dein Interesse geweckt? Dann gestalte mit uns die Zukunft und sende uns Deine Bewerbung an Jennifer Bay.

title: (Senior) Product Manager (all genders)
jd_text:
Across Europe, fleets are under enormous pressure. Fuel costs are rising. Regulatory requirements are tightening. The shift to low-emission vehicles is accelerating. And yet around 70% of European businesses still manage their fleets manually, with spreadsheets, disconnected tools, and processes that were never built for this moment.
Shiftmove
exists to make this simple.
We are a profitable B2B SaaS company and the fastest-growing fleet management platform in Europe, born from the merger of Avrios and Vimcar, and now serving tens of thousands of corporate customers across Germany, France, Spain, Switzerland, Poland, and beyond. Most recently, we acquired Optimum and Ocean in France, expanding our footprint and doubling down on one of Europe's largest fleet markets. Our AI-powered platform connects real-time vehicle data with intelligent software so that businesses can procure smarter, control costs, stay compliant, and lead the transition to cleaner mobility, all from one place.
About the role:
As our
(Senior)
Product Manager,
you will take over one of the most strategically important product domains at Shiftmove – the platform and ecosystem layer,
aka the connective tissue between Avrios, Vimcar, Optimum, Océan, and external partners.
You will own the product direction for this layer: deciding what becomes a reusable platform capability, what should stay market-specific, and where the team focuses first. You will work directly with Engineering, senior leadership, and cross-functional stakeholders across Germany and France to bring clarity to an ambiguous domain and turn complex problems into focused product direction.
This is a build and define phase, with a large scope and direct impact on Shiftmove's ability to expand across Europe, grow cross-sell revenue, and establish the platform foundation behind the leading fleet mobility ecosystem on the continent.
What you'll do:
Platform & Ecosystem Strategy:
Own the product direction for our platform and integration scope, connecting our products, external partners, market-specific systems, and customer workflows across Europe.
Ambiguity into Direction:
Create structure where ownership, priorities, and system boundaries are not yet fully defined. Decide what becomes a reusable platform capability, what should stay market-specific, and where the team focuses first.
Discovery and Delivery:
Lead discovery and delivery for core platform capabilities: shared workflows, reusable integration patterns, APIs, data flows, and interoperability between products.
European Market Expansion:
Build internationalisation capabilities that help our core products scale beyond one market at a time.
Cross-Product Portfolio:
Shape initiatives that bring Avrios, Vimcar, Optimum, and Océan closer together, reducing friction for customers and unlocking stronger cross-sell opportunities across the portfolio.
Stakeholder Alignment:
Work closely with Engineering, GTM, Sales, Customer Success, country teams, and senior leadership to align priorities and move complex topics forward across products and markets.
Focus and Ownership:
Protect the team from reactive request intake by setting clear priorities, defining ownership boundaries, and keeping focus on long-term platform value over one-off solutions.
What the first 6 months might look like:
Months 1 to 2:
Focus on onboarding, learning about our product portfolio, stakeholders, and ways of working.
Months 3 to 4:
Build trusting relationships with your team, translating your learnings into a focused product strategy and roadmap, and working on first challenges.
Months 5 to 6:
You're driving your topics, delivering reusable integration and internationalisation capabilities that make our platform more scalable across markets.
What you'll need to be successful:
Scaleup Mindset:
You possess a fierce ownership mentality, thrive in ambiguity, and enjoy building structure where there isn't a playbook.
B2B Product Background:
You have a solid track record in B2B produ

title: Product Owner (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Unternehmen:
CLAAS 365FarmNet GmbH
Standort:
Berlin
Startdatum:
zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt
Karrierestatus:
Berufserfahrene
Funktionsbereich:
Vertrieb
Seit 2013 entwickeln wir bei CLAAS 365FarmNet innovative Farmmanagement-Software, die landwirtschaftliche Betriebe effizienter und erfolgreicher gestaltet. Unser Ziel ist es, den Arbeitsalltag von Landwirten und Landwirtinnen zu erleichtern, indem wir sie mit fortschrittlicher Technologie und intelligenten Softwarelösungen unterstützen. Jeden Tag arbeiten über 100 engagierte Mitarbeitende an unserem Standort im Herzen Berlins daran, dieses Ziel zu erreichen und die Landwirtschaft der Zukunft mitzugestalten.
Die Stelle ist befristet bis zum 30.06.2027 in Vollzeit zu besetzen. Eine anschließende Beschäftigung in Teilzeit ist gegebenenfalls möglich.
Deine Rolle In Unserem Team
Du verantwortest einen festen Produktbereich innerhalb des CLAAS connect Portals
Übersetzung strategischer Anforderungen in Produktlösungen, die einen Mehrwert für den Kunden liefern
Identifizierung von Kundenproblemen und Konzipierung von Lösungen, die Du zusammen mit dem User Research / Experience Team testest und realisierst
Planung und Erstellung der Roadmap für Deinen Bereich
Erstellung von User Stories, Pflege des Backlogs und Übernahme der fachlichen Führung eines agilen Entwicklungsteams

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Job Description
The
Product Manager
owns and manages product and service profitability, including pricing strategy, margin levers, cost drivers, and investment tradeoffs. Define pricing and competitive positioning strategies based on market dynamics, customer value, and competitive benchmarks.
Product Manager leads the definition and execution of roadmap priorities by partnering closely with Engineering, Operations, Sales, Marketing, Quality, and external industry stakeholders. Success in this role requires strong market knowledge of detection and security products and systems, disciplined P&L and margin management, and the ability to translate strategy into scalable, revenue producing programs delivered through cross functional leadership.
Responsibilities
Strategy, Roadmap & Revenue Growth
Develop and own a clear product and service roadmap aligned to business strategy, technology investments, and market demand, with a direct focus on revenue growth and profitability.
Identify, prioritize, and execute revenue‑generating initiatives across new services, service line extensions, and lifecycle optimization opportunities to meet Vitality Index (NPVI) KPI
Lead business case development for organic growth initiatives, including market sizing, revenue forecasts, cost structures, and margin impact.
Translate market and customer insights into actionable decisions that balance near‑term revenue delivery with long‑term strategic growth.
P&L Ownership & Profitability Management
Cross‑Functional Program Execution
Lead cross‑functional teams (Engineering, Operations, Sales, Marketing, Quality, Client Services) to execute roadmap initiatives from concept through launch and scale.
Drive disciplined execution of programs, ensuring on‑time, on‑budget delivery while meeting defined revenue and profitability targets.
Serve as the single point of accountability for project outcomes, aligning stakeholders around priorities, tradeoffs, and execution plans.
Market, Customer & Industry Leadership
Apply deep knowledge of the detection and security industry to inform strategy and investment decisions.
Engage directly with customers, specifiers, regulators, trade associations, and industry stakeholders to identify unmet needs and emerging opportunities.
Partner with Sales to support customer discovery, value articulation, and commercialization of new and existing services.
Conduct Voice of Customer (VOC) gathering and analysis to address opportunities in the market and eco-system needs.
Go‑to‑Market & Commercial Enablement
Define and execute go‑to‑market strategies for new services, including value proposition, target segments, pricing, and launch plans.
Enable Sales and Commercial teams with training, tools, collateral, and messaging to support successful revenue adoption.
Track post‑launch performance and continuously optimize offerings based on market response and financial outcomes.
Qualifications
Facilitation skills – ability to lead small to medium groups of internal and external stakeholders towards desired outcomes in order to meet business objectives.
Interview skills – ability to conduct discovery sessions with clients, prospects and stakeholders in order to diagnose business opportunities, elicit requirements and conduct other types of research.
Strong communication and presentation (PowerPoint) skills.
Product management tools – product design (e.g. Invision, Zeplin), development task management (e.g. Jira, Azure DevOps), documentation and knowledge (e.g. Confluence, SharePoint), collaboration (e.g. Microsoft Teams, Slack), roadmap and portfolio management (e.g. Aha!, Daptiv).
Formal training and/or certification in Product Management is a plus.
University Degree (Equivalent to Bachelor's degree) in Computer Science, Business, or a related discipline.
5 years or more of related discipline experience or demonstrated competency.
Working with remote teams is highly preferred.
Project management certification is a plus.
About Us
A global leader
